title: Implement OpenAI Realtime gpt-realtime-2 cloud-native S2S provider
description:
Implement OpenAI Realtime gpt-realtime-2 as a SIBLING native-S2S provider, parallel to Gemini Live. Per user's goal correction: gpt-realtime-2 is a native speech-to-speech model (audio in -> audio out) and is NOT a stage in the speech2graph/notes composed pipeline. It is an alternative to Gemini Live, not a stage of the STT->LLM->TTS path.

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/openai_realtime/ mirroring gemini/mod.rs shape (WebSocket session, session resumption, reconnect with backoff, error classification, usage tracking).
- Settings enum entry under RealtimeAgent (sibling to GeminiLive), so the UI can route to it without touching ASR/LLM provider enums.
- Tauri commands: start_openai_realtime / stop_openai_realtime, parallel to start_gemini / stop_gemini.
- Frontend event subscription for the new provider events.
- Tests at the same level as gemini::tests.

References:
- docs/adr/0002-openai-realtime-provider.md
- docs/adr/0003-speech-to-speech-agent-provider-matrix.md
- src-tauri/src/gemini/mod.rs (~90KB; pattern to follow)
- https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-realtime-2

Out of scope: composed-pipeline orchestration (separate issue eee3 — different architectural path), barge-in semantics across providers (separate issue 7fcc), eager turn detection (separate issue 82b3).

queue_lane_2026_06_25.cutline: P2 sibling experience after core app usability
queue_lane_2026_06_25.summary: Owns OpenAI Realtime native speech-to-speech as a sibling path, not a stage in the transcript/notes/graph pipeline. Keep it separate from the composed local/hybrid voice-agent epic.

closeReason: Merged at a118119. New src-tauri/src/openai_realtime/ S2S module (WS session, resumption, reconnect/backoff, usage tracking) + converse FSM wiring + settings enum + frontend listeners/UI. Verified on merged tree: cloud clippy -D warnings, 1147 cloud lib tests inc. 40 openai_realtime tests, 642 vitest (TOTAL_LISTENERS 27->29), tsc.

title: Add TtsProvider trait + Deepgram Aura streaming TTS
description:
ADR-0003 names TTS as a first-class provider category for the speech-to-speech personality — Deepgram Aura, Kokoro, Piper, Coqui — but no implementation exists. TTS is the missing leg of the local/hybrid S2S pipeline (STT -> vLLM -> TTS).

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/tts/ with a TtsProvider trait. Stream-shaped (chunked PCM audio with playback timing, support cancellation/barge-in).
- First implementation: DeepgramAura over WebSocket, parallel to existing asr/deepgram.rs.
- Settings enum entry TtsProvider with variants None / DeepgramAura / Local* (stubbed).
- Wire provider into the S2S turn orchestrator (separate issue) once that lands.
- Tauri commands: test_tts_connection_cmd, list_tts_voices_cmd.
- Frontend types + credentials allowlist sync.

References:
- docs/adr/0003-speech-to-speech-agent-provider-matrix.md (provider matrix)
- src-tauri/src/asr/deepgram.rs (54KB; shape to mirror)
- https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/tts-websocket

Out of scope: Kokoro/Piper/Coqui local TTS engines (separate issues), barge-in semantics (separate issue).

title: Local/hybrid S2S pipeline: STT -> vLLM -> TTS turn orchestrator
description:
Composed-pipeline speech-to-speech orchestrator: STT -> LLM (OpenRouter or local vLLM) -> TTS (Deepgram Aura or local). This is the LOCAL/HYBRID composed pipeline only — NOT a wrapper around Gemini Live or gpt-realtime-2.

Goal alignment: this orchestrator drives the optional 'voice2voice agent that works in parallel to the initial speech2graph pipeline'. The initial pipeline (STT -> graph/notes + chatbot replies) already runs from the same STT source; this orchestrator adds the option of speaking-aloud agent replies.

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/s2s_local/ (or rename to src-tauri/src/voice_agent/) with a TurnState struct: source id, provider ids, sample rate, start timestamp, cancellation token, bounded audio + token buffers.
- Turn protocol: turn_start, audio append, turn_end, cancel. (barge_in is a separate issue.)
- Wires existing ASR finals -> existing OpenAI-compatible LLM (OpenRouter or local vLLM via HTTP) -> new TtsProvider -> audio playback.
- Latency milestone events: turn start, first STT partial, STT final, LLM request start, first LLM token, first TTS audio, final TTS audio, cancel ack.
- Frontend: surface latency milestones beside existing pipeline-latency status.
- Tauri commands: start_voice_agent, stop_voice_agent.

Hard dependencies: TtsProvider trait + at least one TTS impl (3132); audio playback subsystem (8d75); OpenRouter wired (c847) so the agent has a real LLM.

Out of scope: Gemini Live (already shipped), gpt-realtime-2 (separate issue 396f) — those are native-S2S models and bypass this orchestrator entirely.

References:
- docs/adr/0003 turn protocol section
- ../HF/streaming-speech-to-speech (HF reference impl; do not port wholesale)

queue_lane_2026_06_25.cutline: P1 once core transcript/projection and playback prerequisites are green
queue_lane_2026_06_25.summary: Owns the composed STT -> LLM -> TTS voice-agent path. It remains separate from native S2S providers and depends on local/cloud STT readiness, playback resampling, and local/cloud TTS availability.

title: Local TTS providers: Kokoro, Piper, Coqui
description:
Once TtsProvider trait lands, add local TTS engines for fully local S2S pipeline. Each is a separate impl behind a cargo feature flag (tts-kokoro, tts-piper, tts-coqui).

Scope (per provider):
- ONNX or other binding crate selection.
- Voice model bundling / download script update (scripts/download-models.ps1).
- Streaming chunk emission compatible with TtsProvider trait.

Out of scope: TtsProvider trait itself (separate issue).

title: Deepgram Flux EagerEndOfTurn support with TurnResumed rollback
description:
ADR-0003 'Deepgram + local turn strategy' notes EagerEndOfTurn can shave latency by starting LLM/TTS work before the high-confidence EndOfTurn, with TurnResumed as the rollback signal.

Scope:
- Wire Flux events through to S2S turn orchestrator.
- On EagerEndOfTurn: start LLM/TTS speculatively; capture cancel token.
- On TurnResumed: cancel speculative LLM/TTS work, wait for next final/eager event.
- UI surfaces eager-turn count + false-start (TurnResumed) count so users can tune.
- Telemetry: emit per-turn 'speculative cancelled' events for latency dashboards.

Hard dependency: S2S turn orchestrator must exist first.

title: Barge-in / interruption support across S2S providers
description:
ADR-0003 wave 5 — interruption semantics for Gemini Live, OpenAI Realtime, and the local pipeline.

Scope:
- Cancel current LLM/TTS output when a new turn starts.
- Stop UI playback immediately.
- Emit cancel-acknowledgement event before allowing the next turn.
- Immediate-retry guards so user can't double-trigger.
- Per-provider implementation: each S2S provider needs its own cancel path.

Hard dependency: at least the turn protocol from the local S2S epic.

title: Flip clippy to -D warnings once tree is clean
description:
Wave 1 added a lints job that runs cargo clippy --all-targets in warnings-only mode. Once we confirm the tree is clippy-clean (or fix the existing warnings), flip to '-- -D warnings' so clippy regressions can't merge.

Scope:
- Inspect current clippy output on Linux job once it's green.
- Fix any latent warnings.
- Update .github/workflows/ci.yml lints job to add '-D warnings' to the clippy invocation.

This is a trivial follow-up to commit 7fd608f.
closeReason: Current CI already runs cargo clippy with -D warnings; issue is stale.

title: Replace pinned pipewire-debian PPA with stock Ubuntu 24.04 packages or pin to PPA SHA
description:
ci.yml uses 'sudo add-apt-repository ppa:pipewire-debian/pipewire-upstream -y' on every Linux job (rust-linux + lints). This is an unpinned third-party PPA — whoever controls that account can replace pipewire packages on every CI run.

Scope:
- Test whether stock Ubuntu 24.04 'libpipewire-0.3-dev' satisfies rsac's minimum version. If yes, drop the PPA entirely.
- If not, pin a specific version with 'apt-get install libpipewire-0.3-dev=<exact>' or vendor the .deb files.

Found by the Linux CI auditor agent during Wave 1.

The Seed remains open because ci.yml lints and rust-linux jobs still use the PPA and need a remote stock-package validation before removal there.

title: Migrate CI to Node.js 24 actions before September 2026
description:
GitHub deprecation warning on every CI run today: 'Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. Will be removed September 16, 2026.'

Affected actions in our workflow:
- actions/checkout
- ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd

Scope:
- Watch upstream for Node.js 24 releases of these actions.
- Bump SHA pins when available.
- Or set FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true env to opt in early.

Date: hard deadline 2026-09-16.

title: Track actual token usage for chat/LLM responses
description:
src-tauri/src/commands.rs has a '// TODO: track actual token usage' comment. send_chat_message returns hard-coded tokens_used: 0.

Scope:
- Plumb token counts from each LLM provider (LocalLlama, Api/OpenAI-compat, AwsBedrock, MistralRs) through to ChatMessage.tokens_used.
- Match the pattern Gemini Live already uses (it tracks usage via events).
- Ensure get_session_usage / get_lifetime_usage / seed_lifetime_migration aggregations include LLM tokens.

Found by project-mapping agent during Wave 1 audit.
closeReason: Implemented chat/LLM token usage persistence: provider-reported non-zero totals now persist into session/lifetime usage via llm_total/llm_turns, streaming/blocking chat emit llm-usage-update after disk write, and TokenUsagePanel displays combined Gemini + LLM usage. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --lib, bun run typecheck, and TokenUsagePanel tests; focused Rust usage tests were compile-checked but not executed because Cargo test sessions stayed stale in this sandbox without visible child processes.

title: Tighten unwrap/expect calls in commands.rs
description:
Project-mapping audit identified 22 unwrap/expect calls in src-tauri/src/commands.rs — mostly in thread-pool init and parsing. Not panic-prone in practice but could be more defensive.

Scope:
- Audit each unwrap/expect.
- Convert to '?' / Result-returning code where the call site can plausibly fail at runtime.
- Leave unwraps that are genuinely infallible (e.g. inside lazy_static, after compile-time-checked constants) with a comment explaining why.

commands_unwrap_expect_audit_2026_06_25.summary: Re-audited src-tauri/src/commands.rs for exact .unwrap() and .expect() calls. Current code has zero exact unwrap/expect calls before the #[cfg(test)] test module; the 118 remaining exact matches are test assertions/setup helpers only. Runtime fallbacks use Result propagation, lock-error mapping, or explicit unwrap_or/unwrap_or_else defaults rather than production panics. No code churn was needed.
verified: test_start=$(rg -n "^mod tests" src-tauri/src/commands.rs | cut -d: -f1); before=$(rg -n "\.unwrap\(\)|\.expect\(" src-tauri/src/commands.rs | awk -F: -v start="$test_start" '$1 < start { count++ } END { print count + 0 }')

closeReason: Re-audit shows src-tauri/src/commands.rs has zero production exact .unwrap()/.expect() calls; remaining exact matches are inside #[cfg(test)] only, so the runtime hardening criteria are satisfied.

title: Document GraphDelta dual-emission contract or unify to single channel
description:
Project-mapping audit found backend emits both full graph-update snapshots (~10s throttle) AND incremental graph-delta events (per-extraction). UI may be optimized for deltas; dual-emission could cause reconciliation bugs.

Scope:
- Decide: keep both (with explicit contract) or consolidate to delta-only with on-demand snapshot.
- If keeping both: write src-tauri/src/events.rs doc-comment specifying the ordering invariant (e.g. 'a snapshot supersedes any deltas with timestamp <= snapshot.timestamp').
- Frontend: verify the React graph store applies them in the right order; add a vitest if not covered.

Found during Wave 1 mapping; surfaced as an architectural risk, not a confirmed bug.
closeReason: Documented the graph-update/graph-delta ordering contract in Rust and TS: deltas are incremental best-effort updates between authoritative snapshots, and snapshots supersede prior deltas. Added store regression coverage that snapshots replace canonical graph state after earlier deltas. Verified typecheck, Biome, focused store tests, and cargo check.

title: Replace rsac sibling path dependency with published or pinned dependency
description: AudioGraph still uses rsac as a sibling path dependency (../../rsac), while current docs/comments reference newer rsac versions. Decide the release strategy: publish rsac and consume from crates.io, or use a pinned git dependency/input that CI and release share. Scope: coordinate rsac release/version, update src-tauri/Cargo.toml target deps, remove sibling-clone assumptions from CI where appropriate, and update README/local-dev docs. Acceptance: PR CI and release use the same rsac revision; local development remains documented; no workflow depends on a floating rsac branch.

queue_reorg_2026_06_25.summary: Raised from P3 to P2 because the rsac dependency/release strategy now blocks the cross-platform CI and release-readiness epic; it is release hygiene, not a distant watchlist item.

rsac_dependency_pin_scout_2026_06_27.summary: Read-only scout found audio-graph still uses target-specific rsac sibling path dependencies, so Cargo.lock records rsac 0.4.0 without source/checksum while CI/release separately clone rsac at RSAC_REPO_SHA a2d3088b0ae8050d1ce79966298cc792c6694ec2. Local sibling /mnt/e/cs/github/rsac is dirty/newer than the workflow SHA, so local builds can silently differ from CI/release. rsac is not currently published on crates.io; the smallest release-safe move is a Cargo-pinned git dependency at the workflow SHA, publishing later as a separate package/release task.
current_shape: src-tauri/Cargo.toml uses ../../rsac target-specific path deps with feat_linux/feat_windows/feat_macos.
src-tauri/Cargo.lock has no rsac source/checksum because rsac is a path dependency.
ci.yml and release.yml pin RSAC_REPO_SHA externally instead of Cargo pinning it.
recommended_plan: Switch target-specific rsac deps to git = https://github.com/Codeseys-Labs/rust-crossplat-audio-capture.git, rev = a2d3088b0ae8050d1ce79966298cc792c6694ec2 with matching target features.
Refresh src-tauri/Cargo.lock so rsac source/rev is locked by Cargo.
Remove or simplify CI/release parent rsac checkout/verify steps after Cargo is authoritative.
Refresh README/CONTRIBUTING/RELEASE docs; local sibling path should become an explicit developer override only.
verification: clean-ref Blacksmith CI and release dry-run evidence
remaining: Do not implement from this dirty checkout; Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/workflows/docs are shared release surfaces.
Keep open until Cargo manifests, lockfile, CI/release staging, docs, and Linux/macOS/Windows evidence land.

title: Move set_var(GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) out of Vertex AI hot path
description:
Test suite auditor flagged src-tauri/src/gemini/mod.rs:762 — production code calls 'set_var(GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, ...)' inside the VertexAI auth path. This mutates process-global env without restoring; any test that exercises Vertex auth leaks the value.

Scope:
- Replace with per-request credential file path passed via Vertex SDK config object, not env var.
- Or, if the Google auth library only reads the env var, isolate the mutation behind a Mutex + restore-on-drop guard.

Severity: medium — currently no test hits this path, but it's a latent landmine.
closeReason: Replaced the Vertex AI explicit service-account path flow in gemini::open_ws with gcp_auth::CustomServiceAccount::from_file, then token() directly, so GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS is no longer mutated in the Gemini hot path. The default/no-path flow still uses gcp_auth::provider() for ADC/gcloud behavior. Verified rg shows no GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS mutation remains in gemini, cargo fmt, focused Gemini test, cargo check --lib, cargo check --lib --features diarization-clustering, and git diff --check with only pre-existing CRLF warnings.

title: Wire OpenRouter as the default cloud LLM for the primary pipeline
description:
Goal-aligned: OpenRouter is the chosen cloud LLM for the speech2graph/notes pipeline (STT -> T2T -> TTS).

OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible HTTP, so the existing 'Api' LLM provider variant should serve it with base URL pointed at openrouter.ai/api/v1. But credentials/test-connection wiring needs an OpenRouter-specific path.

Scope:
- New OpenRouter provider variant (or first-class config preset for the existing Api variant) so the UI surfaces 'OpenRouter' separately from generic 'OpenAI-compatible'.
- Credentials: api key with allowlist update in src/types/index.ts and src-tauri/src/credentials/mod.rs.
- test_openrouter_connection_cmd Tauri command, parallel to test_deepgram_connection.
- Settings UI: model picker that calls OpenRouter's /models endpoint to populate.
- Frontend: route the chatbot/agent's LLM calls through OpenRouter when configured.
- Ensure HTTP referrer/X-Title headers per OpenRouter docs (they're free + provide attribution).

Acceptance: a fresh install can pick OpenRouter, paste an API key, choose a model, and have the chatbot reply through it. Same pattern as Deepgram setup today.

title: Audio playback for TTS responses (Windows + Linux)
description:
Goal-aligned: chatbot replies must be played aloud as TTS audio. Today there is no audio output path; only audio capture via rsac.

Scope:
- Choose a cross-platform audio output crate (cpal, rodio, kira). cpal is most likely the right call given rsac probably already pulls it.
- Backend: receive PCM chunks from the TtsProvider stream and feed them to an output stream.
- Frontend: optional waveform/level meter; subscribe to playback events for UI affordances (mute, pause).
- Cancellation: a new turn / barge-in must stop playback within ~50ms.
- Format negotiation: TtsProvider yields PCM at a stated sample rate; resampler if device doesn't support it natively.
- Tauri commands: start_playback / stop_playback / set_output_device.

Hard dependency: TtsProvider trait (audio-graph-3132).

Linux + Windows are the priority targets per goal; macOS deferred.

title: Connect chatbot replies to TTS playback (the speak-aloud loop)
description:
Goal-aligned: when the chatbot/agent generates a reply via OpenRouter, route the streaming text to the TtsProvider, which plays via the new playback subsystem. This closes the STT->T2T->TTS loop.

Scope:
- Subscribe to LLM token deltas (already emitted as part of chat).
- Aggressive token-to-TTS flushing as ADR-0003 'turn protocol' specifies.
- Settings flag: enable speak-aloud per session.
- UI affordance: a speaker icon next to chatbot replies; click to replay.
- Cancellation propagation: user canceling a chatbot reply also cancels TTS.

Hard dependencies: TtsProvider (3132), audio playback (new), OpenRouter wiring (new).

title: Moonshine streaming STT provider (local)
description:
Goal-aligned: a low-latency local streaming STT option for the primary pipeline, alongside existing Whisper/Sherpa.

Scope:
- Pick a Rust binding or run via ONNX Runtime (moonshine-onnx publishes weights).
- Hook into existing Asr provider trait (whichever it is — see src-tauri/src/asr/).
- Streaming partial+final transcript emission with normalized turn lifecycle events (per ADR-0003 turn-detection contract).
- Behind cargo feature flag asr-moonshine so it doesn't bloat the default build.
- Voice activity / endpointing strategy: documented in the ADR for the local pipeline (audio-graph-eee3).

Out of scope: training, fine-tuning. Just inference.

References:
- https://github.com/usefulsensors/moonshine
- /mnt/e/CS/HF/streaming-speech-to-speech for streaming patterns

moonshine_runtime_research_2026_06_25.summary: Current primary sources show Moonshine is promising for local low-latency ASR but not yet implementation-ready in this Rust/Tauri app without choosing a runtime path. UsefulSensors publishes tiny/base English ASR models and Python/Keras inference; Hugging Face documents Moonshine Streaming checkpoints with incremental chunk processing; sherpa-onnx has Moonshine ONNX packaging, but currently observed package is not enough to prove the desired streaming runtime path. Created audio-graph-c4fd to decide runtime architecture, model files, cargo feature shape, partial/final strategy, and cross-platform packaging before code integration.

define asr-moonshine feature and model download descriptors

The feature now depends on concrete child Seeds for provider skeleton/packaging, streaming worker span mapping, and downloader/readiness/cross-platform validation.

The parent Moonshine provider remains blocked on runtime worker and model/readiness validation, not on skeleton metadata.

title: Document local LLM topology: vLLM external server with CUDA graphs
description:
Goal-aligned: clarify and document how AudioGraph consumes a local vLLM-served LLM as part of the local primary pipeline.

The user's note: '8B or less model locally with vllm and cuda graphs and our s2s pipeline'. vLLM speaks the OpenAI-compatible API, so AudioGraph's existing 'Api' LLM provider variant works — but the configuration story (CUDA graphs flags, recommended models, base URL) needs documentation so contributors can run a local pipeline reproducibly.

Scope:
- README section: 'Running a local LLM with vLLM' covering vllm serve flags, CUDA graphs (--enforce-eager off), model picks (Llama 3.1 8B, Mistral 7B), and AudioGraph 'Api' LLM provider settings.
- Optional cargo feature flag llm-vllm that runs a smoke test against a local vLLM if available.
- ADR or a docs/design/ note linking to the streaming-speech-to-speech reference.

Out of scope: implementing a Python sidecar (ADR-0003 explicitly defers that).

Reference: /mnt/e/CS/HF/streaming-speech-to-speech ADR-0003 vllm StreamingInput discussion.
closeReason: Documented local vLLM as an external OpenAI-compatible server. README now includes the AudioGraph settings mapping, vllm serve command, CUDA-graphs guidance with --enforce-eager off, API-key note, and 7B/8B model examples. docs/ops/vllm-backend.md now covers local and remote commands, host/port, served-model-name, max-model-len, gpu-memory-utilization, prefix caching, warmup, remote auth, Windows/WSL2 expectations, and links to ADR/research context. Verification: rg confirmed required vLLM flags and README/runbook sections.

title: Windows cargo test fails: rustc-link-arg-tests= without [[test]] target
description:
Wave 1 commit 7fd608f changed build.rs from rustc-link-arg= to rustc-link-arg-tests= to scope the test manifest embedding to test binaries only.

But the audio-graph crate has no [[test]] target in Cargo.toml — its tests live inline in #[cfg(test)] mod blocks within the [lib]. Cargo rejects rustc-link-arg-tests= in this case with:

  invalid instruction `cargo::rustc-link-arg-tests` from build script of `audio-graph v0.1.0`
  The package audio-graph v0.1.0 does not have a test target.

Failure: Windows job in run 26130224852.

Fix options:
  1. Revert build.rs to unscoped rustc-link-arg= (loses defense-in-depth but works).
  2. Add an explicit [[test]] target in Cargo.toml so -tests= is valid.
  3. Use a different gate.

Recommend (1) with a guard: if the env var is set AND we're building a non-test target, panic at build time so we fail loudly rather than embedding the manifest into a production exe.

References:
- src-tauri/build.rs (the offending println line)
- Cargo book: build script outputs

title: Aura: session-layer barge-in suppression of in-flight AudioChunk frames
description:
Reviewer A1+A2 (2026-05-20) flagged HIGH: clear_drops_in_flight_audio_frames test asserts only that the Cleared ack arrives, NOT that audio frames received between the client-side Clear send and the Cleared ack are suppressed. The barge-in contract is currently placed entirely on the consumer with no enforcing mechanism in the session layer.

Fix: AuraSession's session task should set an Arc<AtomicBool> clearing flag when SessionCmd::Clear is dispatched, clear it when Cleared is received. AudioChunk events emitted while the flag is set should be suppressed (or tagged with a cancelled: bool field). The test should then assert trailing_audio_count == 0.

Why P1: this is a Wave-B blocker. The audio playback subsystem will produce audible 'tail' artifacts on barge-in if the session layer doesn't suppress trailing frames.

References: docs/reviews/audio-graph-wave-a-review.md (or commit message of the next review-summary commit), src-tauri/src/tts/deepgram_aura.rs around line 1154-1160.

title: Aura: run session task on caller's runtime in tests for tokio::time::pause()
description:
Reviewer A1+A2 (2026-05-20) flagged MEDIUM: keepalive_sent_after_idle_timeout test waits ~12s wall-clock because the session task spawns its own new_multi_thread runtime that tokio::time::pause() can't drive. Inflates cargo test --lib tts wall-clock by ~12s every CI run.

Fix: factor a #[cfg(test)] AuraSession::start_with_url_on_current_runtime constructor that uses tokio::spawn (caller's runtime) rather than a dedicated runtime. Then tokio::time::pause() and tokio::time::advance() drive the test instantly.

References: src-tauri/src/tts/deepgram_aura.rs:1163-1226 (test) + spawn_session.

title: Aura: flush_seq has tearing race across rapid concurrent flushes
description:
Reviewer A1+A2 (2026-05-20) flagged LOW: flush() increments flush_seq atomically before sending SessionCmd::Flush. Session task reads flush_seq.load(SeqCst) when server sends Flushed. If caller calls flush() twice before the first Flushed ack arrives, both acks report the same sequence value (post-second-increment).

Fix: send the seq value INSIDE the enum variant — SessionCmd::Flush(u64) — and use the dispatch-time snapshot on the ack side, rather than re-reading the shared atomic.

Currently harmless (no consumer depends on the sequence number) but worth fixing before any does. References: src-tauri/src/tts/deepgram_aura.rs:264 + 722-724.
closeReason: Fixed Aura flush ack tearing by carrying dispatch-time sequence in SessionCmd::Flush, queueing pending flush sequences in the session loop, and adding focused non-socket regressions.

title: Streaming chat for LocalLlama, MistralRs, AwsBedrock providers
description:
Plan A3 (streaming chat) was scoped on its retry to ONLY the Api + OpenRouter providers. The LocalLlama (engine.rs), MistralRs (mistralrs_engine.rs), and AwsBedrock providers still use blocking chat.

Scope:
- LocalLlama: use llama.cpp's existing token-callback API to emit deltas through the executor's event_tx.
- MistralRs: use its existing streaming token API.
- AwsBedrock: use Bedrock ConverseStream API. Different SSE shape than OpenAI; needs an adapter.

Each provider can be a separate PR. Until done, the speak-aloud loop (audio-graph-92c7) only works with cloud LLMs (Api / OpenRouter) — local LLM users will see blocking-then-batch TTS instead of streaming TTS.

References: docs/adr/0006-streaming-chat-and-native-s2s-separation.md, docs/research/chat-tts-integration-map.md, plan A3.

Lowest-risk first provider is LocalLlama because engine.rs already owns the token loop and can emit TokenDelta pieces from the persistent context actor. MistralRs should follow via its native streaming response API. Bedrock should be last because the repo has AWS settings/readiness utilities but no aws-sdk-bedrockruntime dependency yet, so it needs a dedicated ConverseStream adapter.

title: Streaming chat: propagate finish_reason from provider through TokenDelta::Done
description:
Reviewer A3 (2026-05-20) flagged HIGH: commands.rs:1229 hardcodes finish_reason: 'stop' in chat-token-done payload. The actual provider finish_reason ('stop' / 'length' / 'content_filter') lives in the last TokenDelta::Delta but is never threaded into Done.

Why P1: Wave C's speak-aloud loop will incorrectly treat a 'length' (truncated reply) as a successful stop. The frontend type ChatTokenDoneEvent already advertises these values.

Fix: thread the last-seen non-null finish_reason from the Delta stream into TokenDelta::Done. Either add finish_reason: Option<String> to the Done variant or capture it in a local during run_sse_stream and pass it on the Done emission.

References: src-tauri/src/llm/streaming.rs run_sse_stream around line 317; src-tauri/src/commands.rs:1229; src-tauri/src/llm/streaming.rs:55-57 (Done variant declaration); src/types/index.ts:870 (ChatTokenDoneEvent.finish_reason field comment).

title: SSE parser: cap SseDecoder.buf to prevent unbounded growth on malicious streams
description:
Reviewer A3 (2026-05-20) flagged LOW: SseDecoder.buf is a Vec<u8> with no size cap. A malicious or broken upstream that never emits \n\n will grow the buffer indefinitely, exhausting memory.

Fix: add a configurable max-frame-size guard (e.g. 1 MB). On overflow, emit SseEvent::Error or similar and discard the buffer.

References: src-tauri/src/llm/sse.rs SseDecoder + feed().
closeReason: Implemented bounded SseDecoder frame cap with explicit overflow error event, streaming consumer handling, and focused llm::sse tests.

title: StreamRegistry::cancel: document TOCTOU window in return-value semantics
description:
Reviewer A3 (2026-05-20) flagged MEDIUM: StreamRegistry removes the cancel token under the lock then calls .cancel() outside the lock. If the stream task's terminal arm calls registry.finish() between the remove and the external cancel, the boolean cancel() returns true even though Done already arrived.

Not a data-corruption bug, but the boolean return is unreliable. Fix: doc-comment the return value as 'best-effort' and clarify Cancelled is not guaranteed to be the next terminal frame.

References: src-tauri/src/llm/streaming.rs StreamRegistry::cancel near line 483-497.
closeReason: Clarified CancellationToken drop behavior in the blocking chat shim and documented StreamRegistry::cancel as a best-effort return value.

title: send_chat_message shim: clarify cancel token drop semantics
description:
Reviewer A3 (2026-05-20) flagged LOW: commands.rs:1382 binds cancel token as _cancel which is dropped at scope end. Dropping a tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken does NOT fire it — but a future reader may misread this as cancellation-on-drop.

Fix: rename to _no_cancel or add a comment: '// CancellationToken does not fire on drop; this binding silences unused-result warnings while keeping the cancel infrastructure intact.'

References: src-tauri/src/commands.rs:1382.
closeReason: Clarified CancellationToken drop behavior in the blocking chat shim and documented StreamRegistry::cancel as a best-effort return value.

title: Gate local ML (whisper-rs, llama-cpp-2, mistralrs) behind cargo feature flags
description:
Per ADR-0007. Original problem: the three native ML crates were non-optional, so every build compiled whisper.cpp + llama.cpp + mistral.rs and required the local native ML toolchain even for cloud-only users. Step 1 scope: make deps optional, add asr-whisper/llm-llama/llm-mistralrs plus local-ml umbrella default, cfg-gate call sites with graceful provider-unavailable errors, and add a cloud-only CI job. Step 2 follow-up: flip default to cloud-only.

Audit 2026-06-24: code side is implemented. src-tauri/Cargo.toml has default = local-ml, cloud marker, optional whisper-rs/llama-cpp-2/mistralrs deps, and weak cuda/vulkan feature refs. cargo tree --no-default-features --features cloud -i whisper-rs / llama-cpp-2 / mistralrs returns package-not-found for all three, confirming the cloud feature graph excludes the heavy local engines. Existing cfg stubs return clear provider-unavailable behavior for local Whisper/llama/mistral builds.

Remaining acceptance: a dedicated cloud-only Rust CI job is not present in .github/workflows/ci.yml as inspected on 2026-06-24. CI edits are approval-gated for this session, so this issue now depends on audio-graph-150f / the Blacksmith CI matrix work. Also still needs a full cloud-only test run in CI or an equivalent documented local run before closure. Acceptance: cargo build/check/test --no-default-features --features cloud passes in the CI matrix without linking whisper-rs, llama-cpp-2, or mistralrs, and the workflow exposes that gate clearly.

README and ADR-0007 now document default local-ml vs --no-default-features --features cloud and the provider_unavailable recovery behavior.

title: vitest collects stale .claude/worktrees copies
description: vitest ran 72 files/581 tests because triplicate copies under .claude/worktrees/** were collected. Fixed by adding include=src/** and exclude for node_modules/dist/.claude/.codex/.agents in vitest.config.ts. Now 18 files/148 tests.
closeReason: Fixed in this loop: vitest.config.ts include/exclude added; suite now 18 files/148 tests

title: Debug build trips _CrtIsValidHeapPointer assertion on Windows (mixed CRT)
description: A --debug Tauri build (debug CRT /MDd) crashes at runtime with a 'Debug Assertion Failed! _CrtIsValidHeapPointer(block)' dialog (debug_heap.cpp:904). Root cause: bundled native ML libs (whisper.cpp/llama.cpp/mistral.rs) are compiled by cc/CMake against the release CRT (/MD), so a buffer allocated in one CRT is freed by the other; the debug CRT validates this and aborts. Release builds share one CRT and run fine. Workaround documented in docs/WINDOWS_QUICKSTART.md (use release). Proper fixes to evaluate: force the C++ deps to build with the matching CRT in debug (CFLAGS /MDd via cc/cmake), or set crt-static consistently, or feature-gate local ML out of debug runs (see ADR-0007 / issue 5fe7). Lower priority than 5fe7 since release works.

queue_reorg_2026_06_25.summary: Lowered from P2 to P3 because the Seed itself records release builds work and the failure is limited to debug CRT/local-ML skew; keep tracked but outside the immediate cross-platform release cutline.

closeReason: FIXED + PROVEN on Windows. CI job rust-windows-debug-crt-smoke (ci.yml run 28360916441, commit c22d564) is GREEN: built the --debug local-ml Tauri app AND ran the debug whisper lib tests (heap-exercising) on blacksmith-4vcpu-windows-2025 with NO is_block_type_valid abort. Fix = force native ML deps to /MDd: CMAKE_MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY=MultiThreadedDebugDLL + LLAMA_LIB_PROFILE=Debug (llama) + per-config CMAKE_{C,CXX}_FLAGS_{RELEASE,RELWITHDEBINFO}=/MDd + base CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS=/MDd (whisper hardcodes RelWithDebInfo). Scoped to Windows+debug only via the CI job + scripts/run-windows-debug.ps1 wrapper — config.toml [env] NOT used (can't cfg-scope), so release/Linux/macOS untouched. Two iters: iter-1 LNK2038 (base flags missed per-config injection) -> iter-2 green. docs/ops/windows-debug-crt-fix.md + WINDOWS_QUICKSTART updated; build.rs emits a guard warning. Release builds were always fine (single CRT).

title: cargo test harness binary fails to launch on Windows (STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND)
description: On this Windows machine, the release/debug app binary runs fine but the cargo unit-test harness binary (target/*/deps/audio_graph-*.exe) exits with 0xc0000139 STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND before any test runs, so cargo test --lib cannot execute Rust unit tests. PE import parse of the test exe shows imports: standard system DLLs + MSVCP140/VCRUNTIME140/VCRUNTIME140_1 + VCOMP140.DLL (MSVC OpenMP, pulled by whisper/llama/ggml). System32 copies are current (14.51.36231.0), so not a stale redist; a dependent DLL is missing an expected entrypoint only in the larger test-harness link. Workaround: verify Rust logic via the release app + targeted example/CLI runs; frontend tests (vitest, 148) run fine. Investigate: dumpbin /dependents + /imports on the test exe under a VS dev shell to find the missing symbol; candidate causes: VCOMP140 OpenMP version, or a delay-load. Blocks write tests for every section on the Rust side until resolved.
closeReason: Stale: docs/ops/windows-rust-test-crt-skew.md now marks the native Windows cargo test harness issue SOLVED via AUDIOGRAPH_EMBED_WINDOWS_TEST_MANIFEST=1, and .github/workflows/ci.yml uses that env var in the Blacksmith rust-windows cargo test job.

title: Chat blocked by extraction holding LLM client lock across blocking HTTP
description: FIXED: background entity-extraction held openrouter_client/api_client Mutex across the 60s blocking HTTP call; start_streaming_chat needed the same lock via sync_openrouter_client_from_settings_cache, so chat hung with an empty assistant bubble while live capture+extraction ran. Fix: derive Clone on OpenRouterClient/ApiClient and clone the client out of the guard before the HTTP call in executor.rs (extract_openrouter/extract_api/chat_openrouter/chat_api).
closeReason: Fixed: clone client out of guard before blocking HTTP (executor.rs); chat no longer blocks during live extraction

title: Backend-driven device direction (Input/Output) via rsac DeviceKind
description: Audio Sources now splits Devices into Input/Output via a frontend heuristic (WASAPI {0.0.1.* }=input, {0.0.0.*}=output, name fallback). Proper fix: add kind() to rsac AudioDevice trait (DeviceKind exists; WasapiDevice already computes it), thread through introspection::list_audio_sources -> audio-graph AudioSourceType -> frontend type, so classification is correct cross-platform (macOS/Linux) instead of id-prefix heuristic.
closeReason: Implemented backend-driven device direction by threading rsac DeviceKind into AudioSourceInfo.device_kind and changing AudioSourceSelector to group from backend metadata, with an Unknown Devices fallback instead of Windows/name heuristics. Verified by cargo fmt --check, cargo check --lib, bun run typecheck, bun run test src/components/AudioSourceSelector.test.tsx src/utils/captureTarget.test.ts, and focused Rust parser/descriptor tests.

title: Device capture: {0.0.1.*} input device 'not found' at start_capture
description: Selecting a capture (input) device like the fifine mic yields 'Audio device not found: {0.0.1.00000000}.{...}' at start_capture, though enumerate_devices (eAll) lists it and list_devices shows it. Investigate id round-trip / enumeration timing on the capture thread (COM apartment?) between list_audio_sources and build()'s enumerate_devices().find(id). Format negotiation already fixed; this is device resolution.

windows_device_capture_id_roundtrip_2026.summary: Fixed the capture source ID round-trip that could send raw Windows MMDevice row IDs into start_capture. Backend source enumeration now publishes canonical capture targets for devices (device:<device_id>), system default, and apps; active-source overlay checks both canonical capture target and legacy rsac row id; parse_capture_target defensively accepts raw Windows MMDevice IDs as Device targets for old UI/store state; the selector synthesizes canonical device targets when capture_target is absent but preserves already-canonical IDs.
windows_device_capture_id_roundtrip_2026.residual_risk: No live Windows microphone hardware smoke was run in this local Linux/WSL checkout; broader rsac/device smoke remains tracked by audio-graph-0d66 and cross-platform capture tests by audio-graph-f166.

closeReason: Fixed and verified capture target ID round-trip for Windows MMDevice devices: list_sources now emits canonical device:<id> targets, start_capture accepts raw MMDevice IDs defensively, and frontend fallback preserves canonical device targets.

title: Provider registry and streaming STT expansion roadmap
description: Build a provider-capability registry and expansion plan for streaming STT providers beyond the current set. Cover Soniox plus providers surfaced by Artificial Analysis streaming STT benchmarks (Speechmatics, ElevenLabs, Gladia, RevAI, Google Chirp streaming, Azure, OpenAI Realtime, xAI, NVIDIA/Together, Cartesia, Alibaba/Qwen, etc.). Acceptance: backend-owned provider registry captures auth key, transport, sample-rate requirements, partial/final semantics, turn events, diarization, language/model listing, health-check support, and UI availability; docs/backlog distinguish implemented vs candidate providers; new providers can be added without manually drifting Rust/TS unions.

title: Streaming transcript to notes and temporal-graph diff pipeline
description: Replace append-only transcript-derived notes/graph behavior with an event-sourced diff/retcon pipeline. Transcription remains saved durably, while notes and graph nodes/relationships are generated as versioned patches from transcript windows. A TTFT/backpressure-aware LLM queue batches incoming finalized turns, waits for in-flight synthesis when needed, merges new context, and emits notes/graph diffs with provenance. Acceptance: transcript JSONL is immutable source of truth; notes and graph state are patchable/versioned; frontend can display updates/retcons; evaluation harness measures latency, diff quality, and graph stability on recorded sessions.

dataflow_review_2026_06_24.summary: Read-only data-flow review found that final transcript events are durable, projection contracts exist, and audio consumer bus is present, but partial ASR revisions are live-only, GraphDelta remains a direct mutation transport rather than a semantic patch, notes are not durable/patch-based, and projection scheduling/materialization is not wired. Recommended backend chain: SourceDescriptor -> ProcessedAudioChunk -> ASR span revisions -> TranscriptLedger -> ProjectionScheduler -> structured LLM patches -> NotesMaterializer and GraphMaterializer -> frontend patch reducers.

add replay/eval harness measuring latency, coalescing, stale discards, graph churn, and note stability

runtime_projection_apply_slice_2026_06_2.summary: Added backend runtime application for accepted ProjectionPatch values. AppState::apply_runtime_projection_patch now requires the queued session id and queued ProjectionBasis, rejects model basis rewrites and session mismatches, validates against the current TranscriptLedger, applies to a cloned MaterializedProjectionState, persists the affected materialized notes/graph artifact, enqueues the accepted projection event, and commits active materialized state only if the session is still current. ProjectionEventWriter::append now reports enqueue success. Focused tests cover accepted notes, accepted graph materialization, and stale-basis rejection with no state or disk mutation.

dynamic_queue_architecture_reconciliatio.summary: Current code has already implemented the core non-append-only architecture the user envisioned: immutable TranscriptEvent JSONL, revisioned TranscriptLedger basis, TTFT-aware ProjectionScheduler, structured ProjectionPatch generation, validated notes/graph materializers, accepted patch replay, frontend transcript/notes/graph reducers, and projection diagnostics. The queue is more efficient than a naive prompt-append loop because it runs one basis-bound in-flight job per projection kind, coalesces newer ledger states while waiting, adapts TTFT from observed generation latency, discards or repairs stale completions, and records latency/token/backpressure telemetry without exposing transcript text or secrets.
critique: The scheduler still has one in-flight job per notes/graph projection kind; this is correct for basis safety but limits concurrency. Parallel shard/speculative approaches should only be added after replay/eval proves quality benefits outweigh stale-patch churn.
Epic closure should not happen until provider-backed smoke proves a real saved-credential LLM path and cross-platform CI validates the runtime surfaces.
The event-sourced data-model Seed remains open for migration boundaries, crash/replay fixtures, and ADR/design closeout even though many implementation slices are already present.

title: Configuration UX and credential health center

Credentials remain local in credentials.yaml; settings UI shows saved credential presence, enables provider/model controls from saved keys without re-entry, runs health/model discovery when Settings opens, and presents provider readiness by capability/mode. title: Expose OpenAI Realtime STT in frontend settings and generated types
description: Backend supports AsrProvider::OpenAiRealtimeTranscription and speech routing, but TS types/settings UI cannot represent or select openai_realtime. This is a P0 provider drift bug. Add OpenAI Realtime STT to frontend settings/types/i18n/tests directly or through the provider registry; registry should not block the minimal fix if it would delay usability. Acceptance: saved settings containing type=openai_realtime load without TS/Rust drift; users can select gpt-realtime-whisper, reuse saved openai_api_key credential, run a side-effect-free health check, save settings, and start transcription.
closeReason: Frontend exposure implemented: TS ASR unions and IPC type now include openai_realtime; Settings renders OpenAI Realtime with key/model/language fields; save maps to AsrProvider { type: openai_realtime } and stores the secret in openai_api_key; i18n and SettingsPage tests cover the path. Remaining saved-key health/model discovery is tracked by audio-graph-cbde/audio-graph-abc1/audio-graph-bc42.

title: Backend provider capability registry with TS schema generation
description: Introduce a backend-owned provider registry for ASR, LLM, TTS, Gemini/RealtimeAgent, and future providers. Registry entries declare provider id, family/stage, display key, settings variant, implementation status, transport, credential key/auth kind, required feature flags, sample-rate/audio requirements, source policy, streaming support, partial/final/turn semantics, model catalog strategy, health-check command, defaults, and UI schema groups: basic, advanced, health, modelCatalog. First slice: backend descriptors, read-only command, enum coverage tests, credential-key allowlist validation, local model required-file validation, and one backend consumer such as ASR source policy. Later slice: generate/export TS-visible schema so Rust settings enums, frontend unions, forms, docs, health badges, and model pickers do not drift. Acceptance: current ASR/LLM/TTS/Gemini providers plus OpenAI Realtime STT are represented; descriptor tests catch drift; generated TS eventually drives at least one settings surface; CI fails on schema drift.

planned_stt_candidates_2026_06_24.summary: Extended the backend provider registry with planned ASR descriptors for Soniox realtime, Gladia Solaria live, Speechmatics realtime enhanced, and ElevenLabs Scribe realtime. Added local credentials.yaml slots and TS credential contracts for soniox_api_key, gladia_api_key, speechmatics_api_key, and elevenlabs_api_key. Planned descriptors are not Settings-selectable ASR variants yet; they exist so registry/readiness/schema work can reason about future providers without Rust/TS drift.

This reduces Rust/TS/provider descriptor drift without adding platform-specific codegen scripts.

audio_input_registry_slice_2026_06_24.summary: Provider descriptors now include an explicit audio_input contract for every ASR provider and native realtime-agent surface. The contract records the cross-platform processed-audio bus format (16 kHz mono f32), provider-side wire/runtime format, transport encoding, adapter resampling, and current multichannel safety. Implemented adapters distinguish local f32 buffers, multipart WAV batch ASR, AWS eventstream PCM16, WebSocket binary PCM16, WebSocket JSON/base64 PCM16, and OpenAI Realtime 24 kHz adapter resampling. Planned Soniox, Gladia, Speechmatics, and ElevenLabs Scribe descriptors now carry provisional 16 kHz mono PCM16 WebSocket-binary audio contracts before they become selectable.

title: Implement Soniox realtime STT provider
description: Add Soniox v5 Real-Time as the first net-new streaming ASR provider from the Artificial Analysis streaming STT shortlist. API shape: wss://stt-rt.soniox.com/transcribe-websocket, api_key in the first JSON config message, binary PCM frames, GET /v1/models for model catalog, model stt-rt-v5, token stream with final/non-final flags, language ID, speaker diarization, endpoint detection, and final_audio_proc_ms latency metadata. Scope: credentials allowlist key, Rust streaming client, normalized partial/final/turn metadata, model/health support through provider readiness, parser fixtures, and live smoke path. Acceptance: live capture can stream to Soniox; transcript events preserve source/provider metadata; saved Soniox key can be validated and used to populate model/provider controls without plaintext key echo.

parser_first_provider_review_2026_06_24.summary: Provider expansion review recommends implementing Soniox only after the ASR parser fixture harness and enriched registry metadata land. The first implementation slice should be parser-only: Soniox config response, token stream partial/final flags, language ID, diarization speaker IDs, endpoint detection, and final_audio_proc_ms latency metadata mapped into normalized ASR span revisions without opening a live WebSocket.
acceptance_additions: Soniox parser fixtures cover partial revisions, final supersession, language metadata, speaker metadata, endpoint events, invalid/empty events, and latency metadata
Soniox Settings/readiness uses credentials.yaml through backend health/model commands without plaintext key echo
live WebSocket transport stays env-gated until parser replay and registry schema tests pass
Soniox audio format/source policy is declared in provider registry before the provider is selectable

soniox_parser_fixture_slice_2026_06_24.summary: Added a parser-only Soniox realtime STT normalization slice without live WebSocket transport or Settings exposure. The parser models Soniox v5 incremental token semantics: final tokens accumulate inside one active app-owned turn span, non-final tokens replace the current tail, <end>/<fin>/finished close the turn, provider errors preserve machine-readable metadata, and normalized ASR span revisions carry speaker/language/progress metadata where consistent.

title: Source descriptor and audio consumer bus refactor
description: Make capture source selection and processed-audio fan-out robust for all planned providers and OSes. Acceptance: backend emits typed SourceDescriptor values from rsac metadata (device kind, default status, supported/default formats, capture target, platform capabilities); frontend stops inferring input/output from IDs; processed-audio consumers register dynamically with bounded queues, drop policy, and per-consumer telemetry instead of hardcoded speech/Gemini channels.

cross_platform_bus_review_2026_06_24.summary: Audio/source architecture review recommends treating processed audio as a provider-neutral bus across macOS, Windows, and Linux. title: Saved-credential health checks and model discovery on Settings open
description: When Settings opens, use saved credentials from credentials.yaml through backend-only provider health/model commands; do not pass plaintext secrets back into React for normal health/model discovery. Scope: add backend-owned provider readiness cache, get_provider_readiness command, provider health probes, model/voice/language discovery with TTL/debounce, cancellation/rate limiting, in-flight coalescing, cached failures, and refresh after save/clear. Cache keys must exclude API keys and use provider id plus non-secret config (endpoint/base URL, AWS region/source/profile) plus a credential epoch incremented by save_credential_cmd/delete_credential_cmd. Use provider_registry descriptors for metadata, but implement explicit Rust readiness/catalog strategies rather than dynamic dispatch by command string. Acceptance: OpenRouter/Deepgram/AssemblyAI/AWS/Gemini/OpenAI-compatible providers use saved keys server-side to enable controls and populate catalogs; UI displays stale/checking/ready/error states without requiring key re-entry; network calls are non-blocking, debounced, safe to retry, and tests prove Settings open does not call load_credential_cmd.

readiness_slice_2026_06_24.summary: Added get_provider_readiness_cmd with non-secret provider readiness payloads, credential-epoch cache invalidation, TTL-based cached health results, explicit health strategies for OpenAI-compatible ASR, Deepgram STT/TTS, AssemblyAI, OpenRouter, active AWS providers, and Gemini API-key mode. Settings calls readiness refresh on open, after Save, and after Clear Saved Key actions; it renders a compact readiness strip and hydrates OpenRouter models from the saved-key backend payload without load_credential_cmd.

title: Harden credentials.yaml writes and Windows ACLs
description: Harden local secret persistence and debug/log exposure. Current Unix temp-file path is restrictive; CredentialStore Debug has now been customized to redact values, but Windows/non-Unix paths still use fs::write before ACL hardening and related runtime/provider config structs can still expose secrets through derived Debug until the dedicated Debug-redaction follow-up lands. Scope: create temp files with restrictive permissions from the start where possible, apply explicit Windows ACLs, keep CredentialStore/manual debug redaction covered by tests, avoid logging secret-bearing payloads, and add tests/docs for credentials.yaml behavior. Acceptance: credentials.yaml and temp files are owner-only on Linux/macOS and restricted to the current user on Windows; CredentialStore debug/log output redacts secret values; broader provider/runtime Debug and provider error-body redaction are tracked as separate P1 security bugs.
closeReason: Credential write hardening completed. credentials.yaml temp writes now use write_owner_only_temp_file: Unix creates the temp file at 0600 before writing, and non-Unix creates an empty temp file, calls fs_util::set_owner_only before writing secret YAML, then writes contents. Existing fs_util Windows path applies owner-only ACLs with icacls and CREATE_NO_WINDOW. CredentialStore Debug redaction is already covered, and broader runtime/provider Debug plus provider error-body redaction are closed in audio-graph-1bd7 and audio-graph-74ed. Verified cargo fmt, cargo test --lib credential -- --nocapture (22 tests), cargo check --lib, cargo check --lib --features diarization-clustering, and git diff --check with only pre-existing CRLF warnings.

title: Redacted credential presence API for provider readiness
description:
Expose backend-owned redacted credential/provider readiness without returning plaintext secrets to normal UI state. Current load_credential_cmd returns Option<String> for legacy explicit readback; normal Settings readiness flows use presence instead. Scope: remove/restrict load_all_credentials_cmd, avoid default plaintext secret readback, make key fields replace-only while showing saved-key presence, and ensure CredentialStore debug/log surfaces do not expose secrets. Acceptance: Settings can show saved/missing credential presence and unlock provider controls without receiving secret values; plaintext keys are only accepted for save/replace and never echoed by default. Explicit acceptance: SettingsPage must not invoke load_credential_cmd for saved OpenRouter/Gemini/Deepgram/AssemblyAI/default provider readiness on open, load_all_credentials_cmd is removed or restricted away from normal UI, and tests prove saved-key presence enables provider controls with blank replace-only secret inputs.

2026-06-24 first slice landed: backend load_credential_presence_cmd returns non-secret key/present/source entries for allowlisted credentials, App first-run Express Setup detection now uses the presence API instead of probing plaintext keys, and tests cover no-secret serialization plus App presence probing.

Second slice landed: OpenRouter and Gemini Settings stop hydrating openrouter_api_key/gemini_api_key into React state, their Test/Load-Models flows can use saved backend keys when password fields are blank, and frontend/Rust tests cover those saved-key paths.

Third slice landed: Deepgram STT, AssemblyAI STT, and Deepgram Aura TTS no longer hydrate saved deepgram_api_key/assemblyai_api_key into React state. Settings uses credential presence to enable tests and saved-key hints while blank password fields pass apiKey: null so Rust resolves credentials.yaml internally. Backend Deepgram, AssemblyAI, and TTS test commands now accept optional draft keys and fall back to saved credentials. Verification: SettingsPage tests 39/39 pass, typecheck pass, Biome pass on touched files, cargo test api_key_resolution 12/12 pass, cargo check pass.

Fourth slice landed: generic OpenAI-compatible endpoint keys and AWS access-key/secret/session fields are replace-only in Settings. Settings no longer invokes load_credential_cmd on open for OpenAI/Groq/Together/Fireworks/OpenRouter/Gemini endpoint keys or AWS key material; saved-key presence shows endpoint/OpenAI/AWS hints and enables blank-field tests. test_cloud_asr_connection now accepts apiKey: null and resolves saved endpoint keys in Rust; AWS SDK test config resolves blank access key/secret/session from credentials.yaml; the clear-saved-AWS action deletes aws_access_key, aws_secret_key, and aws_session_token together. load_all_credentials_cmd was removed from the registered IPC surface. Verification: SettingsPage tests 42/42 pass, typecheck pass, Biome pass on touched frontend files, cargo test key_resolution 16/16 pass, cargo test draft_or_saved_secret 1/1 pass, cargo check pass.

Fifth slice landed: CredentialStore no longer derives raw Debug. It has a manual Debug implementation that prints <present>/<missing> only, with a debug_output_redacts_secret_values regression test. Verification: cargo test debug_output_redacts_secret_values pass, cargo check pass.

Handoff: backend-owned health/model readiness caching is tracked by audio-graph-cbde; broader runtime/provider Debug redaction is audio-graph-1bd7; upstream provider error-body redaction is audio-graph-74ed; Windows ACL/write hardening remains audio-graph-e78e.

title: Live rsac audio smoke tests on CI-capable runners
description:
Add gated live audio capture smokes using virtual devices where CI runners support them. Scope: Linux PipeWire dummy sink/source, Windows VB-CABLE or loopback fixture, macOS BlackHole/native loopback if available; test system/default/device/process capture enough to catch source-id and format-negotiation regressions. Acceptance: workflow_dispatch and scheduled jobs can run live smokes; normal PRs keep lightweight checks; failures attach source/device enumeration logs.

--- RESEARCH COMPLETE 2026-06-27 (docs/research/ci-virtual-audio-devices-2026-06-27.md) ---
ADOPT (do not build) on all 3 OSes. Baseline: LABSN/sound-ci-helpers (BSD-3, one composite action, all 3 OSes, maintained 2026-06-22).

LINUX: PipeWire null-sink + .monitor capture-back. CRITICAL: snd-aloop/snd-dummy CANNOT load on GitHub-hosted runners (Azure VM kernel has no sound support; GitHub confirmed no plans Feb 2026). Userspace PipeWire is the ONLY hosted path — and it is what rsac actually enumerates (rsac Linux capture = PipeWire, not raw ALSA). CI already installs PipeWire from PPA. Add a .monitor shim since LABSN's Pulse-only Linux path doesn't guarantee capture-back.

MACOS: BlackHole 2ch (brew install blackhole-2ch + sudo killall coreaudiod). User-space CoreAudio AudioServerPlugin (HAL plugin), NOT a kext -> no SIP change, no kext approval, no reboot. GitHub preinstalls it but it flakes ~30% so install explicitly. macOS 14.4+ uses 'killall coreaudiod' not 'kickstart -k'. GPLv3.

WINDOWS: VB-CABLE (cert-pretrust into TrustedPublisher -> silent -i -h install -> net start audiosrv). Silent unattended install proven on stock GH runners (freedv-gui, AlekseyMartynov action). CRITICAL: bare Windows runners have NO audio device + audio service off; WASAPI loopback alone is NOT a substitute (GetDefaultAudioEndpoint(eRender) returns ERROR_NOT_FOUND with no hw) -> a virtual driver is mandatory. LICENSING CAVEAT: VB-Audio expects a paid license for automated/server use -> resolve before relying on it in CI.

Nothing requires self-hosting on hosted runners; Blacksmith's only edge is pre-baking certs/drivers to cut per-job time/flakiness. cpal (our playback dep) does NO real-device CI -> audio-graph would be ahead of upstream; LABSN is the closest community pattern.

IMPL PATH: gate behind a 'live-audio-smoke' Cargo feature + workflow_dispatch/scheduled (mirror the existing optional-feature matrix gating); PRs keep lightweight checks; attach enumeration logs on failure. Follow-up: resolve VB-CABLE licensing for CI use.

--- CI EVIDENCE 2026-06-27 (Blacksmith run 28302904922) ---
MACOS AUDIO E2E PASSED: Live rsac audio smoke (macos) GREEN (20m21s). BlackHole 2ch installed via brew (confirmed by system_profiler SPAudioDataType), the feature-gated live_audio test ran and passed = real CoreAudio device enumeration + format negotiation against a virtual loopback device on a real macOS runner. THIS IS THE FIRST REAL AUDIO E2E ON ANY PLATFORM. Linux leg FAILED — environment-only: LABSN sound-ci-helpers runs systemctl --user restart pulseaudio which fails headless (no D-Bus user session: "Failed to connect to bus: No medium found"). Fix in flight: make LABSN macOS-only, run the Linux PipeWire null-sink + .monitor shim under dbus-run-session WITHOUT systemctl --user (the research-recommended path). Windows leg still held on seed d3d3 (VB-CABLE licensing). NET: audio e2e PROVEN on macOS; Linux pending the CI shell/dbus fix; Windows pending d3d3.

--- LINUX+MACOS AUDIO E2E GREEN 2026-06-27 (Blacksmith run 28303714034) ---
Live rsac audio smoke PASSES on Linux(8m54s) AND macOS(19m17s). Linux fixed: LABSN made macos-only + PipeWire null-sink (explicit format=s16le rate=48000 ch=2) + .monitor under dbus-run-session (no systemctl --user); rsac enumerates the virtual device (ag_sink / device:45), list_sources sees it, format negotiation resolves. macOS: BlackHole 2ch CoreAudio HAL loopback. Test asserts device enumeration + format (log-not-fail on pre-bind empty formats, stays non-vacuous) + best-effort capture probe. Windows leg still HELD on seed d3d3 (VB-CABLE licensing). NET: real-audio device e2e now PROVEN on 2 of 3 OS in CI; Windows pending d3d3. Next slice (->f166): full PCM play-through round-trip with correlation assert.
closeReason: Verified: live-audio-smoke job at ci.yml:1032, Linux+macOS legs present (blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404 and blacksmith-6vcpu-macos-15), SHA-pinned LABSN/sound-ci-helpers@d08c889, cargo test command at:1115, log artifact upload at:1140-1155. Windows leg intentionally deferred to d3d3 (VB-CABLE licensing), which was the agreed scope.

title: TTFT-aware LLM synthesis queue for notes and graph diffs
description: Implement a backend ProjectionScheduler that batches transcript deltas into TTFT/backpressure-aware LLM synthesis jobs without overlapping incompatible calls. Strategy: partial ASR updates revise the transcript ledger; stable/final spans enqueue projection work; while an LLM call is in flight, coalesce new spans and repair windows; each response emits structured patch proposals tied to a ProjectionJob basis {span_ids, revision_numbers, transcript_hash}; stale responses are discarded or converted into repair jobs. Scope: provider latency/TTFT metrics, bounded queues, cancellation/retcon, deterministic coalescing tests, and hooks into existing LLM executor without blocking chat. Acceptance: no LLM call is made per partial token; canonical notes/graph update through patches with provenance; queue telemetry shows lag/coalescing/stale-discard counts.

ttft_queue_review_2026_06_24.summary: Projection/data-flow review supports the TTFT-aware dynamic queue, but recommends treating in-flight LLM work as basis-bound jobs. While a call is in flight, new transcript revisions should coalesce into a repair or follow-up window; responses must be checked against the current transcript hash before applying notes/graph patches.
acceptance_additions: scheduler never calls the LLM per partial token; it batches stable/final spans and high-stability windows
in-flight responses with stale basis are discarded or converted to repair jobs before materialization
queue telemetry records coalescing, stale discards, repair jobs, TTFT estimate, and lag separately from ASR delay
streaming chat paths do not bypass projection queue backpressure when used for notes/graph synthesis

transcript_ledger_basis_slice_2026_06_24.summary: ProjectionScheduler now has a concrete basis-freshness primitive to consume once runtime wiring begins. TranscriptLedger validates ProjectionBasis objects against current transcript span revisions and classifies stale, missing, unknown, hash-mismatched, and diarization-unavailable basis states. The scheduler itself is still not implemented and this Seed remains blocked until the data-model runtime pieces are wired.

projection_scheduler_contract_slice_2026.summary: Added a pure basis-bound ProjectionScheduler contract. The scheduler starts ProjectionJob objects from the current TranscriptLedger basis, coalesces newer ledger state while an LLM call is in flight instead of overlapping calls, validates completed jobs with TranscriptLedger::validate_basis, discards stale completions, and starts replay-priority repair jobs from the latest basis. Metrics now track jobs_started, coalesced_updates, and stale_discards. No provider I/O or materializer runtime wiring is started yet.

title: Temporal graph patch and retcon engine

title: Event-sourced transcript/notes/graph synthesis data model
description: Define the persisted data model for immutable transcript events plus revisioned derived artifacts. Scope: TranscriptEvent JSONL with span_id, provider_item_id, provider, source_id, speaker_id, start/end times, text, is_final, stability, revision_number, supersedes, received_at; ProjectionJob with basis {span_ids, revision_numbers, transcript_hash}; ProjectionPatch with sequence, llm_request_id, basis, operations, confidence, and provenance. Persist transcripts/<session>.events.jsonl, projections/<session>.events.jsonl, graphs/<session>.json, notes/<session>.json, and session metadata paths. Acceptance: transcript event log remains immutable; canonical transcript/notes/graph are replayable projections; stale projection jobs can be basis-checked and discarded or repaired; Rust types and ADR/design doc define migration boundaries.

materialized_reconciliation_implementati.summary: Read-only scout identified the smallest load-time reconciliation slice: when projection logs exist and replay succeeds, replay becomes canonical for missing/stale/ahead materialized notes and graph artifacts. Missing/stale artifacts should be rewritten from replay; ahead artifacts should be timestamp-quarantined and replaced from replay when replay has content; empty logs preserve current backward-compatible behavior.
behavior: Missing materialized notes/graph plus non-empty replayed state: write replayed artifact to disk and return replayed state.
Stored last_sequence below replayed last_sequence: overwrite with replayed artifact and return replayed state.
Stored last_sequence above replayed last_sequence: quarantine existing file and never return ahead state; write replayed artifact if replay has content.
Equal sequence but unequal artifact: treat replay as canonical and rewrite without frontend/API schema changes.
If projection logs are empty, preserve current behavior.

title: Typed SourceDescriptor contract from rsac to React
description: Replace lossy AudioSourceInfo with a richer backend-issued SourceDescriptor. Include canonical source id, display name, capture target string, source kind, device kind/input-output, is_default, supported/default format when available, platform capability flags, permission hints, and process/app metadata. Acceptance: AudioSourceSelector no longer classifies devices by Windows id/name heuristics; process vs process-tree semantics are explicit; TS type is generated or derived from Rust schema.

remaining: generate or derive the TypeScript schema from Rust instead of hand-maintaining the union
add richer process/app metadata and refined platform-specific permission actions

remaining: generate or derive the TypeScript schema from Rust
add richer process/app metadata and refined platform-specific permission actions

title: Dynamic processed-audio consumer registry
description: Replace hardcoded dispatcher outputs for speech and Gemini with a registry of active processed-audio consumers. Consumers register id, stage/provider, bounded channel capacity, drop policy, source filter/mixing mode, and lifecycle handle. Acceptance: speech, Gemini notes, native converse, OpenAI Realtime, and local/hybrid S2S can coexist or be rejected by policy without adding new fields to AppState for each provider; per-consumer drop counters and queue health are emitted to the UI.

registry_first_slice_2026_06_24.summary: Added ProcessedAudioConsumerRegistry with descriptors for id, stage/provider, capacity, drop policy, source filter, and mixing mode. AppState registers the existing speech and Gemini channels through the registry, start_capture dispatches via the registry instead of hardcoded speech/Gemini branches, and backend emits audio-consumer-health snapshots with per-consumer queue/drop telemetry. Frontend types/store/useTauriEvents now retain the latest health payload.

registry_policy_slice_2026_06_24.summary: Added registry-owned processed-audio consumer conflict groups. Descriptors and health payloads now include optional conflict_group; register rejects a consumer whose conflict group is already reserved. Gemini notes and native converse reserve the gemini-live-client group before mutating the shared Gemini client, so overlap rejection no longer depends on scattered command cross-guards. Unregistration now belongs to stop/session-owner teardown instead of audio-sender exits, preventing premature release of an exclusive provider slot while a shared session remains active.

title: Replay/evaluation harness for transcript-to-notes graph synthesis
description: Build an offline replay harness that feeds recorded transcript/audio sessions through the notes and graph synthesis pipeline. Metrics: latency per patch, queue coalescing behavior, node/edge churn, duplicate rate, correction accuracy, and prompt/token cost. Acceptance: deterministic fixtures can evaluate the diff/retcon pipeline without live capture or paid provider calls; CI can run a small no-network fixture.

title: Reusable streaming WebSocket ASR session harness
description: Factor common cloud streaming ASR client machinery across Deepgram, AssemblyAI, OpenAI Realtime, Soniox, Speechmatics, Gladia, and future providers. title: Pin release rsac input to the same SHA tested by CI
description: Release workflow currently clones rsac from a moving branch while PR CI pins a concrete RSAC_REPO_SHA. Because src-tauri depends on ../../rsac as a path dependency, release can ship rsac code not covered by CI. Scope: make release workflow use the same pinned rsac SHA/input as CI, expose the SHA in workflow_dispatch, and record the rsac revision in release artifacts. Acceptance: a release build cannot use rsac master/main unless that exact SHA was selected and shown in the run summary.

title: Canonical rsac CaptureTarget IPC migration
description: Migrate capture command boundaries from ad hoc UI strings such as system-default and process-tree:<pid> to backend-issued canonical rsac CaptureTarget descriptors/strings (system, device:<id>, app:<pid>, name:<name>, tree:<pid>). Keep legacy parsing for one compatibility window, but emit only canonical SourceDescriptor targets. Acceptance: start_capture accepts canonical descriptors, TS/Rust parsing tests cover legacy and canonical forms, and no new frontend code hand-rolls capture target strings.

source_descriptor_dependency_unblocked_2.summary: audio-graph-3251 now provides a Rust-generated AudioSourceInfo contract with canonical capture_target, device/app/process source variants, backend capability flags, format metadata, optional app bundle_id metadata, permission_status, and typed permission_recovery display metadata. The remaining descriptor-preserving active capture limitation belongs here: start_capture still accepts compatibility source-id strings, so active metadata for app:<pid> cannot reconstruct friendly app name or bundle_id without a structured capture target payload or backend source descriptor lookup cache.
acceptance_additions: start_capture accepts a backend-issued canonical descriptor or typed capture target payload while legacy strings remain for one compatibility window
frontend code stops hand-rolling new capture target strings and uses SourceDescriptor.capture_target or generated helpers
active capture metadata preserves friendly app name and bundle_id when a structured descriptor is supplied or cached
Rust and TS tests cover legacy strings, canonical strings, structured descriptors, and app/process-tree/name modes

canonical_capture_target_migration_2026_.summary: Migrated the capture command boundary to accept backend-issued source descriptors and canonical capture target strings while preserving legacy sourceId compatibility for one window. start_capture now accepts optional source/captureTarget context, resolves descriptor.capture_target before legacy sourceId, and passes descriptor metadata into AudioCaptureManager so active capture metadata can preserve friendly app names, bundle_id, permission status, and permission_recovery. Rust and TypeScript parsers now accept canonical system/tree/name aliases plus legacy forms. New frontend starts pass the selected AudioSourceInfo descriptor when available, stale selections keep the legacy call shape, backend SourceDescriptor system targets emit system, and frontend fallback target construction is centralized in src/utils/captureTarget.ts with canonical app/tree/name/system output.

title: Provider audio policy registry: per-source vs mixed vs sessions
description: Declare each provider consumer's audio policy up front instead of hiding mixing decisions inside provider routing. Policies include PerSource, Mixed synthetic source, and PerSourceSessions; source filters; capacity; drop policy; priority; and attribution semantics. Acceptance: Deepgram/OpenAI single-WebSocket mixed-audio policy, local/fixed-window per-source policy, Gemini/converse policy, AssemblyAI/AWS multi-source attribution or explicit multi-source rejection, and future local S2S policy are represented in one registry used by validation, bus registration, and UI readiness.

diarization_audio_policy_review_2026_06_.summary: Provider audio policy registry should declare diarization/channel behavior as well as mixing behavior. Policies need to distinguish mixed synthetic source, per-source sessions, source-native multichannel, provider-native diarization, local timeline join, and experimental speaker-separated channels so future providers do not duplicate routing logic or assume one OS/source model.
acceptance_additions: registry validation catches providers that claim diarization without parser/fixture support
provider policies include channel limits, source filters, and speaker attribution semantics
UI readiness can explain why a provider cannot handle the selected source/channel mode
local diarization and ASR consumers can share audio through declared bus policies

speaker_channel_routing_decision_2026_06.summary: audio-graph-56da recommends mono processed audio plus revisioned speaker-timeline metadata joins as the default. Source-native multichannel ASR should remain disabled unless source descriptors prove stable channel provenance and provider descriptors prove channel support, max channel count, and speaker/channel attribution semantics. supports_multichannel alone is too coarse; provider audio policy needs explicit speaker_attribution_mode, channel_label_semantics, max_channels, and requires_source_native_channels fields before routing can opt in.
acceptance_additions: provider audio policy distinguishes local_timeline_join, provider_speaker_labels, provider_channel_labels, provider_channel_plus_speaker, and experimental_source_separation
registry validation keeps supports_multichannel false unless source provenance and provider capability are both true
UI readiness explains why selected source/provider falls back to mono plus timeline join

Verified bun typecheck, focused SettingsPage tests, Biome, cargo check, and targeted Rust test.

title: Upgrade AssemblyAI streaming to Universal-3.5 Pro Realtime/v3
description: Move the existing AssemblyAI ASR integration from the older v2 JSON/base64 WebSocket shape to v3 Universal-Streaming. Scope: wss://streaming.assemblyai.com/v3/ws query config, binary audio frames, Authorization header/temp-token support, Begin/Turn/Termination events, end_of_turn handling, word timing, speaker_labels/SpeakerRevision handling, parser fixtures, and backwards-compatible migration for saved settings. Acceptance: AssemblyAI streaming produces normalized partial/final/turn events with optional diarization, uses saved credentials through backend health/model readiness, and old settings either migrate or fail with a clear unsupported-version message.

assemblyai_v3_backend_runtime_2026_06_26.summary: Implemented the backend AssemblyAI v3 cutover: live WebSocket endpoint now uses wss://streaming.assemblyai.com/v3/ws with speech_model=universal-3-5-pro, sample_rate=16000, encoding=pcm_s16le, optional speaker_labels, and Authorization header; audio frames are binary PCM16 LE; Stop sends the v3 Terminate control message. Live server text frames are surfaced as v3 ServerMessage events and parsed in the speech receiver with source-aware AssemblyAiV3Parser. Turn revisions now flow through the shared AsrSpanRevisionPayload path, EndOfTurn emits turn-event, unformatted final turns are downgraded to non-durable span revisions to avoid duplicate transcript/notes/graph/proposal side effects before the formatted final, and SpeakerRevision emits diarization-span-revision sideband events keyed by turn. Registry default model/transport now reports universal-3-5-pro + web_socket_binary and generated TS registry is current.

title: Evaluate Google Chirp and Azure Speech for enterprise STT
description: Evaluate Google Chirp 3 streaming and Azure Speech as enterprise STT candidates after the WebSocket-first provider work lands. Scope: auth burden, gRPC/SDK requirements, platform packaging impact, model/language catalog APIs, diarization/endpointing semantics, data residency, and whether these should be native providers or optional enterprise adapters. Acceptance: research note ranks Google/Azure against Soniox, AssemblyAI v3, Gladia, and Speechmatics; no implementation starts until credential/config and packaging costs are explicit.

google_azure_enterprise_stt_evaluation_2.summary: Researched official Google Cloud and Microsoft Learn sources for Google Chirp 3 and Azure Speech. Decision: keep both as optional enterprise adapters behind Soniox, AssemblyAI v3, Gladia, and Speechmatics. Google is blocked by gRPC-only streaming plus immature Rust streaming RPC coverage and no safe claim for live diarization; Azure has real-time diarization but needs SDK/native packaging, endpoint/private-domain settings, and headless health probes before provider exposure.
