title: Transcript revision ledger and canonical span projection
description: Implement a transcript revision ledger that applies normalized ASR partial/final revisions into a canonical transcript view while preserving an immutable audit log. Scope: span identity generation, provider_item_id preservation, revision ordering, superseded partial handling, finalization, transcript_hash calculation for projection basis checks, and session persistence/replay. Acceptance: live transcript UI and saved sessions can show current text without duplicating partials; notes/graph jobs consume stable finalized or high-stability spans; replay from events produces identical canonical spans.

ledger_review_2026_06_24.summary: Projection/data-flow review recommends making TranscriptLedger the canonical transcript view, with legacy transcript rows derived from immutable ASR and diarization span revisions. Partials as well as finals should be persisted so projection jobs can coalesce, basis-check, and retcon notes/graph state accurately.
acceptance_additions: partial revisions are persisted and replayed, not only held in live UI state
canonical transcript state is rebuilt from events with deterministic supersession ordering
projection basis includes span ids, revision numbers, and transcript hash from the ledger
legacy saved-session transcript views are derived from ledger projection or clearly migrated

Keep broad partial persistence gated until legacy session migration and downstream frontend/session lifecycle work are done.

title: Structured LLM schemas for notes and graph patches
description: Define structured output schemas for notes and temporal graph projection patches so LLM synthesis emits operations, not replacement prose. Scope: NoteOp insert/update/delete/reorder with stable note ids and transcript provenance; GraphOp add/update/merge/split/invalidate/strengthen/weaken edge/node with valid_from/valid_until/confidence/provenance; JSON schema or typed validation; repair prompts for invalid patches. Acceptance: patch validators reject malformed/stale operations; fixture tests cover duplicate entity avoidance, later-context correction, and notes item retcon; schemas are provider-agnostic across OpenRouter/OpenAI/local LLM paths.

structured_patch_basis_review_2026_06_24.summary: Projection/data-flow review recommends every LLM output be a structured patch with operation-level provenance and basis metadata. The schema should support repairs and retcons over append-only prose, and validators should reject patches whose transcript basis is stale, malformed, or missing required provenance.
acceptance_additions: NoteOp and GraphOp schemas include projection sequence, transcript span provenance, confidence, and basis reference
validators reject replacement prose, duplicate entity creation, stale basis hashes, and unsupported operation ordering
repair prompts can request a smaller corrected patch without reprocessing the full session
schemas work across OpenRouter, OpenAI-compatible, local LLM, and future provider paths

structured_projection_patch_draft_schema.summary: Defined a provider-agnostic model-output draft schema for projection patches. The schema accepts only operations plus optional confidence, rejects model-supplied trusted top-level metadata through deny_unknown_fields, rejects malformed replacement prose, wrong-kind operations, blank ids/fields, invalid confidence, and out-of-range graph edge weights. Trusted ProjectionPatch metadata is stamped from ProjectionJob/runtime context.
remaining: provider-specific structured-output optimization for vLLM/MistralRs
repair prompts for invalid patch drafts
fixture tests for duplicate entity avoidance and later-context correction

ad44_dependency_reconciliation_2026_06_2.summary: Removed stale audio-graph-ad44 blocker. Remaining e9b6 work is schema-specific repair prompting and fixture coverage, not blocked on ad44.

repair_prompt_retry_slice_2026_06_25.summary: Closed child audio-graph-7fb0. Projection patch generation now retries at most once with a provider-agnostic repair prompt when first-pass model JSON fails schema validation. The repair prompt includes validation error, expected kind, schema, and compact invalid output while forbidding model-owned trusted metadata; executor retries the same successful backend attempt, sums real token usage, stamps repaired patches with projection_patch_repair_v1, and fails after one repair without a third call.
remaining: fixture coverage for duplicate entity avoidance, later-context correction, and notes item retcon behavior
provider-specific structured-output optimization for vLLM/MistralRs or other local structured-output surfaces
cross-platform GitHub/Blacksmith validation on a clean branch

duplicate_id_retcon_fixture_slice_2026_0.summary: Closed child audio-graph-0e3a and then closed e9b6. ProjectionPatchDraft validation now rejects duplicate operation identities inside one model draft, and provider-agnostic fixtures prove later note and graph context can update stable ids through patch operations rather than replacement prose. Provider-specific structured-output optimization was split into follow-up audio-graph-685c instead of blocking the schema contract.

Provider-specific structured-output optimization moved to audio-graph-685c.

title: Projection queue latency and backpressure telemetry
description: Instrument the transcript-to-notes/graph projection queue so batching decisions are visible and tunable. Scope: TTFT estimate per LLM provider/model, total latency, queued span count, coalesced spans, in-flight job age, stale-discard count, repair-job count, token cost, and UI diagnostics hooks. Acceptance: recorded-session replay and live sessions can report projection lag and patch latency; scheduler tests can assert adaptive coalescing behavior; user-facing diagnostics distinguish ASR delay from LLM projection delay.

projection_telemetry_review_2026_06_24.summary: Projection/data-flow review recommends queue observability that makes TTFT/backpressure decisions explainable. Telemetry should report provider/model TTFT estimates, in-flight age, queued span count, coalesced span count, stale-discard count, repair-job count, token cost, patch latency, and materializer lag.
acceptance_additions: scheduler tests assert adaptive coalescing based on TTFT/in-flight state
diagnostics distinguish capture/ASR latency from LLM projection delay and materializer delay

title: Frontend reducers for transcript, notes, and graph retcon events

Acceptance: partial transcript revisions replace the same span, notes corrections update in place, graph merge/split/invalidate events render deterministically, and tests cover out-of-order/stale events without duplicate UI artifacts.

title: Fix OpenRouter test/model discovery to honor draft base URL
description: OpenRouter Settings exposes an editable base URL, but test_openrouter_connection_cmd and list_openrouter_models_cmd currently use openrouter::DEFAULT_BASE_URL. Scope: thread the draft/saved base URL from SettingsPage into both Test Connection and Load Models, validate/normalize it consistently, and keep OpenRouter model catalog cache keyed by base URL plus API key presence. Acceptance: OpenRouter Test and Load Models honor non-default base URLs without requiring Save; tests cover a custom base URL and ensure no credential/settings persistence occurs.
closeReason: Fixed OpenRouter Test Connection and Load Models to honor the editable endpoint/base URL. Settings now normalizes the draft OpenRouter URL, sends it to both Tauri commands, and keys the model catalog cache by non-secret request inputs so endpoint changes refetch. Backend commands accept optional baseUrl and default only when blank/omitted. Added regression coverage for custom endpoint and cache invalidation. Verified bun typecheck, focused SettingsPage tests, Biome, and cargo check.

title: ElevenLabs Scribe v2 Realtime provider watch/spike
description: Track ElevenLabs Scribe v2 Realtime as a candidate from the Artificial Analysis streaming STT list, but keep it behind Soniox/AssemblyAI v3/Gladia/Speechmatics until the provider registry and normalized span-revision contract exist. Current API shape: wss://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/speech-to-text/realtime, model_id=scribe_v2_realtime, partial_transcript and committed_transcript events, optional word timestamps/language detection, manual or VAD commit strategy, keyterms/no_verbatim, and xi-api-key or single-use token auth. Acceptance: after registry lands, produce a spike note comparing its partial/committed semantics, timestamp support, retention/logging controls, cost, and transcript/graph suitability; do not expose in Settings before health/model readiness and parser fixtures exist.
closeReason: Research spikes (commit 81dc708): decision docs in docs/research/spike-*.md with grounded recommendations; go/no-go = user

title: Fix backend generic OpenRouter endpoint credential routing
description: Rust credential_key_for_endpoint still maps generic OpenRouter-compatible URLs to openai_api_key while the frontend maps them to openrouter_api_key. This means saved generic LLM/ASR Api endpoints pointed at openrouter.ai can fail to hydrate from the OpenRouter credential slot even though first-class OpenRouter works. Scope: update Rust endpoint routing, add tests for known endpoint patterns and runtime hydration from openrouter_api_key. Acceptance: TS and Rust endpoint credential routing agree for OpenRouter/Groq/Together/Fireworks/Gemini/OpenAI, and generic OpenRouter Api settings hydrate from openrouter_api_key without plaintext UI readback.
closeReason: Fixed backend generic OpenRouter endpoint credential routing end to end. Rust credential_key_for_endpoint now maps OpenRouter URLs to openrouter_api_key and credential_value_for_endpoint reads the OpenRouter store slot; added routing and hydration regression tests. Verified cargo check and targeted routing/hydration tests.

title: Fix Sherpa streaming preflight model filename drift
description: Sherpa streaming capture preflight checks encoder.onnx/decoder.onnx/joiner.onnx, but SherpaStreamingWorker and model metadata expect encoder-epoch-99-avg-1.onnx, decoder-epoch-99-avg-1.onnx, joiner-epoch-99-avg-1.onnx, and tokens.txt. A valid downloaded model can be rejected before capture, or the checks can fail to catch the real runtime missing files. Scope: make preflight use the same required-file list as the runtime/model catalog and add regression coverage. Acceptance: start_capture preflight and Sherpa runtime agree on required filenames; corrupt/missing model tests mention the actual missing filename.
closeReason: Fixed Sherpa streaming preflight to use the canonical SHERPA_ZIPFORMER_REQUIRED_FILES list shared with model validation/runtime filenames, and reject zero-byte required files before capture. Verified cargo check and existing Sherpa required-file validation test.

title: Provider registry skeleton and descriptor coverage tests
description: Implement the first backend-only provider registry slice under the provider-capability epic. Scope: add Rust ProviderDescriptor structs and static entries for current ASR variants, LLM variants, TTS none/deepgram_aura, and Gemini/OpenAI realtime-agent surfaces; expose a read-only get_provider_registry_cmd; add tests that every Rust settings enum variant has a descriptor, provider ids are unique, credential keys are allowed, model/default files match runtime checks, and source_policy can replace single_session_streaming_asr_name. Out of scope: moving the whole Settings UI onto generated schema in this slice. Acceptance: registry descriptors catch drift like provider ids, credential keys, source policy, and local model file names before TS/UI refactors land.
closeReason: Implemented first backend-owned provider registry slice. Added ProviderDescriptor/static registry for current ASR, LLM, TTS, Gemini Live, and planned OpenAI Realtime voice-agent surfaces; exposed get_provider_registry_cmd; moved ASR single-session source guard to registry source_policy; added TS ProviderDescriptor contract types; added coverage tests for ids, settings variants, credential allowlist keys, local model file constants, realtime-agent surfaces, and source-policy behavior. Verified cargo check, cargo test provider_registry::tests, cargo test streaming_source_guard, bun typecheck, and Biome on src/types/index.ts.

title: Session artifact migration for transcript and projection events
description: Define and implement session artifact migration and rotation semantics for the event-sourced transcript/projection pipeline. Scope: transcript event JSONL path, projection event JSONL path, notes materialization path, graph materialization path, session metadata schema/versioning, recovery from partial writes, delete/purge behavior, and new-session reset/flush behavior for transcript ledger, projection queue, notes, and graph state. Acceptance: starting a new session cannot leak prior graph/projection state into the new session; loading old sessions remains compatible; new sessions list and restore transcript events, projection events, notes, and graph artifacts deterministically.

title: Local streaming diarization and speaker timeline architecture
description: Build diarization as a first-class local/provider-agnostic speaker timeline service instead of a provider-specific checkbox. Provider diarization exists in Deepgram/AWS and planned Soniox/AssemblyAI/Speechmatics paths, but the app also needs local streaming diarization that works when providers lack speaker labels and can merge or override provider speaker metadata. Scope: define DiarizationSpan/SpeakerTimeline events with stable speaker IDs, rolling-window revisions, source_id/channel metadata, confidence, basis transcript/audio ranges, flexible auto/max speaker-count policy, local-only/provider-only/hybrid modes, model readiness, persistence, and UI health state. Acceptance: local diarization can run alongside any ASR provider; ASR span revisions can reference stable speaker IDs; provider speaker labels normalize into the same timeline; settings expose simple defaults plus advanced speaker-count controls; recorded-session fixtures can replay transcript + diarization projections deterministically.

title: Local streaming diarization worker with flexible speaker counts
description: Implement the local diarization runtime behind a provider-neutral worker. Build on the existing ADR-0017 direction, Sortformer/clustering experiments, and model-readiness descriptors, but make the runtime emit revisioned speaker-timeline events instead of directly mutating transcript labels. Scope: rolling audio windows, overlap-aware scheduling, speaker embedding/clustering where available, stable speaker registry, auto speaker-count mode, optional max speakers, confidence/stability scores, feature gating for ORT conflicts, and graceful fallback when models are missing. Acceptance: local diarization works with cloud ASR, Sherpa, local Whisper, and future providers; tests cover speaker label stability across rolling windows; model readiness reports missing dependencies before capture; settings can choose auto/unbounded or a bounded speaker cap.

provider_neutral_consumer_review_2026_06.summary: Diarization review recommends making local streaming diarization a first-class processed-audio consumer instead of a side path that competes with ASR providers. The worker should consume source-local audio windows from the audio consumer bus, emit revisioned speaker-timeline events, and enrich ASR spans through a metadata join. It should not overwrite provider-native speaker labels or synthesize speaker-separated channels from mixed mono audio by default.
acceptance_additions: local diarization can run alongside Deepgram, AWS, OpenAI Realtime, Sherpa, AssemblyAI, and Soniox without provider-specific audio forks
missing model/dependency state is reported through readiness before capture starts
provider-native speaker labels are preserved unless a higher-confidence local revision explicitly supersedes them with provenance
flexible speaker counts support auto/unbounded plus optional max-speaker caps without assuming a fixed channel count

diarization_compile_review_2026_06_25.summary: Review found the optional diarization-clustering feature failed to compile because DiarizationSpanRevisionPayload was missing capture_latency_ms/asr_latency_ms while speech emitters supplied them. The payload contract and TypeScript event shape now include optional latency fields, and the transcript-derived diarization initializer supplies None. Remaining acceptance is tracked in audio-graph-b05b for macOS/Windows/Linux CI smoke evidence before this epic closes.

Speaker attribution should remain timeline metadata, not synthesized speaker channels.
candidates: sherpa-onnx: local cross-platform Rust wrapper/engine, Apache-2 engine, supports diarization/VAD/enhancement/source separation; needs rolling windows and label stabilization for live use; model licenses must be reviewed per artifact.

parakeet-rs Sortformer: Rust, MIT/Apache code, NVIDIA streaming Sortformer supports up to 4 speakers; fixed max-speaker model and ORT/link/model-license risk require optional feature/process isolation.
pyannote/diart: research oracle only, not embedded Rust desktop runtime.
acceptance: Measured label latency and cross-window speaker-label churn on overlap/turn-taking fixtures.

title: Investigate speaker-separated multichannel ASR feed vs metadata join
description: Research whether the desired speaker-attributed input to transcribers should be implemented as actual multi-channel audio or as a speaker-timeline metadata join. Diarization can label who spoke when, but it does not cleanly separate a mixed mono signal; actual per-speaker channels require source capture with separate channels or an added source-separation/speaker-extraction stage. Scope: compare provider support for multi-channel ASR/channel diarization, local source-separation options, rsac capture capabilities, latency/quality cost, and failure modes. Acceptance: produce a design recommendation that chooses per provider/source mode: mono audio + speaker timeline, source-native multichannel, or experimental separated-speaker channels; include tests/fixtures needed before implementation.

metadata_join_decision_review_2026_06_24.summary: Diarization review recommends choosing mono audio plus revisioned speaker-timeline metadata join as the default architecture. Source-native multichannel can be enabled only when capture metadata and provider descriptors prove channel semantics. Separated-speaker PCM lanes should remain experimental because diarization labels mixed audio but does not reliably separate overlapping voices into clean channels.
acceptance_additions: design compares source-native multichannel, timeline metadata join, and experimental separated-speaker channels per provider/source
provider descriptors state max channels, diarization mode, and whether channel attribution is supported
rsac source descriptors carry enough channel/source provenance before any channel-aware provider routing is enabled
failure modes for overlap, speaker-count changes, and source separation artifacts are documented before implementation

mono_pipeline_boundary_review_2026_06_25.summary: Diarization review found the current source pipeline still collapses interleaved capture to mono before ASR and provider descriptors generally advertise mono input / supports_multichannel=false. Treat speaker attribution as a revisioned metadata join by default until rsac/source descriptors and provider capability metadata prove channel semantics. Speaker-separated PCM lanes should stay experimental and source/provenance-gated, not inferred from diarization labels alone.

local diarization emits session/source timeline metadata rather than provider-ready speaker channels
provider capability descriptors currently do not establish multichannel ASR routing

Next step is to turn the existing findings into a dated design recommendation with provider/source mode matrix, failure modes, and fixture/test requirements before closing this investigation.

title: Redact upstream provider error bodies before returning UI errors
description: Provider probes and API clients sometimes include upstream response bodies in returned errors. A malicious or echoing endpoint can reflect a submitted API key, sending it to UI state/log consoles. Scope: add a small redaction helper for known submitted secrets and credential-like tokens before constructing AppError::Unknown/string errors in test_cloud_asr_connection, OpenRouter model/chat errors, generic LLM ApiClient errors, and cloud ASR errors. Acceptance: unit tests feed response bodies containing draft/saved sentinel keys and assert returned errors do not contain the sentinel while preserving status/category/actionable context.
closeReason: Implemented shared provider-error redaction for known submitted secrets before UI-visible diagnostics. Covered test_cloud_asr_connection, generic CloudAsr worker errors, generic LLM ApiClient non-2xx errors, and OpenRouter model/chat errors. title: Speaker timeline to channel-aware ASR projection
description: Design and implement the bridge from local/provider diarization spans into ASR-friendly speaker attribution. Scope: define when to use provider diarization, local diarization, or hybrid reconciliation; map DiarizationSpan/SpeakerTimeline revisions onto normalized ASR span revisions; evaluate channel-aware providers that can consume separate audio channels; if feasible, build a bounded speaker-channel mux that projects stable local speaker IDs into per-speaker or per-source PCM lanes without breaking timing, overlap handling, backpressure, or provider sample-rate/channel limits. Must support flexible/unknown speaker counts, but cap exported physical/provider channels per provider and preserve overflow/overlap metadata. Acceptance: provider descriptors declare diarization support and channel handling; local diarization can enrich ASR events without re-transcribing; providers with multi-channel support can opt into deterministic channel projection; replay fixtures prove speaker remaps generate revisions/diffs rather than duplicate transcript entries.

title: Capture stop should tear down native converse runtime consumer/session
description: Found while splitting processed-audio runtime consumers. stop_capture currently stops transcription and Gemini notes when the last capture source stops, but it does not explicitly tear down an active native converse session. With runtime audio consumers this can leave is_converse_active, the Gemini client, playback, and gemini-converse consumer lifecycle dependent on later stop_converse/manual cleanup. Acceptance: when remaining captures reaches zero, active converse is stopped or transitioned through the same teardown path as stop_converse; the gemini-converse consumer unregisters; playback stops; thread handles are taken/joined bounded; tests cover capture-stop while converse is active without requiring a live socket.

title: Moonshine local STT runtime architecture spike
description: Decide the implementation path for the Moonshine local streaming STT provider before adding runtime dependencies. Compare UsefulSensors moonshine Python/Keras package, Hugging Face Moonshine Streaming checkpoints, sherpa-onnx Moonshine ONNX packaging, and a Rust ONNX Runtime route. Acceptance: choose a cross-platform runtime path; document model files/downloads, cargo feature shape asr-moonshine, expected audio chunk contract, partial/final emission strategy, packaging risk on macOS/Windows/Linux, and first implementation slice. Research sources so far: UsefulSensors/moonshine HF model card, Hugging Face Transformers Moonshine Streaming docs, sherpa-onnx Moonshine HF package, and the Moonshine arXiv paper.

moonshine_native_c_api_decision_2026_06_.decision: Use the Moonshine Voice native C API as the production runtime behind optional asr-moonshine. Keep Python and Hugging Face Transformers as tooling/reference only; keep sherpa-onnx Moonshine as fallback if native packaging fails; do not hand-roll ONNX Runtime first.

title: Persist capture/ASR timing for projection replay latency split
description: Found while advancing audio-graph-3f24. The live projection diagnostics can now show capture latency, ASR latency, ASR event age, projection queue lag, LLM generation, and materializer apply separately, but recorded replay cannot split capture-to-ASR, ASR-to-queue, queue, generation, and apply timing because TranscriptEvent/AsrSpanRevisionPayload persist only received_at_ms. Scope: add optional non-secret timing fields that let replay distinguish capture/ASR processing delay from projection/LLM/materializer delay; preserve backward compatibility for older transcript JSONL; extend ProjectionReplayLatencyMetrics/UI/tests without logging transcript text, graph labels, or credentials. Acceptance: existing old replay fixtures still load; new fixture proves capture/ASR timing split; get_projection_replay_report_cmd reports separate capture_asr, asr_to_queue, projection_queue, generation, and apply maxima/totals where available; UI renders the split clearly.

title: LocalLlama streaming chat via persistent-context actor
description: Child of audio-graph-b373. Add streaming chat support for LocalLlama using the existing llama.cpp actor/context loop rather than moving LlamaContext across threads. Acceptance: LocalLlama enters the streaming branch, emits TokenDelta deltas plus exactly one done/error/cancel terminal event, reports usage where available, honors cancellation between tokens, preserves blocking chat behavior, and cloud/no-llm-llama stubs compile.

worker_local_llama_streaming_2026_06_25.summary: Implemented a narrow LocalLlama streaming compatibility adapter on the provider-neutral StreamChatRequest path. The adapter requires explicit StreamBackendHandles.local_llama, calls the existing persistent-context LlmEngine::chat on a blocking thread, emits one complete TokenDelta::Delta without pretending token boundaries, then emits exactly one Done/Error/Cancelled terminal event. Local token usage maps only total_tokens when the engine reports a non-zero ChatOutcome.tokens_used value; prompt/completion split remains None. Added focused LocalLlama tests for missing handle, unloaded engine handle, and feature-gated successful single-delta/done via a test actor handle.

classifications.provider: xAI Grok Speech to Text Streaming
classifications.evidence: Official xAI STT docs expose wss://api.x.ai/v1/stt, raw binary audio frames, query-configured sample rate/encoding/interim/endpointing/multichannel/keyterm/smart_turn, transcript.partial/transcript.done events, and optional diarization in voice reference docs.

classifications.recommendation: watch descriptor, likely enterprise/self-hosted profile first
classifications.evidence: NVIDIA Speech NIM exposes Nemotron ASR Streaming through deployable gRPC/realtime services; Together exposes nvidia/nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b through audio transcription APIs. This is valuable but has heavier packaging/deployment assumptions than direct WebSocket providers.

Official Inworld STT docs expose wss://api.inworld.ai/stt/v1/transcribe:streamBidirectional, first transcribeConfig message, audioChunk streaming, interim/final transcription, speechStarted/speechStopped, endTurn/closeStream, LINEAR16 streaming, and optional Voice Profile.

Official Smallest docs expose wss://api.smallest.ai/waves/v1/stt/live?model=pulse with bearer auth, raw audio binary frames, is_final/is_last transcription messages, finalize and close_stream controls, and word timestamp options. Pulse Pro is HTTP-only for now.

Official Gradium docs expose wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr, x-api-key auth, setup/audio/end_of_stream messages, text/end_text/step/flushed events, semantic VAD, flush, multiplexing, and PCM/WAV/Opus/mu-law/a-law formats.

Official Mistral docs expose realtime transcription with voxtral-mini-transcribe-realtime-2602 through client.audio.realtime.transcribe_stream, audio byte streams, session/text delta/done/error events, target_streaming_delay_ms, and note that realtime is not compatible with diarize.

Official Qwen/Alibaba docs expose qwen3-asr-flash-realtime over WebSocket, DashScope bearer auth, VAD/manual modes, base64 audio append/commit, pcm/opus formats, emotion recognition, no speaker diarization, and China/international endpoints.

Official Cartesia docs expose Realtime STT Auto /stt/turns/websocket with built-in turn detection and Realtime STT Manual /stt/websocket with finalize, binary audio, query params for model/encoding/sample_rate/cartesia_version, and Ink-2 turn lifecycle events. Current Ink-2 is English-only.

title: LocalLlama token-callback streaming and interruptible cancellation
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-e2b6. The current LocalLlama streaming compatibility adapter uses the existing blocking LlmEngine::chat path and emits one honest full-text delta, so cancellation finalizes the wrapper/UI but cannot interrupt llama.cpp inference once decode starts. Scope: expose token callback streaming from the persistent-context actor without moving LlamaContext across threads; emit real TokenDelta pieces; check CancellationToken between generated tokens; release the local engine promptly on cancel; preserve blocking chat behavior and token usage telemetry. Acceptance: LocalLlama start_streaming_chat emits multiple deltas when the backend produces tokens, cancel_streaming_chat stops decode between tokens and frees the engine for a subsequent request, tests cover cancel-before-start/cancel-mid-decode/happy path/error path, and cloud/no-llm stubs still compile.

engine_loop_refactor_plan_2026_06_25.summary: Read-only engine review found that resolved llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 does not expose a safe generation token-callback or streaming iterator for local generation. The correct callback point is AudioGraph owned: engine.rs already samples one token, converts it with token_to_piece, appends it, and decodes the next token on the existing actor thread. Implement true LocalLlama streaming by adding an EngineReq::StreamChat path and emitting local stream events from that loop while checking cancellation between tokens.
implementation_plan: Add EngineReq::StreamChat carrying messages, graph context, StreamParams, cancellation signal, and bounded event sender.
Add engine-level stream events for delta, done, cancelled, and error; bridge those to TokenDelta in streaming.rs.
Refactor chat prompt/sampler setup out of do_chat so blocking chat and streaming reuse the same prompt path.
Split run_inference into a shared inner loop that optionally emits token_to_piece output and checks cancellation before the next token decode.
Keep StreamBackendHandles boundary unchanged and keep executor.rs blocking chat on engine.chat.
Do not hold Arc<Mutex<Option<LlmEngine>>> for the whole stream; clone the lightweight engine sender handle under the mutex and drop the guard before waiting.
risks: Cancellation can stop between generated tokens but cannot interrupt an in-progress ctx.decode call through the current safe API.
Prompt prefill is not interruptible except before/after decode.
Usage should preserve prompt/completion/total counts where possible; current Cancelled terminal frames carry no usage.
Actor serialization remains intentional because one LlamaContext decodes one request at a time.

implementation_started_2026_06_25.summary: Starting the true LocalLlama streaming implementation in the repo-owned engine actor loop. Scope is limited to src-tauri/src/llm/engine.rs, src-tauri/src/llm/streaming.rs, and Seed metadata; provider registry/generated/docs/CI/Settings files are intentionally untouched in this dirty checkout.

preserve blocking LlmEngine::chat behavior and usage telemetry

title: Wire rubato output resampling into CPAL playback
description: Follow-up from the Rust ecosystem audit. Playback currently accepts provider PCM but the CPAL output path notes no output resampling, so 24 kHz TTS/native-S2S audio can pitch-shift on common 48 kHz devices. Scope: add a backend-owned rubato-backed output resampling stage before the realtime ring buffer or otherwise prove device-rate negotiation handles Aura/native-S2S formats. Acceptance: 24 kHz and 16 kHz PCM play correctly on 48 kHz devices without blocking the CPAL callback; frame accounting and cancellation tests cover 24k->48k and 16k->48k; Linux/macOS/Windows behavior is documented or CI-smoked where practical.

rubato_research_refresh_2026_06_25.summary: Rust ecosystem review confirms rubato is the right existing dependency for provider/playback sample-rate adaptation. Keep resampling producer-side before the CPAL callback/ring buffer; do not add rodio for low-latency barge-in playback.
acceptance_additions: 24 kHz and 16 kHz provider/TTS PCM play correctly on common 48 kHz devices
resampling never runs inside the realtime CPAL callback
frame accounting and cancellation tests cover 24k->48k and 16k->48k
Linux/macOS/Windows behavior is CI-smoked or documented with evidence

playback_resampling_slice_2026_06_25.summary: Implemented producer-side playback output resampling with the existing rubato/audioadapter stack. CPAL streams now request the device default/preferred sample rate, while AudioPlayer stores a per-stream producer wrapper that resamples source-rate mono i16 samples before they enter the ring buffer. The CPAL callback remains a device-rate ring-buffer drain plus cancel/silence/sample-format conversion path. Added explicit flush_samples for resampler tail handling; speak-aloud flushes when the TTS event stream ends, and the converse FSM emits FlushPlayback on GenerationComplete while staying in Speaking. Cancel resets producer-side resampler buffers so partial source chunks are not replayed after resume.
subagent_findings_integrated: Russell audit: stream negotiation should use device default rate and keep resampling out of CallbackState/CPAL callback.
Russell audit: producer-side buffers need flush/reset coverage so final chunks and cancel do not strand or replay stale audio.

Curie audit: reuse existing rubato/audioadapter dependencies rather than adding rodio or a second resampler dependency; hardware-free unit tests are required because headless CI may lack audio devices.

title: VAD and AEC crate bakeoff for local turn detection and barge-in
description: Follow-up from the Rust ecosystem audit. Evaluate Rust VAD/AEC options before hard-wiring local turn detection or barge-in behavior. Scope: compare earshot, silero/silero-vad-rs, fast-vad or wavekat-vad, and WebRTC AEC/NS/AGC options such as webrtc-audio-processing and sonora against curated 16 kHz chunks plus playback-reference fixtures. Acceptance: dated bakeoff report with license/build/runtime notes for Windows/macOS/Linux, false-start/noise/echo metrics, model/native dependency footprint, and a keep/build/buy recommendation; no runtime dependency is adopted without CI build proof.

crate_bakeoff_refresh_2026_06_25.summary: Rust ecosystem review recommends a bakeoff rather than immediate adoption. earshot is the best pure-Rust first VAD candidate; silero-vad-rs/vad-rs/wavekat-vad are accuracy candidates with ORT/packaging risk; webrtc-audio-processing and sonora are AEC/NS/AGC candidates with native/MSRV/licensing tradeoffs.
acceptance_additions: compare earshot, silero-vad-rs or vad-rs, wavekat-vad, webrtc-audio-processing, and sonora on curated speech/noise/echo fixtures
record license, MSRV, native dependency, ONNX Runtime conflict, and Windows/macOS/Linux build notes
recommend one local turn detector path and one AEC/NS path; no runtime dependency adopted without CI proof

queue_reorg_2026_06_25.summary: Lowered from P1 to P2 because it is a required bakeoff before barge-in/local turn-detection adoption, but it does not block the core transcript/notes/graph or cross-platform packaging cutline.

local_turn_vad_aec_bakeoff_research_2026.summary: Research scout recommends splitting this into two measured outputs: a local turn/VAD recommendation and an AEC/noise-suppression recommendation. Keep rsac capture and canonical 16 kHz mono ProcessedAudioChunk bus as baseline; do not attach AEC directly to the ASR bus because AEC needs a playback/render reference path and 10 ms capture/render frames before downmix/resample.

vad: earshot first: pure Rust, MIT/Apache, small footprint, 16 ms frames at 16 kHz, avoids ONNX Runtime conflicts.
silero as accuracy candidate behind ORT-isolated feature/process; streaming 8/16 kHz 512-sample chunks but pulls ort.
fast-vad as alternate matching 512-sample chunks, but default pyo3/numpy deps are poor Tauri fit.
aec_ns: sonora first for pure Rust WebRTC APM-style AEC3/NS/AGC/HPF with BSD-3 licensing and 10 ms 48 kHz examples.
webrtc-audio-processing as battle-tested wrapper candidate but C++/system/bundled build risks require CI proof.
nnnoiseless/RNNoise useful as NS-only baseline but not AEC/barge-in.

acceptance: Windows/macOS/Linux build proof for selected candidates.
16 kHz VAD frame metrics: false-start rate, missed speech/barge-in rate, latency, CPU/RTF, binary/model footprint.
48 kHz 10 ms AEC frame adapter proof with render-reference alignment.
Echo-leak rate and user-barge-in-over-assistant fixture metrics.
Decision doc separating turn detector recommendation from AEC/NS recommendation.
guardrails: AEC/NS runs before ASR downmix/resample when a playback reference is available.
Do not let NS-only candidates satisfy AEC/barge-in acceptance.
Do not add ORT-heavy VAD candidates to default features without optional feature/process isolation.

closeReason: Research spikes (commit 81dc708): decision docs in docs/research/spike-*.md with grounded recommendations; go/no-go = user

title: Credential and YAML config backend migration ADR
description: Follow-up from the Rust ecosystem audit. App settings already use YAML and credentials.yaml with redaction/owner-only writes, but serde_yaml is no longer maintained and plaintext local secrets are an operational risk. Scope: write an ADR and migration spike for OS keychain/Stronghold/keyring primary secret storage, YAML fallback/import, config parser replacement or compatibility wrapper, saved-key health/model discovery behavior, and headless CI/dev-mode behavior. Acceptance: ADR covers Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service/headless fallback, no plaintext secrets in config.yaml/logs/Seeds, config compatibility fixtures, and a staged migration plan that preserves existing credentials.yaml users.

adr_0019_credential_config_migration_202.summary: Added ADR-0019 for credential and config storage migration. Decision: introduce a backend CredentialBackend facade, keep YAML as compatibility/import fallback, prefer OS-native keychain storage for production desktop builds, keep Stronghold as a future backend candidate, and add ConfigCodec fixtures before replacing serde_yaml.
acceptance_coverage: Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, and headless fallback are covered in the target architecture
no plaintext secrets in config.yaml/logs/Seeds/frontend state remains an explicit acceptance gate
saved-key readiness and model discovery stay backend-owned without key re-entry
existing credentials.yaml users get idempotent non-destructive import and YAML fallback
config parser replacement is gated by compatibility fixtures

closeReason: ADR-0019 added with migration decision, platform/headless behavior, saved-key readiness requirements, config codec fixture plan, and implementation follow-up Seeds audio-graph-799a/audio-graph-0c08/audio-graph-6ec7/audio-graph-a3d8.

title: Reusable ASR provider transport and parser fixture harness
description: Follow-up from the Rust ecosystem audit and provider expansion roadmap. Existing WebSocket/SSE plumbing is capable, but Soniox, Gladia, Speechmatics, RevAI, AssemblyAI v3, Deepgram, and OpenAI Realtime need repeated reconnect/backlog/keepalive/terminal/parser code. Scope: build an internal provider transport harness over tokio-tungstenite plus fixture-driven parser tests, considering insta/serde_path_to_error for snapshots/diagnostics. Acceptance: harness models connect/init/audio/write/read/reconnect/cancel/terminal behavior without leaking secrets; parser fixtures cover partial/final/revision semantics for implemented and planned providers; adding Soniox/Speechmatics/Gladia does not require copy-pasting provider lifecycle code.

provider_research_refresh_2026_06_25.summary: Provider research confirms the real blocker for Soniox/Speechmatics/Gladia/AssemblyAI v3 is a reusable lifecycle harness, not provider choice. Keep current tokio-tungstenite/reqwest streaming primitives, but centralize connect/init/audio/write/read/reconnect/cancel/terminal/keepalive behavior and fixture-driven parsers before live runtimes.
acceptance_additions: fake WebSocket server tests cover connect/init/audio/write/read/reconnect/cancel/terminal behavior
fixture packs cover Soniox v5, AssemblyAI Universal-3.5 Pro Realtime/v3, Speechmatics Realtime, Gladia Live, RevAI, Deepgram, and OpenAI Realtime
parser diagnostics use serde_path_to_error or equivalent structured paths without logging plaintext credentials
provider speaker metadata and turn events are normalized separately from transcript finality
normal CI uses parser/fake-server fixtures; live smokes stay env-gated

asr_harness_subagent_design_2026_06_25.summary: Provider architecture subagent confirmed the correct first abstraction is WebSocket-only transport plus normalized parser fixtures, not a universal SDK/gRPC sidecar abstraction. Deepgram should be the extraction source; Soniox/Gladia/Speechmatics/RevAI parser fixtures should move first because they are parser/config-first modules today.
architecture_decisions: Add backend ASR WebSocket transport under src-tauri/src/asr/transport.rs for connect/init/audio/read/write/reconnect/cancel/terminal/keepalive behavior.
Add normalized parser fixture harness under src-tauri/src/asr/fixtures.rs plus src-tauri/fixtures/asr/... targeting AsrSpanRevisionPayload and TranscriptLedger outcomes.
Keep protocol-specific config builders, JSON parsers, audio encoding quirks, close-code classification, and diarization knobs inside provider modules.
Do not abstract AWS SDK, gRPC, SDK-native, sidecar, or browser-origin capture in this first harness wave.
Provider registry remains metadata/capability/readiness descriptor input, not runtime behavior control.
implementation_order: normalized fixtures for Soniox, Gladia, Speechmatics, RevAI parser modules
Deepgram lifecycle extraction into fake WebSocket runner tests
port AssemblyAI then OpenAI Realtime ASR wrapper
implement Soniox/Speechmatics/RevAI live runtimes through the harness
add Gladia REST-init/tokenized WebSocket and ack/range replay after simple WS providers
fixture_coverage: partial/final/revision collapse and stale revision rejection
invalid JSON diagnostics and provider errors
speaker/channel metadata without treating provider speaker IDs as durable identity
terminal/close frames and redacted URL/header diagnostics

Coverage now includes RevAI, Soniox, Gladia, and Speechmatics partial-to-final revision collapse fixtures. Live transport extraction and fake WebSocket server tests remain separate follow-up work.

The runner replays raw provider JSON frames through existing server-message handlers, drains serialized provider events, and asserts ordered output without forcing these event-style providers into the normalized AsrSpanRevisionPayload ledger harness prematurely. Deepgram fixtures cover Results transcript+SpeechFinal turn, SpeechStarted, UtteranceEnd, TurnInfo TurnResumed, metadata/unknown no-event frames, and invalid JSON errors. AssemblyAI fixtures cover partial/final transcript, SessionBegins no-event, SessionTerminated, server error, unknown no-event, and invalid JSON. OpenAI Realtime fixtures cover session readiness, delta accumulation, completed final replacement, failed-frame error/accumulator clearing, top-level error, ignored control/unknown frames, and invalid JSON.

title: CredentialBackend facade and YAML adapter
description: Implement the ADR-0019 first execution slice. Add a backend-owned CredentialBackend facade plus a YamlCredentialBackend wrapper around the existing credentials.yaml behavior before changing storage. Scope: move save/delete/presence/diagnose, settings hydration, provider readiness/model discovery, and AWS credential refresh off direct credentials.yaml access and onto the facade. Acceptance: no behavior regression for existing credentials.yaml users; no direct credentials.yaml reads outside the YAML backend, migration fixtures, or docs; mock/in-memory/temp-file backend coverage exists for commands/settings/AWS refresh; plaintext secrets still never cross normal IPC or logs.

credential_backend_facade_slice_2026_06_.summary: Added a backend-owned CredentialBackend trait plus YamlCredentialBackend adapter while preserving existing credentials.yaml behavior. Existing public load/save/set/delete wrappers now delegate to the backend. CredentialStore now owns get/get_owned/is_present/present_count helpers so command presence/loadback/diagnostics no longer duplicate key-to-field mapping. AWS YamlRefreshingCredentialsProvider now loads through the YAML backend rather than duplicating read_to_string plus serde_yaml parsing.

title: OS keychain credential backend and non-destructive import
description: Implement the ADR-0019 production credential backend after the CredentialBackend facade exists. Scope: add OS-native secret storage for macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, and Linux Secret Service using keyring/keyring-core as appropriate; keep explicit headless/dev fallback; import existing credentials.yaml keys idempotently; expose non-secret source labels such as os_keychain, file_fallback, imported_file, missing, and error. Acceptance: saved-key health/model discovery works without key re-entry; malformed/unavailable keychain states have non-secret diagnostics; migration never deletes credentials.yaml automatically in the first wave; tests cover Windows/macOS/Linux/headless behavior through mocked or gated backends.

keychain_backend_local_slice_2026_06_25.summary: Added the ADR-0019 OS-keychain credential backend slice using keyring v1 Entry for native macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, and Linux Secret Service selection, with explicit YAML/file fallback for tests and headless recovery. Added non-secret credentials-state.yaml migration tracking so imported/keychain-owned/deleted keys are filtered from automatic YAML fallback and stale credentials.yaml values cannot resurrect deleted credentials. YamlCredentialBackend set/delete now parse first and preserve malformed files instead of overwriting recoverable secrets.

title: Formalize processed PCM contract across rsac, ASR, playback, and projections
description: Follow-up from the Rust ecosystem review. The repo already has rsac capture and ProcessedAudioChunk-style backend timing, but provider expansion, diarization, playback, and projections need one explicit PCM/timing contract. Scope: document and test f32 mono 16 kHz pipeline invariants, source identity, timestamp basis, chunk duration, conversion to i16 LE/provider formats, playback resampling boundaries, and provider registry accepted audio formats. Acceptance: contract doc plus fixtures prove rsac input through ASR/provider/playback conversions; provider registry declares accepted PCM formats; tests guard timestamp/source invariants and conversion behavior; no provider invents an ad hoc PCM contract.

processed_pcm_contract_slice_2026_06_25.summary: Formalized the backend processed-audio bus as finite mono f32 at 16 kHz with 512-frame full chunks, shorter final flush chunks, stable source_id, and per-source timestamps that now advance by emitted frame duration instead of repeating the first accumulation timestamp. Added a shared f32->PCM16LE conversion helper and routed AWS, Deepgram, AssemblyAI, and OpenAI Realtime adapter wrappers through it. Provider registry audio descriptors now expose public canonical pipeline-format constants and test every audio-capable provider against the f32/16k/mono contract and adapter_resamples truth. ADR-0020 documents raw rsac/device-native input, processed bus ownership, provider conversion, playback boundary, and projection timing provenance.
subagent_findings_integrated: rsac/raw capture is device-native interleaved f32 with optional/usually absent rsac timestamps; AudioGraph capture currently stamps chunks with receive-time elapsed values.
The pipeline timestamp bug was real: emitted chunks reused the first accumulation timestamp. It now advances per emitted processed frame duration and has direct/resampled regression tests.
rubato/audioadapter-buffers remain the correct in-repo resampling path for the processed bus; no new audio crate was introduced.
Playback remains a separate i16 TTS/output contract and is tracked by audio-graph-f53b rather than being conflated with ASR processed PCM.

title: ConfigCodec compatibility harness for YAML settings and credential redaction
description: Follow-up from credential/config storage work and Rust ecosystem review. Before migrating YAML parsers or layering config libraries, build a compatibility harness that treats config writeback/redaction as a contract. Scope: fixtures for current config.yaml, legacy settings.json migration, corrupt YAML recovery, unknown fields, defaults, redaction, credentials.yaml separation, and writeback preservation. Acceptance: harness compares current serde_yaml behavior against serde-saphyr or another candidate before migration; no plaintext credential enters config.yaml/logs/snapshots; parser errors preserve recoverable files; config provenance/writeback behavior is documented.

implementation_2026_06_25.summary: Added a ConfigCodec compatibility harness that keeps serde_yaml as the runtime/oracle codec and compares a dev-test serde-saphyr candidate on current settings fixtures, legacy JSON import, defaults, unknown-field tolerance, corrupt YAML rejection, unknown provider rejection, and redacted writeback interoperability.

research.serde_yaml: 0.9.34+deprecated remains the compatibility oracle until migration is explicitly gated.
research.serde_saphyr: 0.0.28 is the immediate parser candidate because it exposes Serde from_str/to_string APIs and parser options.
research.config_rs_figment: Useful for future layered reads/provenance, not a writeback-preserving user settings editor.

verification: cargo test -p audio-graph settings::tests::config_codec --lib --no-default-features --features cloud -- --nocapture (10 passed)

closeReason: Implemented ConfigCodec compatibility harness against serde-saphyr candidate, documented writeback contract, and verified targeted tests.

title: Decide codec/decode boundary for imported audio and provider Opus support
description: Follow-up from Rust ecosystem review. Realtime pipeline should stay linear PCM unless a provider or replay/import feature requires codecs. Scope: decide whether symphonia is needed for imported/replay audio fixtures and whether audiopus/opus is needed for provider transports. Acceptance: licensing review covers MPL/libopus implications; no codec dependency enters realtime capture/playback without a real provider or fixture use case; imported-audio fixture plan is documented if symphonia is adopted.
closeReason: merged c765309. ADR-0022 codec/decode boundary (symphonia at import edge, no codec in realtime path). docs/adr/0022 + README index.

title: Correct xAI streaming STT registry diarization capability caveat
description: Provider research found current xAI voice/STT docs claim word timestamps, multichannel, diarization, smart turn, and partial/done events while the generated registry currently does not advertise diarization. Scope: verify current xAI docs against registry descriptors and settings selectability. Acceptance: either supports_diarization is updated with required_not_wired/unselectable caveat, or the registry/docs include a clear reason AudioGraph will not advertise it yet; provider capability tests cover the chosen claim; no selectable runtime is implied without adapter/health support.

asr_harness_subagent_refinement_2026_06_.summary: Provider harness review reinforced that xAI diarization capability must be resolved before any stronger registry claim. Keep xAI watch/planned metadata unselectable unless docs support the claim and the provider-specific parser fixture maps speaker/channel metadata into span or speaker-timeline revisions without treating provider speaker IDs as durable identities.
acceptance_additions: Verify current xAI streaming STT docs for partial/final, speaker, channel, and diarization semantics before changing supports_diarization.
If advertised, add fixture coverage for speaker/channel metadata and explicit caveat that provider speaker IDs are not durable person identities.
Keep xAI provider non-selectable until credential schema, runtime adapter, readiness behavior, and cross-platform evidence exist.

xai_diarization_registry_caveat_2026_06_.summary: Updated xAI Grok STT watch metadata after checking current primary xAI STT streaming docs. Registry now advertises supports_diarization=true and provider speaker attribution, with source URL/date metadata, while keeping xAI as watch/non-selectable with required_not_wired auth and no credential keys/runtime health/model commands. Multichannel remains false/source-native unclaimed because AudioGraph has no xAI runtime/channel adapter yet.
verified: git diff --check -- registry/generated/docs files and .seeds/issues.jsonl -> passed
remaining: xAI remains non-selectable until credential schema, saved-key readiness, runtime adapter, parser fixtures, and provider speaker-timeline mapping are implemented under separate provider-platform Seeds.

closeReason: Verified xAI watch metadata now advertises provider speaker diarization capability while preserving non-selectable required_not_wired runtime caveat.

title: Audit Express Setup credential readiness copy and actions
description: Focused remainder split from audio-graph-e634. Provider settings now use Add/Replace/Clear actions, but the Express Setup flow still needs a focused pass so it does not blur provider configuration with runnable credential readiness. Scope: review ExpressSetup copy/actions/tests for saved-key presence, missing credentials, runnable readiness, and plaintext-key handling. Acceptance: saved keys are shown as available without re-entry; missing credentials are framed as an action, not a blank field state; Express Setup never requests stored plaintext; tests cover saved OpenRouter/Deepgram/Gemini/AWS presence and provider-configured-but-not-runnable messaging.

express_setup_credential_copy_2026_06_25.summary: Audited Express Setup credential readiness flow and added explicit missing-key and saved-key reuse hints. Missing credentials are now framed as the action that makes the selected provider path runnable. Saved keys are shown as backend-reused without re-entry and without loading plaintext into the form. Existing saved-key tests cover OpenRouter, Deepgram, and Gemini; AWS remains outside the Express Setup quickstart provider set and stays handled by Settings credential readiness.

notes: No Express Setup code path calls load_credential_cmd; tests assert no secret readback for saved-key flows
AWS quickstart is not represented in Express Setup choices, so AWS saved presence remains covered by full Settings credential controls

title: Track settings load provenance to prevent corrupt config overwrite
description: Follow-up from ConfigCodec audit. load_settings_from_paths preserves a corrupt canonical config.yaml by returning defaults, but later startup demo-mode or logging load-patch-save paths can persist those defaults over the recoverable corrupt file. Acceptance: settings load exposes provenance/status for canonical_ok, canonical_error_defaulted, legacy_imported, and defaults_missing; save paths refuse or require explicit recovery when the current in-memory settings came from a parse-error fallback; tests prove corrupt config.yaml is not overwritten by startup demo-mode or set_logging_config flows; logs/errors remain sanitized.

implementation_2026_06_25.summary: Added settings load provenance/status and guarded automatic plus normal settings writeback when an existing canonical config.yaml cannot be parsed. Startup credential migration/demo-mode saves, load_settings_cmd inline credential migration, set_logging_config load-patch-save, and save_settings/save_settings_locked now avoid silently replacing recoverable corrupt config files. Parse/load warnings and save refusal messages avoid echoing parser diagnostics.

title: Add YAML migration edge-case fixtures for ConfigCodec
description: Extend the ConfigCodec compatibility matrix beyond current happy/corrupt/unknown-field fixtures. Acceptance: fixture tests cover duplicate keys, YAML 1.1 boolean spellings, nulls, anchors and merge keys, comments/style rewrite expectations, tags, multi-document YAML handling, and alias/budget-heavy input; serde_yaml remains the compatibility oracle until a migration decision; behavior deltas versus serde-saphyr are documented without leaking secrets.

yaml_edge_fixture_resolution_2026_06_26.summary: Extended ConfigCodec fixtures and tests for duplicate keys, YAML 1.1 boolean spellings, nulls, anchors and merge keys, comment/style writeback normalization, explicit tags, multi-document YAML, and alias-heavy unknown input. serde_yaml remains the compatibility oracle; serde-saphyr matches accepted fixtures and documented rejections except the intentional YAML 1.1 boolean delta, which is now covered by an explicit test.

closeReason: Implemented YAML edge-case fixture matrix for ConfigCodec with serde_yaml oracle behavior, serde-saphyr delta documentation, and green Rust gates.

title: Generate public non-secret settings schema
description: Use the existing schemars dependency to make the settings contract inspectable without exposing runtime-only credential fields. Acceptance: generate or test a public AppSettings schema that omits api_key/access_key runtime fields and other credential-bearing fields; fixture or unit test proves secret fields are absent; docs explain the schema as non-secret settings only; optional JSON-schema validation is dev-only and redacted.

public_settings_schema_resolution_2026_0.summary: Added schemars JsonSchema derives for AppSettings and nested settings enums/structs, explicit #[schemars(skip)] on runtime credential material, and public_app_settings_schema_json() for the inspectable non-secret config/IPC contract. Unit tests prove api/access/secret/session/service-account credential fields are absent while non-secret provider/settings fields remain present. Docs clarify the schema is not the credentials schema.

closeReason: Implemented public non-secret AppSettings JSON Schema with schemars derives, explicit credential-field schema skips, unit coverage, docs, and green Rust gates.

title: Evaluate secrecy wrappers for runtime provider credentials
description: Follow-up from Rust config/credential research. Current CredentialStore uses ZeroizeOnDrop and redacted Debug, but runtime provider structs still carry plain Strings. Acceptance: evaluate secrecy::SecretString or SecretBox for backend-only provider credentials; document how it interacts with keyring/YAML fallback, serde serialization, Debug redaction, IPC presence flows, and zeroization; implement only if it does not break config redaction or provider tests.

runtime_secret_wrappers_subagent_audit_2.summary: Runtime credential security review should include both SecretString/SecretBox evaluation and provider error-body redaction. A dedicated follow-up Seed audio-graph-ad98 now tracks redacting HTTP/WebSocket excerpts before UI/log surfacing.
acceptance_additions: Evaluate secrecy wrappers for runtime provider structs without breaking serde/config redaction or backend-only health checks.
Ensure provider error strings cannot carry API keys, bearer tokens, AWS credentials, or signed URLs into React/logs.
Document how wrappers interact with keyring/YAML fallback, Debug, zeroization, and IPC presence flows.

runtime_secret_wrapper_resolution_2026_0.summary: Read-only subagent review found secrecy/SecretString is not a drop-in win now. The repo already uses zeroize on CredentialStore, redacted Debug, redacted settings serialization, non-secret IPC presence/readiness, and centralized provider diagnostic redaction. Provider clients still need plaintext &str/String for reqwest/tungstenite/Gemini/AWS SDK calls, so a wrapper swap would add dependency and serde/type churn without reducing the current runtime clone graph. Prefer a future internal runtime credential layer if credential ownership is redesigned.

closeReason: Evaluated secrecy wrappers with read-only subagent review; documented defer decision because current zeroize/redaction/IPC controls are stronger than a dependency/type churn wrapper swap until a runtime credential layer is redesigned.

title: Classify provider file paths and multi-field credentials in settings
description: Follow-up from ConfigCodec/credential audit. Gemini VertexAI service_account_path is serialized in config.yaml while Debug treats it as sensitive and CredentialStore has a google_service_account_path slot; AWS AccessKeys only models access_key in settings, so older secret_key/session_token style fields would be ignored or dropped on writeback. Acceptance: decide which provider paths are secrets versus non-secret routing; move secret-like paths or multi-field credential material to the credential backend or document why not; add tests proving config.yaml writeback does not persist classified secrets and legacy inline multi-field credentials are not silently lost.

credential_classification_subagent_audit.summary: Credential audit highlighted Gemini VertexAI service_account_path as serialized settings while Debug treats it as sensitive and the credential store has google_service_account_path. Also keep AWS legacy secret/session-token import/writeback semantics in scope.
acceptance_additions: Classify Gemini VertexAI service_account_path as secret-like credential material or document why it is non-secret routing.
Move secret-like provider file paths to the credential backend or prove config writeback redacts/preserves them safely.
Add tests for legacy AWS access_key/secret_key/session_token import and config writeback so multi-field credentials are not silently dropped.

credential_classification_resolution_202.summary: Classified Gemini Vertex AI service_account_path and AWS access/secret/session token material as credential backend data rather than config.yaml data. Backend now imports legacy inline fields, skips serializing them, redacts them from IPC/config copies, and hydrates runtime-only settings from CredentialStore. Settings UI now saves google_service_account_path through credential IPC and saves AWS secret/session before settings persistence so runtime readiness sees coherent credentials.

closeReason: Implemented credential classification/import/writeback: Vertex service_account_path and AWS access/secret/session are credential-backend material, redacted from config/IPC, hydrated at runtime, and covered by focused Rust/frontend tests.

title: First-class Cerebras OpenAI-compatible LLM provider readiness
description:
Cerebras is not currently a first-class AudioGraph LLM provider. Local code exposes LocalLlama, generic OpenAI-compatible Api, OpenRouter, AwsBedrock, and MistralRs, so users can likely point the generic Api provider at https://api.cerebras.ai/v1, but Settings cannot show Cerebras-specific provider metadata, saved-key health/model discovery, defaults, or recovery guidance.

Scope:
- Add a Cerebras LLM provider descriptor or provider-registry metadata path for the OpenAI-compatible Cerebras endpoint.
- Decide whether this should be a new LlmProvider enum variant or a typed preset over the generic Api provider. Prefer the least invasive path that still enables saved-key readiness/model catalog and clear Settings UX.
- Store any Cerebras key only in credentials.yaml / local credential backend, never config.yaml, logs, screenshots, docs, or Seeds.
- Add backend-owned health/model discovery if Cerebras exposes a safe endpoint; otherwise mark the provider as saved-key present with explicit no-probe/catalog limitations.
- Add Settings tests proving saved credential presence enables model/provider controls without plaintext key readback.

Acceptance:
- Cerebras appears as a clear LLM provider/preset with endpoint/default model metadata.
- Saved credentials are detected and can drive health/model readiness without re-entering the key.
- Generic Api behavior remains available for arbitrary OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Cross-platform Tauri/Settings tests pass without secret leakage.

References checked 2026-06-25:
- Cerebras OpenAI compatibility docs: https://inference-docs.cerebras.ai/resources/openai
- Current code: src-tauri/src/settings/mod.rs LlmProvider and src-tauri/src/provider_registry.rs LLM provider descriptors

work_started_2026_06_26.summary: Starting first-class Cerebras OpenAI-compatible LLM provider readiness. Scope for this pass: verify current Cerebras endpoint/model/auth docs from primary sources, inspect existing generic API/OpenRouter/LLM registry paths, then implement the least-invasive first-class preset or provider descriptor that preserves generic API behavior and saved-key/no-plaintext rules.

blocking: Generic llm.api readiness falls back to every OpenAI-compatible credential key instead of resolving the active endpoint credential key, so arbitrary generic LLM endpoints can look missing unless all OpenAI-compatible keys are present.
nonblocking: Backend base readiness does not expose fixed fallback catalogs for remote-command descriptors such as llm.cerebras before remote probe success.
Provider setup card model display falls back to descriptor default for llm.cerebras instead of explicitly preserving a custom selected Cerebras model.
acceptance_before_close: Add dynamic llm.api credential routing that mirrors credential_key_for_endpoint(endpoint).
Verify generic Api remains available for arbitrary OpenAI-compatible endpoints while Cerebras uses cerebras_api_key.

cerebras_provider_readiness_closeout_202.summary: Implemented Cerebras as a first-class Settings/provider-registry preset over the existing OpenAI-compatible LlmProvider::Api shape. Cerebras now has a dedicated cerebras_api_key slot, canonical endpoint routing, generated llm.cerebras descriptor metadata, saved-key readiness and model discovery through the backend /models probe, Settings UI controls, provider setup integration, i18n, and no plaintext saved-key loadback. Reviewer-discovered generic llm.api readiness regression was fixed by routing required credentials from the active endpoint and allowing loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints to be unauthenticated.
review_findings_resolved: Generic llm.api readiness no longer falls back to every OpenAI-compatible credential key.
Cerebras fixed fallback model catalog is available before remote probe success.
Provider setup mode cards preserve a selected Cerebras model.
