title: Competitive product roadmap: overtake Granola and Cluely
description:
Later-roadmap umbrella for making AudioGraph stronger than Granola-style AI notetaking and Cluely-style live assistance after the core cross-platform capture, credentials, transcript, notes, graph, diarization, and provider platform are stable.

Competitive target checked 2026-06-25: Granola emphasizes bot-free computer-audio capture, calendar briefs, searchable memory, sharing, and privacy/security; Cluely emphasizes live real-time answers, meeting notes, modes, transcript/live-insight cards, and desktop/mobile availability.

Goal: define product and architecture work that compounds on AudioGraph's durable transcript/notes/temporal-graph foundation instead of copying competitors superficially. This epic should stay P2/later-roadmap until the P1 usable cross-platform cutline is green.

Acceptance:
- Child Seeds cover pre-meeting, in-meeting, post-meeting, trust/privacy, collaboration, integrations, and benchmark loops.
- Every feature is compatible with local-first/cross-platform architecture and saved credential rules.
- Competitive work is measured by repeatable quality/latency/usability benchmarks, not only UI claims.

title: Competitive benchmark suite for notes, memory, and live assist quality
description:
Create a repeatable evaluation harness that compares AudioGraph against Granola, Cluely, Otter, Fireflies, Fathom, and platform-native meeting assistants.

Scope:
- Define transcript accuracy, speaker attribution, note usefulness, action-item precision/recall, decision capture, hallucination rate, live-assist latency, time-to-first-note, and sharing/privacy criteria.
- Use fixture meetings across sales, product research, engineering standup, recruiting, support, and in-person conversations.
- Score AudioGraph generated notes/graph/live cards against human rubrics and competitor outputs where lawful and practical.
- Record benchmark runs as non-secret artifacts; no private meeting content in Seeds.

Acceptance:
- A documented benchmark plan exists with datasets/fixtures, metrics, scoring, and run cadence.
- The benchmark can gate later roadmap work and expose regressions before release.
- Results identify which competitor capability each AudioGraph feature is trying to beat.

title: Calendar and prior-context pre-briefs from the temporal graph
description:
Build Granola-class pre-meeting briefs, but backed by AudioGraph's temporal graph and local/private configuration.

Scope:
- Calendar connector architecture for local calendar and optional cloud calendars.
- Match attendees, companies, recurring meetings, prior sessions, unresolved decisions, commitments, and open questions from the graph.
- Generate a brief before the meeting without exposing secrets or private context to unapproved providers.
- UI affordance for reviewing/editing the brief before capture starts.

Acceptance:
- Users can open an upcoming meeting and see a concise brief: who is attending, what happened last time, what is unresolved, and suggested questions.
- Brief generation uses saved provider credentials and honors local-only/BYOK modes.
- Missing calendar access degrades to manual meeting context without breaking capture.
closeReason: Duplicate created during competitive roadmap seed creation; canonical linked Seed is audio-graph-53cf.

title: Consent-aware live assist overlay and dynamic action cards
description:
Build the ethical/productive version of Cluely-style live assistance: real-time suggestions, answers, and follow-up prompts, with explicit capture controls and mode-aware behavior.

Scope:
- Live action cards from transcript/graph deltas: follow-up question, clarify risk, summarize thread, answer from memory, fact-check against known context, draft response.
- Per-session modes such as sales, research, recruiting, support, standup, and general notes.
- Latency budget and cancellation model compatible with the streaming notes/graph projection queue.
- UI that is visible and controllable to the user, with clear audio/capture status.

Acceptance:
- In a live session, AudioGraph emits useful assist cards without waiting for the full meeting to end.
- Cards cite transcript spans or graph context when possible.
- Users can pause, hide, pin, dismiss, and ask follow-up questions without corrupting transcript/graph state.
closeReason: Duplicate created during competitive roadmap seed creation; canonical linked Seed is audio-graph-392b.

title: Calendar and prior-context pre-briefs from the temporal graph
description:
Build Granola-class pre-meeting briefs, but backed by AudioGraph's temporal graph and local/private configuration.

Scope:
- Calendar connector architecture for local calendar and optional cloud calendars.
- Match attendees, companies, recurring meetings, prior sessions, unresolved decisions, commitments, and open questions from the graph.
- Generate a brief before the meeting without exposing secrets or private context to unapproved providers.
- UI affordance for reviewing/editing the brief before capture starts.

Acceptance:
- Users can open an upcoming meeting and see a concise brief: who is attending, what happened last time, what is unresolved, and suggested questions.
- Brief generation uses saved provider credentials and honors local-only/BYOK modes.
- Missing calendar access degrades to manual meeting context without breaking capture.

title: Live assistance agent triggers, question detection, and key-note capture
description:
Build the ethical/productive version of Cluely-style live assistance: real-time suggestions, answers, and follow-up prompts, with explicit capture controls and mode-aware behavior.

Scope:
- Live action cards from transcript/graph deltas: follow-up question, clarify risk, summarize thread, answer from memory, fact-check against known context, draft response.
- Per-session modes such as sales, research, recruiting, support, standup, and general notes.
- Latency budget and cancellation model compatible with the streaming notes/graph projection queue.
- UI that is visible and controllable to the user, with clear audio/capture status.

Acceptance:
- In a live session, AudioGraph emits useful assist cards without waiting for the full meeting to end.
- Cards cite transcript spans or graph context when possible.
- Users can pause, hide, pin, dismiss, and ask follow-up questions without corrupting transcript/graph state.

user_refinement_2026_06_25.summary: The live assistance path is the AudioGraph agent surface: it should trigger on detected questions, unresolved topics, and key-note candidates during the meeting, not just produce post-meeting notes. It should feed useful live cards and also write revisioned key-note suggestions into the notes/graph pipeline with provenance.

competitive_live_assist_ui_research_2026.summary: Cluely-style research supports keeping AudioGraph live assist visible, session-scoped, and controllable rather than hidden or mixed into diagnostics. The key UI pattern is a lightweight live-assist layer: session timer/end, audio pause, mode dropdown, transcript/insights toggle, default actions, Ask AI, dynamic suggested actions, move/hide. AudioGraph should adopt the controllability and citation model while explicitly avoiding invisible/stealth defaults.
acceptance_additions: Live-assist cards sit in the active meeting workspace, not only a top-bar popover.
Cards support pause/hide/pin/dismiss/ask and preserve transcript/graph provenance.
Default live actions include follow-up question, recap thread, fact-check against known context, answer from memory, and draft response.
Mode selection is visible during a session and persisted with card provenance.
Audio capture and assistant visibility are always explicit; no hidden screen/audio capture path is implied.

title: Architecture session: cross-session meeting memory workspace and recall UX
description:
Design the user-facing meeting memory workspace that makes the temporal graph valuable beyond a single session.

Scope:
- People, organizations, topics, commitments, decisions, risks, recurring themes, and unresolved questions as first-class navigable objects.
- Search and Q&A over notes, transcript spans, speaker timeline, and graph revisions.
- Confidence/provenance model so users can inspect why a memory exists and what updated it.
- Retcon behavior when new transcripts revise older notes or relationships.

Acceptance:
- Architecture doc describes persisted objects, indexes, provenance, privacy boundary, and UX navigation model.
- Design avoids append-only duplicate memories; graph changes are revisioned/diffed.
- Follow-up implementation Seeds are split by storage, indexing, backend query API, and frontend workspace views.

competitive_memory_workspace_research_20.summary: Granola, Otter, MeetGeek, and Notta all make cross-meeting memory/search a post-session workspace, but most do it as document/search lists rather than a provenance-first temporal graph. AudioGraph should turn the graph into navigable memory objects after the meeting: people, orgs, topics, decisions, commitments, risks, unresolved questions, and revisions with source spans.
acceptance_additions: After Meeting workspace has a memory/search view separate from the live capture shell.
Every memory object exposes transcript span, note, graph revision, confidence, and update history.
Cross-meeting Q&A cites meetings and spans, not just generated summaries.
Graph visualizations explain and filter memory; they are not the only navigation model.

title: Privacy-first sharing, redaction, ACLs, and export links
description:
Make sharing a strength instead of a liability. AudioGraph should beat meeting-note tools by making privacy state explicit and reversible.

Scope:
- Local redaction workflow for transcript spans, speaker names, entities, sensitive topics, and generated notes.
- Explicit ACL/sharing model for note links, exports, and team workspaces.
- Share previews that show exactly what recipients can see.
- Expiring links, revoke, copy-safe exports, and audit trail.

Acceptance:
- Users cannot accidentally share transcript quotes, graph facts, or hidden speaker data through note links.
- Shared artifacts preserve provenance where allowed and omit private data where redacted.
- Tests cover redaction, revocation, export, and link-permission edge cases.

priority_refinement_2026_06_25.summary: User clarified sharing/privacy product work can be later. Keep trust boundaries in architecture, but detailed sharing/redaction/ACL feature work is no longer a P2 competitive cutline.

competitive_privacy_default_research_202.summary: Competitor privacy research reinforces the original direction: AudioGraph should compete on explicit, reversible privacy defaults. Public/unlisted note links and opt-out training defaults are a known trust risk in AI notetaker products. Even if sharing/redaction remains later than core usability, the UX model should preserve local-first defaults and make data routes/sharing previews non-secret and inspectable.
acceptance_additions: Default share state remains private/off until explicit user action.
Share/export preview must show exactly which transcript spans, notes, graph facts, speakers, and metadata leave the device.
Training/model-use status is visible per provider/workspace where applicable.
Existing local-first single-user mode stays viable without cloud sharing.

title: Action item lifecycle and integration sync
description:
Move beyond summaries by turning meeting outputs into tracked work.

Scope:
- Extract action items with owner, due date, source transcript span, confidence, and status.
- Let users accept/edit/reject suggested actions before syncing externally.
- Integrations architecture for Linear, Jira, GitHub Issues, Slack, Notion, Google Docs, HubSpot/Salesforce, and generic webhooks.
- Conflict/update model when action items are corrected by later transcript/graph revisions.

Acceptance:
- Post-meeting action review exists as a first-class workflow.
- Synced external tasks retain source provenance and do not leak redacted data.
- Integrations are provider/credential-gated and do not require hardcoded platform assumptions.

priority_refinement_2026_06_25.summary: User clarified integrations can be later. Keep action extraction in mind for live agent/post-meeting workflows, but external sync integrations should not compete with live memory/agent architecture for the next roadmap cutline.

competitive_action_review_research_2026_.summary: Fathom/Otter/Fireflies/MeetGeek consistently sell the post-call handoff: summaries, decisions, action items, follow-up emails, CRM/project updates. Keep external integrations deferred per prior user refinement, but the post-meeting UI should still include an action-review queue with owners, due dates, confidence, and source spans before any sync work.
acceptance_additions: After Meeting view has a first-class actions/decisions review queue.
Suggested actions are editable/accept/reject with transcript-span provenance before external sync.
External integrations stay provider/credential-gated and can remain later than local action lifecycle.

title: Architecture session: trust and compliance model for local-first AudioGraph
description:
Define the trust architecture needed to compete with enterprise-grade meeting assistants while preserving AudioGraph's local-first advantage.

Scope:
- Data retention policy for audio, transcript, notes, embeddings, graph nodes, provider logs, and exports.
- BYOK/provider-routing policy, local-only mode, cloud-disabled mode, and secret redaction guarantees.
- Audit log model for capture start/stop, provider calls, sharing, exports, deletion, and credential changes.
- SOC 2/GDPR readiness gap analysis without claiming certification prematurely.

Acceptance:
- Architecture session produces a concrete trust model and implementation Seed list.
- UI can eventually explain where audio/text went for each session.
- Deletion/export behavior is testable and does not depend on chat-only policy claims.

description:
Compete with polished notetakers by making the first successful note fast on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

Scope:

title: Domain mode packs and customizable meeting playbooks
description:
Add configurable meeting modes that shape notes, live assist cards, and post-meeting outputs by use case.

Scope:
- Mode packs for sales, product research, recruiting, engineering standups, customer support, investor calls, and generic meetings.
- User-editable prompts/templates with versioning and test fixtures.
- Per-mode extraction schemas for decisions, objections, feature requests, bugs, commitments, risks, and follow-up questions.

Acceptance:
- Users can select a mode before/during a meeting and see mode-specific notes/live cards/actions.
- Mode changes are persisted with the session and included in provenance.
- Template changes do not break the core transcript/graph schema.

competitive_mode_template_research_2026_.summary: Competitor products increasingly shape notes by meeting type or workflow: sales/customer calls, recruiting, research, standups, education, and leadership reviews. AudioGraph should use mode packs as IA simplification: mode selection changes visible live-assist defaults, note sections, post-meeting action schema, and memory object emphasis instead of exposing every provider/graph capability upfront.
acceptance_additions: Mode picker is present before/during session and survives into provenance.
Mode drives live actions and note sections; users should not manually assemble graph/task/question settings per meeting.
Generic meeting mode remains the default; domain modes are optional presets with editable prompts later.

title: Architecture session: team workspace and shared graph governance
description:
Design multi-user collaboration for shared meeting memory without turning every personal note into organization data.

Scope:
- Personal vs team workspace boundaries.
- Shared graph merge/conflict model across users and meetings.
- Role-based access, shared tags/collections, team search, and admin policy.
- Migration path from local single-user storage to optional team sync.

Acceptance:
- Architecture doc defines workspace boundaries, sync model, permissions, and conflict handling.
- Follow-up Seeds separate storage/sync/backend/frontend/security work.
- The design preserves single-user local-first operation.

title: Architecture session: mobile and in-person capture companion
description:
Explore mobile/in-person capture so AudioGraph can cover hallway, phone, and off-laptop conversations later.

Scope:
- iOS/Android companion capture options, watch/shortcut triggers, upload/sync protocol, and offline behavior.
- In-person diarization implications and consent prompts.
- How mobile captures become regular AudioGraph sessions with transcript, notes, speaker timeline, and graph projection.

Acceptance:
- Architecture session recommends build/buy/defer for mobile companion work.
- Cross-platform desktop remains the primary MVP; mobile work is staged after core usability.
- Privacy and consent requirements are explicit before implementation.

title: Architecture session: screen-context assist with explicit capture controls
description:
Evaluate screen-context assistance as a later Cluely-class capability while keeping capture explicit, auditable, and cross-platform.

Scope:
- OS APIs for screen/window context on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
- Permission model, redaction boundaries, and opt-in session state.
- Fusion of screen context with transcript/graph for live assist cards and answers.
- Risks around screen sharing, sensitive data, and non-meeting workflows.

Acceptance:
- Architecture doc defines whether/when to implement screen context and which OS APIs are acceptable.
- No hidden screen capture path is added without explicit product/security approval.
- Follow-up implementation Seeds include cross-platform validation and privacy tests.

title: Architecture spike: SurrealDB 3.x embedded memory and federated org knowledge
description:
Evaluate SurrealDB 3.x as the local and optional federated knowledge substrate for AudioGraph session memory, notes accumulation, and promotable organization knowledge.

Context from user direction 2026-06-25: look at SurrealDB 3.x Rust embedding for session memory and notes accumulation, and plan a cloud sync or federated org knowledge system where users can promote their local notes/memories into an organization knowledge space.

Initial source checked 2026-06-25:
- SurrealDB Rust embedding docs: https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/rust/embedding
- Docs show Rust embedded operation can use in-memory storage, file-backed RocksDB, or distributed deployment; this maps well to local-first desktop plus optional cloud/org sync if the architecture is sound.

Scope:
- Evaluate SurrealDB 3.x embedded Rust features for local session store: transcript revisions, notes accumulation, temporal graph nodes/edges, speaker timeline, agent memory, and provenance.
- Design tables/records/relations for session_memory, note_revision, key_note, question_trigger, agent_card, graph_fact, graph_edge, person, organization, commitment, decision, and promotion events.
- Assess local file-backed engine behavior on macOS, Windows, and Linux, including packaging, migrations, backups, compaction, and failure recovery.
- Design optional cloud/federated org knowledge: user-owned local copy, explicit promotion to team/org space, conflict resolution, provenance, ACL hooks, deletion/retention boundaries, and offline-first sync semantics.
- Compare SurrealDB against the current/likely storage stack and simpler alternatives before committing the backend to a new database dependency.

Acceptance:
- Architecture note recommends adopt/spike/defer/no-go for SurrealDB with concrete reasons.
- Prototype plan includes a thin repository trait so transcript/notes/graph code is not hard-wired directly to SurrealDB APIs.
- Promotion model is explicit: local private notes remain private unless promoted, and promoted org facts preserve source/provenance/redaction state.
- Follow-up Seeds are filed for schema, migration, storage adapter, sync protocol, and UI surfaces only if the spike recommends adoption.

Keep current JSON/JSONL artifacts as baseline, preserve event-sourced transcript/projection contracts, and model org knowledge as explicit redacted promotion events rather than hidden sync of private sessions.

title: SurrealDB embedded local memory adapter spike
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-5dde. Add an opt-in SurrealDB adapter behind LocalMemoryRepository after the file adapter exists. Start with kv-mem for tests, then evaluate kv-surrealkv and kv-rocksdb file-backed engines for durability, build friction, binary size, and Linux/macOS/Windows packaging. Acceptance: adapter can append/load transcript span revisions and projection patches and save/load materialized notes/graph records; file adapter remains default; cross-platform CI evidence is recorded before any selectable/default storage switch.

surrealdb_kv_mem_adapter_wave_2026_06_26.summary: Added a non-default, feature-gated SurrealDB embedded LocalMemoryRepository adapter using kv-mem. The adapter stores transcript events, projection patches, materialized notes, materialized graph state, session metadata, live-assist cards, promotion/redaction/org-knowledge records, and sync state as typed JSON envelopes in schemaless SurrealDB tables. FileMemoryRepository remains the default runtime store; no Settings/runtime selectability was added. Current SurrealDB Rust docs support embedded Mem, SurrealKV, and RocksDB local engines with feature flags kv-mem, kv-surrealkv, and kv-rocksdb, and the Rust SDK docs require Rust 1.89+ with current 3.1.x compatibility. Local cargo resolved stable surrealdb 3.1.5, not the 3.2 beta. The feature adds a heavy compile/link surface, so it must remain opt-in until Blacksmith packaging evidence exists. Follow-up writer routing (audio-graph-f2b6), DB artifact/delete semantics (audio-graph-ff32), bounded writer queue policy (audio-graph-3a09), and UI queue-pressure surfacing (audio-graph-24dc) are now closed. Selectable/default storage remains blocked on file-backed engine packaging evidence.

description: Follow-up from audio-graph-48bb. LocalMemoryRepository currently exposes session_artifact_paths as Vec<PathBuf>, which maps naturally to JSON/JSONL file storage but not to database-backed records. Define how DB-backed repositories support session export, backup, deletion, diagnostics, and user-facing artifact inspection without pretending every artifact has a filesystem path. Acceptance: repository contract either separates file artifact paths from portable export bundles or introduces typed artifact descriptors; FileMemoryRepository behavior is preserved; DB-backed repositories have a tested export/delete story before selectability.

db_artifact_semantics_wave_2026_06_26.summary: Introduced typed SessionArtifactDescriptor and SessionArtifactStorage into LocalMemoryRepository so storage backends can expose file artifacts separately from repository records. session_artifact_paths is now the compatibility file-only view, while session_artifacts is the portable descriptor view. Added SessionArtifactDeleteReport and delete_session_artifacts: file repositories delete file descriptors and report deleted/missing/failed paths; SurrealDB repositories delete session-scoped records and return repository-record URIs without pretending DB records are filesystem paths. FileMemoryRepository behavior is preserved, and SurrealMemoryRepository has tested descriptor and deletion semantics before any storage selectability.

title: Evaluate SurrealDB file-backed engines on Blacksmith before storage selectability
description:
Follow-up from audio-graph-48bb. The kv-mem adapter proves the repository shape, but file-backed SurrealKV and RocksDB engines need durability, binary-size, native-dependency, and packaging evidence on Linux, macOS, and Windows before any selectable/default storage switch. Acceptance: compare kv-surrealkv and kv-rocksdb features, build/link time, binary size, native dependencies, corruption/backup behavior, and Tauri packaging behavior on Blacksmith runners; document recommendation and keep FileMemoryRepository default unless all platform evidence is green.

--- EVIDENCE UPDATE 2026-06-27 (backlog-zero wave) ---
LOCAL CROSS-PLATFORM PROBE (docs/reviews/2b2c-local-linux-evidence-2026-06-27.md). Throwaway isolated builds, NOT wired into product:

LINUX (native): kv-surrealkv compiles+links, +1.17 MiB stripped, native cross-process durability PASS. kv-rocksdb compiles+links, +6.68 MiB, vendored C++ RocksDB 11.0 (cc+cmake+bindgen + bzip2/zstd/lz4-sys). Neither adds a NEW native-toolchain class beyond the cloud baseline. SurrealKV wins Linux: 2.4x faster build, ~1/6 size, one C dep vs a C++ tree.

WINDOWS (cross-compile cargo-xwin/clang-cl, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc): BOTH engines cross-compile AND link clean. ADR-0021's top feared failure mode (RocksDB C++ + bindgen/libclang under MSVC) did NOT occur — clang-cl built the 337-file RocksDB tree into a 56 MB rocksdb.lib; bindgen parsed RocksDB headers vs the xwin MSVC SDK. BACKFLOW SIGNAL: softens the ADR's RocksDB-Windows risk. Native-Windows CI prereqs: NASM + rustc>=1.94.

CAVEATS: compile/link + Linux-only durability. NOT tested: native Windows run, RocksDB durability anywhere, NTFS LOCK/WAL, Windows PE size. macOS leg UNGATHERED (Apple SDK license -> needs Blacksmith macos-15; user approved).

GATING UNKNOWNS before advancing 48bb: macOS native leg + native-run durability for both engines on all 3 OSes. Keep FileMemoryRepository default per ADR-0021.

--- THROUGHPUT BENCHMARK 2026-06-27 (docs/reviews/2b2c-throughput-benchmark-2026-06-27.md) ---
Linux in-process append bench (N=10k transcript events): file O(1)=3.26ms/append; SurrealKV as-is O(n) full-table-scan=66.78ms (20.5x slower, p99 ~40x, O(n^2) growth) CONFIRMS the ADR hot-path fear; SurrealKV keyed (in-mem max-seq = the Wave-3 rewrite)=3.53ms = file PARITY, flat with N. Verdict: regression is an ADAPTER-ALGORITHM defect (surreal.rs:153/167), NOT a SurrealKV-engine limit -> lean-in viable AFTER the keyed rewrite (Wave 3, gated behind 3-OS build evidence; macOS leg still pending). Last locally-gatherable evidence; remaining gate = macOS native build + run.

--- CI EVIDENCE 2026-06-27 (Blacksmith run 28302904922, workflow_dispatch) ---
MACOS GATE CLEARED: 2b2c storage-engine probe PASSED on macOS (7m26s) AND Linux (7m40s). Both kv-surrealkv + kv-rocksdb BUILD+LINK natively on macOS-15 (arm64). Probe binary sizes (durability-probe, release): surrealkv 57.1MB, rocksdb 65.5MB (~8MB RocksDB delta, consistent with Linux/Windows). Durability probe ran (best-effort kill/restart). Windows probe FAILED — environment-only (Git /usr/bin/link.exe shadows MSVC link.exe under shell:bash; fix in flight, not an engine issue; the earlier cargo-xwin cross-compile already proved Windows links clean). NET: SurrealKV now GREEN on Linux+macOS native build/link + Windows cross-compile; only native-Windows build pending the CI shell fix. Per ADR-0021 decision rule (SurrealKV green on all 3 OS), this is nearly satisfied — once native-Windows passes, the gate to advance 48bb (keyed rewrite -> selectable-not-default) is met. Throughput already showed keyed rewrite = file parity. RocksDB carried but +8MB and not preferred.

--- 3-OS GATE FULLY CLEARED 2026-06-27 (Blacksmith run 28303714034, ALL 33 jobs green) ---
2b2c storage-engine probe PASSES on ALL THREE native OSes: linux(1m8s) + macos(30s) + windows(11m20s). SurrealKV + RocksDB build+link natively on Linux/macOS/Windows (Windows fixed by resolving the Git-link.exe-shadows-MSVC shell issue). Combined with the throughput benchmark (keyed rewrite = file parity), ADR-0021 decision rule "SurrealKV green on all 3 OS" is now SATISFIED. The gate to advance audio-graph-48bb (keyed/indexed rewrite -> selectable-not-default) is MET. Remaining before any DEFAULT cutover (per ADR-0021): the Wave-3 keyed rewrite itself + a demand signal; FileMemoryRepository stays default until then. RocksDB carried but +8MB and not preferred vs SurrealKV.
closeReason: Seed body records CI run 28303714034: '2b2c storage-engine probe PASSES on ALL THREE native OSes: linux(1m8s)+macos(30s)+windows(11m20s)' — kv-surrealkv + kv-rocksdb build+link natively on all 3. ADR-0021 decision rule 'SurrealKV green on all 3 OS' SATISFIED; eval acceptance (compare/build/link/size on 3 OS + keep FileMemoryRepository default) met. Keyed-rewrite cutover is downstream seed 48bb, not 2b2c.

title: Define bounded event-writer queues and repository backpressure policy
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-f2b6 review. TranscriptEventWriter and ProjectionEventWriter now support repository-backed sinks behind the same writer-thread/shutdown contract as the file path, but both file and repository paths still use std::sync::mpsc::channel, which is unbounded. If live DB-backed storage becomes selectable, a slow repository adapter could grow memory rather than applying explicit backpressure, dropping, or surfacing a degraded state. Scope: define queue limits, try_send or sync_channel behavior, metrics, UI diagnostics, and provider/session shutdown semantics for transcript/projection event writers. Acceptance: chosen queue policy is documented; tests cover slow sink behavior, full queue behavior, shutdown drain/drop semantics, and no secret/log leakage; FileMemoryRepository disk-full diagnostics remain file-specific; repository adapter failures surface as non-secret storage degraded diagnostics.

bounded_writer_queue_wave_2026_06_26.summary: Implemented a bounded non-blocking event-writer queue policy for TranscriptEventWriter and ProjectionEventWriter. Both file-backed and repository-backed event writers now use sync_channel with EVENT_WRITER_QUEUE_CAPACITY=2048, append via try_send, drop new events on full queues, track dropped counts, and emit a redacted persistence-queue-backpressure event on pressure transitions. Shutdown now sets the atomic flag and best-effort enqueues a sentinel with try_send so it cannot block behind a full queue. Projection runtime now enqueues the projection patch before saving materialized notes/graph, so queue-full rejection does not save materialized state without a replayable event. ASR span revision recording now validates on a cloned ledger, enqueues if a writer exists, then commits the ledger only after enqueue succeeds; queue-full rejection does not advance the live ledger. Disconnected enqueue logs intentionally discard the returned message payload to avoid transcript/note text leakage.

AssemblyAI and OpenAI Realtime session_task reconnect failure paths appear to continue back through run_io with stale socket halves after open_ws fails, unlike the Deepgram inline reconnect ladder that avoids double-counting attempts and stale run_io detours. title: Guard Deepgram disconnected lifecycle event deduplication

Deepgram can emit duplicate Disconnected lifecycle events because disconnect() sends Disconnected directly and session_task also sends Disconnected on user-requested exit. OpenAI Realtime already uses a one-shot disconnected guard pattern. Acceptance: Deepgram emits at most one Disconnected per user disconnect / clean stop path; reconnect and server-close paths still emit the expected lifecycle events; provider-local fake WebSocket tests cover the duplicate prevention; ASR cloud tests pass.

implementation_2026_06_26.summary: Deepgram now routes client disconnect(), clean session exits, user-disconnect races, and reconnect-backoff cancellation through a provider-local emit_disconnected_once guard. title: Prove ASR revision session and projection lifecycle
description: found-by: audio-graph-3709 parser/projection reviewer 2026-06-26. Frontend ASR revision replacement is span_id/revision based without a session dimension, and loadSession/loadSessionTranscript do not clear asrSpanRevisions/asrPartial. Notes/graph projection tests validate basis staleness with handcrafted patches but do not prove a transcript text retcon repairs/rejects derived notes or graph state. Acceptance: loadSession and loadSessionTranscript clear or session-scope ASR revision state; tests prove reused span ids/lower revisions in a newly loaded session are not suppressed by prior session history; add one projection replay fixture where a note or graph patch based on transcript rev1 is rejected/repaired after rev2; leave speaker-timeline retcon proof to audio-graph-eb6c/audio-graph-1fbd.

frontend_session_state_slice_2026_06_26.summary: loadSession and loadSessionTranscript now clear asrPartial/asrSpanRevisions so reused span ids or lower revisions from a newly loaded session are not suppressed by prior session history. Added store regression tests for full-session and transcript-only loads using the same span id at lower revision. Added a projection-history replay fixture where a rev1-derived note is accepted, a stale rev1 patch after transcript rev2 is rejected, and the rev2 repair updates the existing note instead of creating a duplicate. Speaker-timeline retcon proof remains scoped to audio-graph-eb6c/audio-graph-1fbd.

title: Sample session preview isolation and export boundaries
description: Found by sample-preview review. Loading the built-in sample session hydrates normal frontend transcript/notes/graph state without a preview-mode boundary, so later real capture/projection events can mix with fake sample data; JSON/TXT/graph exports can also target different backend/current-session data than the visible sample UI, and backend projection diagnostics stay visible beside sample state. Acceptance: sample preview has an explicit active flag/exit boundary; starting/loading/clearing real sessions or receiving real transcript/projection events exits or prevents preview mixing; sample exports are either disabled or clearly serialize visible sample data with a preview session id; backend-live diagnostics are hidden or sample-aware while preview is active; tests prove no backend persistence/session/capture/graph-write invokes are required for preview.

sample_preview_isolation_wave_2026_06_26.summary: Added explicit samplePreviewActive state, preview exit helpers, sample-aware transcript/graph exports with session_id and preview flag, stable preview getSessionId, and App hiding of backend ProjectionRuntimeStatusPanel while preview is active. Real transcript, ASR revision, diarization, turn, projection, materialized notes/graph, graph snapshot/delta, speaker, capture, transcription, Gemini, and loaded-session paths clear preview state before applying real data so fake sample data cannot mix with live session state.

title: Session data movement ledger and audit event schema
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-eeec. Add a backend-owned, session-scoped data movement ledger that answers where audio/text/provider data went. Scope: redacted audit events for capture start/stop, provider calls, artifact writes/loads/export/delete, credential save/delete/readiness, projection jobs/patches, and org promotion state. Acceptance: ledger persists redacted events without raw audio, raw transcript, prompt bodies, API keys, bearer tokens, service-account JSON, or full provider payloads; tests prove provider calls and artifact/delete/export events record data classes, provider/model ids, destination boundary, counts/hashes, status, and redacted errors.

title: Session data route UI and privacy report
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-eeec. Add an active and historical session data-route view that tells users which data stayed local and which data left the device. Scope: capture source, privacy mode, ASR/LLM/TTS/S2S provider ids and models, whether audio/transcript/notes/graph context left device, artifact descriptors, export/delete status, redacted provider errors, saved credential source/readiness summary, and org promotion/export history when present. Acceptance: loaded sessions can render a non-secret route report from backend data; local-only sessions clearly show no content transfer; cloud sessions show provider/model/data-class transfer without exposing secrets or raw hidden payloads.

title: Backend-enforced local-only and cloud-disabled runtime policy
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-eeec. Add a Rust-owned privacy/provider policy resolved at session start so UI state is not the enforcement boundary. Modes should include local_only, byok_cloud, cloud_disabled_readiness_only, and org_promotion. Acceptance: ASR, LLM/projection, TTS, native S2S, live-assist, embeddings/future memory, and org sync routes refuse session-content transfers when blocked; no-content saved-key readiness/model catalog probes can remain allowed; tests cover representative provider clients and verify blocked calls emit redacted policy events instead of sending audio/text/graph context.

runtime_policy_wave_started_2026_06_26.summary: Starting a bounded Template A implementation wave for backend-enforced local-only/cloud-disabled runtime policy. Scope for this wave: discover current settings/provider/session seams, add the smallest Rust-owned policy contract that can block representative session-content provider routes while preserving no-content readiness/model checks, and record any broader ASR/TTS/S2S/embedding/org-sync gaps as Seeds rather than pretending one slice closes the whole policy surface.
constraints: Do not touch CI/release workflows from the dirty checkout.
Do not read or log plaintext credentials beyond existing backend-owned credential paths.
Preserve saved-key readiness/model discovery when no session content is transferred.
Keep React as display/control; enforcement belongs in Rust.

runtime_policy_entrypoint_wave_2026_06_2.summary: Added a Rust-owned privacy_mode setting with modes local_only, byok_cloud, cloud_disabled_readiness_only, and org_promotion. Current session-content entry points now enforce policy before cloud provider content transfer: start_transcribe gates ASR audio and LLM projection/extraction, chat and notes gate transcript/graph/prompt transfer, streaming chat gates cloud TTS speak-aloud, and Gemini notes/native converse gate audio/model-response transfer before consumer registration/connect. LLM executor jobs now carry allow_cloud_fallbacks so restricted modes cannot silently fall back from local providers to OpenRouter/API cloud clients. Readiness/model discovery paths were left ungated for no-content saved-key checks. Frontend Settings and ExpressSetup preserve privacy_mode without exposing secrets or adding a visible control in this slice.

title: Provider-internal privacy guards for socket and request egress
description: Follow-up from audio-graph-d598. Command/session entry points now enforce runtime privacy mode, but provider clients should also refuse direct content egress when handed a blocked policy. Scope: add defense-in-depth policy checks or policy tokens inside ASR WebSocket/audio senders, LLM API/OpenRouter/Bedrock request builders, projection/extraction request paths, Deepgram Aura TTS, Gemini Live send_audio/run_io, and future embedding/org-sync clients. Acceptance: direct provider-client tests prove blocked modes cannot send audio/text/graph/prompt/generated-text payloads even if a command gate is bypassed; no-content readiness/model catalog probes remain allowed; errors/events stay redacted and never include keys, transcript text, prompt bodies, graph payloads, or provider raw payloads.

asr_provider_client_egress_guard_2026_06.summary: Added a shared ProviderContentEgressPolicy and threaded it from runtime PrivacyMode through SpeechConfig into Deepgram, AssemblyAI, Soniox, and OpenAI Realtime transcription ASR client configs. Direct provider-client tests prove the privacy error is redacted and does not include API keys, sample values, or transcript-like text.

title: OpenRouter accelerator routing and API-surface compatibility
description: Add first-class OpenRouter provider-routing controls and API-surface compatibility planning for fast accelerator-backed inference. Scope: expose safe model/provider routing preferences such as ordered provider slugs, fallback/data-policy/latency-or-throughput preferences where supported by OpenRouter; preserve chat/completions compatibility while deciding whether Responses and Messages should be typed provider surfaces or health/smoke-only probes; surface model catalog metadata without hardcoding one accelerator; and add tests that saved OpenRouter credentials can drive model discovery/routing controls without plaintext key readback. Acceptance: Settings can configure OpenRouter routing preferences for low-latency providers without free-form secret exposure, backend request builders serialize provider-routing fields safely, model catalog/readiness handles models served by accelerators such as Cerebras/Groq/SambaNova when OpenRouter exposes them, and docs/Seeds/tests contain no secret values. Live smoke evidence on 2026-06-26: OpenRouter /models, /chat/completions, /responses, and /messages returned HTTP 200 with a temporary key and nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b:free; candidate models nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b:free, qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-thinking, and microsoft/phi-4 were present in /models. Do not store temporary keys in repo or Seeds.

Add list_openrouter_providers_cmd for /providers and list_openrouter_model_endpoints_cmd(model) for /models/:author/:slug/endpoints using saved-key readiness.
Keep /chat/completions as production default; treat /responses as beta opt-in and /messages as Anthropic-compatible follow-up.
Add Settings presets: balanced, low latency, high throughput/Nitro, privacy, strict accelerator; hide advanced routing under disclosure.
Capture selected upstream provider metadata when OpenRouter metadata headers are enabled.
risks: Provider slug drift must come from catalog APIs, not hardcoded accelerator names.

title: Fail closed on privacy-sensitive settings read failures
description: Several privacy-sensitive command paths read settings with unwrap_or_default before enforcing cloud/session-content policy. Because AppSettings::default uses byok_cloud, a poisoned/unreadable settings lock can fail open and allow cloud behavior. Scope: replace privacy-mode reads in capture/chat/streaming/Gemini/converse/TTS command paths with fail-closed helpers or explicit errors, add tests for poisoned or failed settings reads, and avoid defaulting to ByokCloud for enforcement decisions. Acceptance: if privacy settings cannot be read, content-bearing cloud ASR/LLM/TTS/S2S commands do not send session content and return a redacted actionable error.

fail_closed_settings_helper_2026_06_26.summary: Added read_settings_for_session_content in commands.rs and switched content-bearing command paths to fail closed instead of defaulting unreadable settings to ByokCloud. Covered ASR session start, streaming chat, blocking chat streaming shim, notes synthesis, Gemini Live notes mode, and native S2S converse. Streaming chat now passes the already-read AppSettings snapshot into spawn_stream_task so the spawned request/TTS policy cannot re-read settings with a permissive default.

title: Remove session content from logs and provider parse errors
description: Local logs and error strings still include session content: chat commands log the start of user messages, ASR receiver logs include transcript text, and LLM extraction parse failures return/log raw provider output that may contain transcript-derived content. title: Redaction-safe Debug for transcript and projection artifacts
description: Follow-up from transcript/projection integration review. Dirty main's event-sourced projection contract uses content-bearing String fields with derived Debug on TranscriptEvent, ProjectionPatch, MaterializedNotes, and MaterializedGraph-related structures. Acceptance: serialization still writes durable user artifacts, but Debug/log output redacts content and reports only shape/ids/counts/lengths; focused tests prove sensitive sentinel text is absent from Debug for transcript events, projection patches, materialized notes, and materialized graph artifacts; no artifact path or schema regression.

title: Multiple tauri::test mock apps per process panic on gtk main-context (blocks command-layer tests in CI)
description:
FIXED on branch fix/gtk-test-harness-65f0 (2026-06-27), independently verified green. ROOT CAUSE: tao acquires the gtk main context on first tauri::Builder::build() and never releases it; the Rust harness runs each #[test] on its own thread even at --test-threads=1, so the 2nd command_test_app/mock_context app construction in a process panicked, poisoning process-global test locks and cascading 47 failures.

THREE-PART FIX: (1) poison-recovery — 49 process-global test-lock .lock().unwrap() sites converted to .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) [TEST_HOME_LOCK x35 in commands/state/sessions/speech/user_data, USAGE_TEST_LOCK x12 in sessions/usage, STORAGE_FLAG_LOCK x2 in persistence/io]; this alone stops any panic from cascading. (2) ONE gtk app per process — new pub(crate) fn shared_test_app_handle() in speech/mod.rs builds the tao app exactly once via OnceLock and leaks it 'static; the 3 speech app-tests (tests_status moonshine + 2 tests_integration) reuse the shared handle. (3) impl-extraction — start_capture/stop_capture/load_session split into thin #[tauri::command] wrappers calling *_impl(&AppState, &AppHandle); the 5 commands::tests now use an owned AppState::new() + the shared handle instead of a per-test app; command_test_app helper deleted.

VERIFICATION (independently re-run): cargo test --no-default-features --features cloud --lib -- --test-threads=1 => 1072 passed; 0 failed (was 1025/47). Clippy: 37 warnings (baseline preserved, 0 new). Each previously-failing test passes both in isolation and in-suite.

ENVIRONMENTAL NOTE: under WSL/xvfb the process exit code is 1 even with 0 failed — but this reproduces for a zero-gtk pure-logic test in isolation too, so it is a pre-existing WSL/xvfb harness artifact, not a test failure or a regression from this fix. Should be confirmed on Blacksmith Linux runners as part of the CI-evidence track.

UNBLOCKS: audio-graph-1d59 (the capture lifecycle tests now pass in-suite) and removes the test-evidence blocker for afca. NOT yet committed — the fix lives in the dirty working tree on its branch and folds into the commit-sequencing plan (docs/reviews/dirty-tree-commit-plan-2026-06-27.md) Phase G (commands.rs/speech).
closeReason: Fixed + verified, committed 0ded1c8 (poison-recovery on 49 test-lock sites + shared_test_app_handle OnceLock + start/stop/load_session *_impl(&AppState,&AppHandle) extraction; command_test_app deleted). cargo test --no-default-features --features cloud --lib --test-threads=1 = 1072 passed / 0 failed (was 47 failed). macOS ignores preserved. WSL exit-1-at-teardown confirmed environmental (zero-gtk test also exits 1 alone).

title: SettingsPage rewrite renders duplicate credential inputs — 23 SettingsPage.test.tsx failures (ambiguous findByLabelText)
description:
FOUND-BY: backlog-zero frontend commit verification (2026-06-27), branch fix/gtk-test-harness-65f0.

SYMPTOM: 23 of 642 frontend tests fail, ALL in src/components/SettingsPage.test.tsx. Backend is green (1072/0 Rust), tsc is clean (0 errors). Failure mode is uniform: TestingLibraryElementError: 'Found multiple elements with the text of: /access key id/i' (also /openrouter api key/i, /service account path/i, /deepgram api key/i). The test helper openCredentialInput() does within(scope).findByLabelText(label) and gets >1 match.

ROOT CAUSE: the in-flight SettingsPage.tsx rewrite (uncommitted dirty-tree work, M vs HEAD 5516202) renders the same labeled credential input in TWO places at once — the provider capability card AND the main provider section — so the accessible label is ambiguous within a scope. Matches the audit's flagged pattern (seed-audit-2026-06-27.md item 6: 'SettingsPage and ExpressSetup duplicate source-blocker/readiness/credential logic; every fix lands twice'). Likely the new SecretCredentialControl/ProviderReadinessPanel/capability-card components mount the credential control redundantly.

SCOPE: src/components/SettingsPage.tsx + SettingsPage.test.tsx (both M, uncommitted), plus the new untracked SecretCredentialControl.tsx / ProviderReadinessPanel.tsx / ModelCatalogPicker.tsx / AdvancedSettingsDisclosure.tsx the rewrite introduced. NOT caused by the gtk-fix work (that was backend-only; zero frontend files committed). Pre-existing in the dirty tree.

IMPACT: BLOCKS committing the frontend layer green (Phase H of dirty-tree-commit-plan-2026-06-27.md). The backend + foundation + docs already committed clean on the branch; the frontend cannot land green until this is resolved.

FIX DIRECTION (for the SettingsPage-rewrite owner — do NOT hack inline): either (a) render each credential control exactly once per provider (dedupe capability-card vs section), or (b) scope the inputs so labels are unique within their container (distinct aria-labels or scoped within() regions), and update openCredentialInput()/the tests to match. Decide whether capability cards own the credential control or the section does — the audit says pick one shared abstraction.

ACCEPTANCE: bun run test => 642/642 pass (0 SettingsPage failures); tsc clean; no duplicate accessible labels for any credential field within a provider scope; the capability-card<->section navigation tests (preserves AWS/OpenRouter/Gemini/Deepgram fields) pass.
closeReason: Fixed (test-only), committed eedd0d9. Root cause was query-side, not duplicate inputs: new SecretCredentialControl action buttons carry descriptive aria-labels embedding the field name (a11y feature), so findByLabelText matched input+buttons. Scoped queries with {selector:'input'} + readinessStatus() helper; production code byte-identical, a11y invariant (no two inputs share a label) holds. bun run test = 642/642, tsc 0.

title: Resolve VB-CABLE licensing for automated CI use (Windows live-audio smoke)
description:
FOUND-BY: virtual-audio CI research (2026-06-27, docs/research/ci-virtual-audio-devices-2026-06-27.md), blocking the Windows leg of seed audio-graph-0d66.

VB-Audio VB-CABLE is the only proven silent-unattended virtual audio device for stock Windows CI runners (WASAPI loopback alone fails: GetDefaultAudioEndpoint(eRender)=ERROR_NOT_FOUND with no hardware). However VB-Audio's terms expect a PAID license for automated/server/commercial use; the free 'donationware' license is for personal interactive use.

ACCEPTANCE: confirm a license path for using VB-CABLE in audio-graph CI (purchase VB-Audio's redistribution/server license, OR find a permissively-licensed alternative virtual audio driver that installs silently on Windows runners, OR confirm donationware terms permit ephemeral CI use). Document the decision before the Windows live-audio-smoke job is enabled in 0d66. Until resolved, the Linux (PipeWire) and macOS (BlackHole, GPLv3) legs of 0d66 can proceed; the Windows leg is gated on this.

title: biome.json schema 2.5.1 + 'preset' key fails on installed Biome 2.4.16 (lints job blocker)
description: FOUND-BY: 1f0d frontend verification (2026-06-27). biome.json (committed in the dirty-tree as schema 2.5.1 with formatter/linter 'preset':'recommended') is NOT understood by the locally-installed Biome 2.4.16: 'biome ci' / 'biome check' error with 'unknown key preset'. The CI lints job runs 'biome ci .' and would fail until the Biome toolchain is bumped to >=2.5.x (package.json devDep + CI setup) OR the 'preset' usage is reverted to the 2.4.x-compatible explicit form. ACCEPTANCE: 'biome ci .' passes clean against whatever Biome version CI installs; biome.json $schema, the installed @biomejs/biome devDep, and the CI setup-biome step all agree on one version. Note: vitest 642/642 + tsc 0 are already green; this is purely the biome lints path.

Bumped @biomejs/biome devDep ^2.4.16 -> ^2.5.1 to match the committed biome.json schema 2.5.1 + preset key; ran formatter on the 5 files Biome 2.5.1 reflows (AgentProposalsPanel+test, ProviderReadinessPanel, TokenUsagePanel+test). title: Populate and validate ProjectionBasis.diarization_span_revisions against a durable SpeakerTimeline ledger
description: ProjectionBasis::from_transcript_events hardcodes diarization_span_revisions: Vec::new() (src-tauri/src/projections.rs:174) and validate_basis returns DiarizationBasisUnavailable for any non-empty diarization basis (projections.rs:252-256). This is the precise structural blocker preventing notes/graph from consuming speaker retcons. It is the load-bearing sub-task of eb6c and worth tracking as its own slice because it is the seam every downstream speaker-aware projection plugs into. Remove the hardcoded reject: populate the field from a durable SpeakerTimeline ledger and validate against it. RelatesTo audio-graph-eb6c. Lane: transcript-graph-data.
closeReason: Acceptance satisfied in current PR branch: ProjectionBasis::from_transcript_events_and_speaker_spans populates diarization_span_revisions from SpeakerTimeline basis spans; TranscriptLedger::validate_basis_with_speaker_timeline validates non-empty diarization bases against SpeakerTimeline and rejects them only when no timeline is provided; tests projection_basis_populates_and_validates_speaker_timeline_revisions, speaker_timeline_validation_reports_diarization_mismatch_reasons, and offline_replay_revises_speaker_timeline_in_parallel_with_transcript pass with cargo +1.95.0.

title: Add tauri-plugin-updater + plugin-process (blocked on OS code-signing)
description: Audit finding: no auto-updater on an RC app shipping 4 bundle targets. release.yml passes TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY under an 'updater signing' comment but there is NO updater plugin/config, so tauri-action emits no latest.json/.sig — a silent trap. Add tauri-plugin-updater + plugin-process, plugins.updater config (endpoints + pubkey), updater:default capability, createUpdaterArtifacts. PREREQUISITE: OS code-signing/notarization — tauri.conf.json has signingIdentity:null and no notarization, so on unsigned macOS bundles 'add the plugin' does NOT yield working updates; the updater keypair is separate from OS signing but OS signing must land first/in-tandem for macOS.

title: System tray: recording indicator + start/stop + close-to-tray
description: Audit finding — strongest product gap for a long-lived background-capture privacy tool: capture is invisible/uncontrollable when the window isn't focused. Add TrayIconBuilder (behind the tray-icon Cargo feature) with a recording-indicator glyph (also a consent affordance), a start/stop/show menu, and a CloseRequested->hide-to-tray intercept. Backend already emits PIPELINE_STATUS_EVENT + CAPTURE_ERROR/STORAGE_FULL and exposes clean start/stop IPC, so the tray is a thin consumer of existing state.

competitive_capture_privacy_research_202.summary: Bot-free and background-capture products make capture state, consent, and quick pause/end controls central to trust. AudioGraph already has backend capture status; the tray/recording indicator should be treated as product UX, not polish. It is the always-visible privacy affordance when the Tauri window is hidden or unfocused.
acceptance_additions: Tray indicator clearly differentiates idle, listening/capturing, transcribing, paused, warning/error, and storage-full states.
Tray menu exposes start, pause/stop, show app, current source summary, and privacy/data-route shortcut without revealing transcript content.
Close-to-tray copy makes continued capture explicit; no hidden recording when the window disappears.

title: Tighten flaky clear_drops_in_flight_audio_frames test (test-only race)
description: Root-caused as a TEST-ONLY race (production Clear-suppression is correct via a round-trip happens-before: clearing.store(true) before the socket write, server sends trailing only after receiving Clear). The test's pre-clear drain loop (src-tauri/src/tts/deepgram_aura.rs ~lines 2024-2038) is bounded by BOTH pre_clear.len()<3 AND a 500ms deadline but only asserts pre_audio_count>0, so under multi_thread scheduling it can collect 2 of 3 pre-clear frames, call clear(), then miscount the legit 3rd pre-clear frame as trailing (observed left:1). Fix: require all 3 pre-clear frames (assert ==3 or loop on events.next() until 3 AudioChunks), drop the 500ms early-exit loophole. ~1/25-50 flake under multi_thread, 0/40 under --test-threads=1. No production change.

title: API-shape nit: Category::from_frontend_id ignores its argument
description: In src-tauri/src/analytics/mod.rs, Category::from_frontend_id unconditionally returns Category::Frontend and ignores its param. This is intentional and privacy-correct (backend picks the enum, never trusts the frontend string) — flagged only as a mild API-shape smell. Low priority; either document more explicitly or drop the unused param.

Verified: During centers Notes+Transcript (no graph hero), After = review artifact, Analysis owns graph+diagnostics; narrow reflows sanely. Vite 'no-bytes' blocker RESOLVED — was wrong-port (real port 1420) + short-timeout (first request cold-optimizes ~tens of sec) + missing bun install, NOT a server fault. Remaining before close: (1) merge the branch (PR pending); (2) pre-existing ControlBar narrow-overlap filed as separate bug. Shell itself is done + correct.

competitor_ui_research_started_2026_07_0.summary: Continuing Tauri v2 UI critique with external UI/product research. Scope: compare AudioGraph main workspace, onboarding, notes/transcript/graph/live-assist surfaces, privacy/capture affordances, and post-session memory against current meeting notetaker and live-assist products; convert findings into concrete IA/design follow-up Seeds rather than chat-only TODOs.

competitor_ui_research_2026_07_04.summary: Current competitor UI research reinforces that AudioGraph should become a session-first product, not a graph/debug cockpit. Granola/Fathom/Otter lead with calm note capture and post-call summaries/actions; Cluely keeps live assistance as a session overlay with start/stop/audio/hide controls; MeetGeek/Notta emphasize post-session workspace, templates, visual deliverables, and cross-meeting search. AudioGraph advantage: local-first/provenance/graph/privacy. Main UX gap: the live Tauri window still gives source picker, graph, notes, transcript, chat, agent proposals, token usage, route status, and pipeline diagnostics roughly equal weight.
recommendations: Create two primary product modes: During Meeting and After Meeting; During Meeting should prioritize capture state, running notes, live transcript, and live assist; After Meeting should prioritize summary, actions, searchable transcript, graph diff/provenance, and exports.
Demote KnowledgeGraphViewer from default hero to an Analysis/Graph tab or secondary pane; the graph becomes proof/explanation, not the first useful artifact.
Replace three always-visible columns with a task-ranked shell: left compact capture/source strip, center notes/transcript/live-assist workspace, right contextual details only when selected.
Move token usage, projection runtime, data route, and backpressure into a Health/Diagnostics drawer/status menu except when blocking user action.
Add a one-click session path: choose mode + source + start; sample preview should demonstrate the final note/action/memory outcome before exposing provider details.
Validate at 1280px, 1024px, and narrow side-window sizes with screenshots and keyboard passes; acceptance should include no primary action hidden behind resizers or bottom diagnostics.

workspace_phase_shell_wave_2026_07_04.summary: Implemented the first During/After/Analysis shell wave. During now makes live notes plus live transcript the main workspace and only shows live assist inline when activity exists; After routes sample/loaded sessions to review notes plus transcript instead of graph-first review; Analysis owns graph, transcript/chat context, projection runtime, and data-route diagnostics. Sample preview closes the agent popover so the After workspace is not obscured. Starting capture forces the shell back to During and restores transcript focus.

title: delete_credential_cmd leaves stale key in app_settings cache (inverse of the save-path 401 fix)
description: P6 Wave-1 review (BLOCKING). PR #39 added store->cache re-hydrate to save_credential_cmd but NOT to symmetric delete_credential_cmd (commands.rs:6384). After deleting/revoking a key, capture read-path (read_settings_for_session_content, commands.rs:303/1261) clones the cache which still holds the OLD key -> session transmits a deleted key to the provider. Readiness chip reads a separate store snapshot so UI shows 'no key' while session still uses it (masks the bug). ACCEPTANCE: delete_credential_cmd takes State<AppState> + re-hydrates app_settings from reloaded store after delete (mirror save_credential_cmd), OR shared helper for both writers; test asserts cached provider api_key is cleared after delete. Related: this pairs with a save-path re-hydrate test gap (fold in).

A user typing 'flux-general' (shared stem) passes frontend blur + migration + sanitize unchanged -> sent verbatim -> 400. ACCEPTANCE: tighten is_valid_deepgram_streaming_model to accept only the concrete flux ids (flux-general-en/flux-general-multi), OR alias 'flux-general'->'flux-general-en'; test that 'flux-general' sanitizes-to-valid or is rejected pre-wire.

title: ArtifactRef.path_hash doc/sample say sha256 but producer emits h64 DefaultHasher (contract lie to 51e0 TS)
description: P6 Wave-1 review (non-blocking). session_data_movement.rs:205 doc + sample_event() say path_hash is 'sha256:<hex>' but the only producer hash_artifact_path (persistence/data_movement.rs:38) emits 'h64:{:016x}' via DefaultHasher. Generated TS (sessionDataMovement.ts) is the chain-root contract for the privacy-report frontend (51e0) — a consumer trusting 'sha256' mismatches. ACCEPTANCE: fix the doc comment + sample_event literal to describe the 'h64:' opaque non-crypto fingerprint (drop sha256 claim); OR switch producer to sha256 if crypto strength is actually wanted. Keep doc and producer in lockstep.

title: SessionDataRoutePanel shows stale prev-session privacy report during sessionId switch
description: Unaddressed CodeRabbit Major on merged PR #42 (51e0). SessionDataRoutePanel.tsx (~199,221-232): on sessionId A→B switch, the cleanup cancels A's in-flight fetch (good, no late overwrite) but 'events' still holds A's data while B loads → the panel renders session A's data-egress/privacy report under session B's header until B resolves. For a PRIVACY panel, showing the WRONG session's what-left-the-device report (even transiently) is a real correctness/trust bug. ACCEPTANCE: clear events (setEvents(null)) at the start of the effect/load when sessionId changes, before the new fetch resolves, so no stale prev-session report renders; add a test asserting a session switch shows loading (not the prior report).

title: Fake Bearer/JWT sentinel (eyJ...) in redaction test trips GitGuardian (missed in the sk- defang)
description: Unaddressed CodeRabbit Major on merged PR #37 (70a3). session_data_movement.rs:708 still has a static 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload.signature' JWT-shaped literal in the redaction test — GitGuardian flags it. When I defanged the sk- sentinels (PR #37 follow-up) I MISSED this Bearer/JWT one (same class). It's on master now. ACCEPTANCE: runtime-assemble the fake JWT sentinel (e.g. 'Bearer ' + ['eyJ','...'].join(...)) so no static eyJ/Bearer key-shape literal remains, redaction still exercised; git grep for eyJ/Bearer in the crate returns 0 static hits.

title: user_data.rs session path-resolvers lack session_id validation (defense-in-depth, sibling of e692)
description: Unaddressed CodeRabbit Major on merged PR #37. user_data.rs transcript_path/transcript_events_path/projection_events_path/data_movement_ledger_path build paths from raw session_id via format!('{session_id}...') with NO validation — a '..'-containing id escapes the dir. My e692 fix added validate_session_id at the load_session_data_movement_cmd COMMAND layer, but these path-BUILDER functions remain unguarded (other callers could pass unsanitized ids). ACCEPTANCE: either validate_session_id inside the path resolvers themselves, or audit every caller ensures validation upstream + document the invariant; test a '..' id is rejected/contained. Defense-in-depth (app-controlled ids in normal flow).

title: STT→LLM context efficiency + diff-based note/KG retroactive maintenance
description: Design: docs/plans/2026-07-04-stt-llm-context-efficiency-design.md (PR #60). Thesis: the event-sourced retcon substrate (ProjectionOperation, append-only JSONL log, ProjectionBasis gate, valid_until soft-delete, SpeakerLabelRemap→supersede_entity) already covers the graph; this epic (a) stops re-sending the full transcript (rolling summary + delta + stable-prefix caching) and (b) extends retcon to notes + fans the speaker remap into notes. ADR: docs/adr/ADR-00XX. Child seeds = the 9 pillars. First vertical slice: 2c+2d+2g for the Notes scheduler. HOLD build pending user review of PR #60.

title: [d7bb 2g] Ledger new remote-LLM data flows (summary + cached prefix) behind cloud-transfer policy
description: Effort S. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(g). Emit DataMovementEvent per projection LLM call (ProviderCallStarted/Succeeded/Failed) via DataMovementLedgerBuilder; tag DataClass::Prompts (hash/size) + TranscriptText; ledger rolling summary (DataClass::Notes) + pinned-fact block (DataClass::GraphContext) as derived artifacts; record cache_control vendor-side prefix persistence; GATE whole context-efficiency path behind cloud-transfer MovementPolicy. Files: persistence/data_movement.rs, crates/ipc-contract/src/session_data_movement.rs, projection_scheduler.rs hooks. Acceptance: every projection LLM call + summary + cached prefix appears in the ledger; a local-only-pinned session writes NO summary/prefix to a remote cache (test). MUST ship with 2c/2d.
closeReason: Fixed in PR #77: first production data-movement emitter (projection_data_movement.rs + run_projection_job wiring) — ProviderCallStarted/Succeeded/Failed with Notes/GraphContext classes + vendor_cached_prompt_prefix artifact, consent-gated, content-free; terminal event derives from patch.provenance (ACTUAL backend incl. fallback, Codex P2 fix — failures ledger conservatively as remote when cloud fallback allowed), tested incl. forced-fallback path.

title: [d7bb 2c] Windowed basis + incremental rolling summary + delta feed (kill O(n²) transcript re-send)
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(c). Change basis_events()/format_transcript_events_json() (projection_llm.rs:509-540) to feed rolling summary of older turns + last K verbatim turns + delta since last patch. Fold summary INCREMENTALLY (only the turn leaving the hot buffer, never rebuild — avoids Telephone drift). Store 'summarized-through revision R' on ProjectionBasis so validate_basis keeps coalescing/repair correct. Pin typed facts from graph snapshot at prompt top. Acceptance: tokens_used (executor.rs:638) drops materially on long sessions; validate_basis still rejects stale patches; summary never rebuilt from scratch (test).
closeReason: Fixed in PR #77: RollingSummary incremental fold (fold==single-pass proven) + 6-turn hot window in projection prompts; ProjectionBasis.summarized_through_revision with serde-default + SummaryWindowMismatch staleness (Some-vs-Some only after Codex P1 fix — legacy patches replay clean, tested). Kills the O(n²) full-transcript re-feed. Live tokens_used measurement deferred to 8925.

title: [d7bb 2d] Stable-prefix prompt ordering + provider cache_control (session-cache reuse)
description: Effort S. EXECUTABLE (first vertical slice). Epic d7bb pillar 2(d). Re-order projection prompt (projection_llm.rs:219-237) static→dynamic: [system+schema]→[pinned facts]→[summary]→[append-only transcript]→[per-tick metadata]. Add cache_control/cachePoint breakpoint on last stable block (openrouter.rs:335, api_client.rs:54, bedrock.rs:551), gated on catalog supports_implicit_caching/input_cache_read. Set prompt_cache_key per (session, RESOLVED-provider) — a fallback hop is an expected cold cache. Acceptance: cache-read tokens > 0 on 2nd+ turn within TTL for a cache-capable provider; prefix ordering verified stable across turns (test).
closeReason: Fixed in PR #77: projection prompt reordered static→dynamic with byte-stable prefix (asserted in test); OpenRouter cache_control:ephemeral breakpoint + session-scoped prompt_cache_key; no-hint request byte-identical to legacy. Bedrock cachePoint + live cache-read measurement → seed 8925.

title: [d7bb 2e] Diff-based note patching: sub-note blocks + search/replace + soft-invalidate
description: Effort L. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(e). Replace whole-body UpsertNote clobber (projections.rs:1203-1227). Add MaterializedNote addressable blocks (Vec<NoteBlock{block_id,text,valid_from_ms,valid_until_ms}>); ops UpsertNoteBlock/InvalidateNoteBlock; ReplaceNoteText{note_id,block_id,search,replace} applied by exact/expanding-unique anchor (materializer REJECTS non-matching anchor — fail loud, not blind); note-level valid_from/until_ms + InvalidateNote (copy InvalidateGraphNode). Slots into ProjectionOperation enum + JSONL log (state.rs:438) + FE reducer (store/index.ts:253). Acceptance: a note correction emits a minimal anchored patch not a full replace; bad anchor is refused; superseded block hidden-but-auditable.

title: [d7bb 2f] Teach LLM patch-in-place vs record-a-supersession + fact typing
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE (90% prompt work over shipped ops). Epic d7bb pillar 2(f). Extend operation_guidance (projection_llm.rs:195-202): (i) classify fact STATIC vs DYNAMIC/TEMPORAL; (ii) InvalidateGraphEdge(valid_until)+new UpsertGraphEdge only for DYNAMIC facts on temporally-overlapping contradiction; (iii) Upsert-replace for same-time correction; scope contradiction check to semantically-similar edges only. Add validate_basis guard: reject Invalidate on STATIC fact (over-retraction guard). Optional M: superseded_by provenance edge. Acceptance: 'Sarah VP→CEO' produces invalidate+new-edge (old kept with valid_until); a typo-correction produces upsert-replace; a static fact cannot be invalidated (test).

title: [d7bb 2b] Persist diarization span revisions on the live path (close replay gap)
description: Effort S. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(b) move 2. append_diarization_span_revision trait exists (persistence/mod.rs:516-533) but is called ONLY from a test (commands.rs:10312). Add the durable-write call in emit_and_dispatch_diarization_span_revision (speech/mod.rs:378-427). Without it, retcons can't be replayed and the basis gate has no durable ground truth. Unblocks seeds 3588/1fbd persist-and-replay item. Acceptance: a live diarization revision is written to JSONL and survives a replay; basis gate reads durable truth (test).
closeReason: Merged via #67 (master 82e546f): live diarization dispatch now durably appends ACCEPTED span revisions to <session>.speaker.jsonl outside the timeline/graph locks (ASR-writer posture: best-effort, log-on-fail, never breaks live path). session_id threaded through all 5 dispatch sites. Test: live_diarization_relabel_persists_to_jsonl_and_replays_latest_attribution. Shared first slice of epics d7bb+0d72.

title: [d7bb 2b] Fan SpeakerLabelRemap into notes (ReattributeNoteSpeaker consumer)
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(b) move 3 — the missing consumer (ground-graph-retcon §4.4). Today SpeakerLabelRemap (projections.rs:349-461) drives ONLY graph.supersede_entity (speech/mod.rs:365). Add a notes-side consumer emitting a ReattributeNoteSpeaker note op through the SAME patch log + basis gate, so a speaker re-attribution retroactively fixes notes AND graph. Depends on 2(e) note ops. Acceptance: a late speaker correction re-attributes the affected note block(s) without deleting content; same remap signal fans to both graph + notes (test).

title: [d7bb 2a] Provider-neutral TurnUnit assembly seam with retcon-native eager EOT
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE. Epic d7bb pillar 2(a) move 1. New type TurnUnit{turn_id,speaker_label,text,span_ids,start_ms,end_ms,is_eager} in events.rs. Group AsrSpanRevisionPayload(is_final) finals under a turn_id opened by SpeechStarted/StartOfTurn, closed by EndOfTurn/UtteranceEnd (events.rs:330-338 seam, speech/mod.rs:4712-4744). Emit Deepgram Flux EagerEndOfTurn as Provisional revision, TurnResumed/EndOfTurn as superseding revisions — reuse AsrSpanRevisionPayload revision_number/supersedes/stability (events.rs:231-270). Acceptance: transcript emitted as coherent {speaker,punctuated,turn_id} units at semantic boundaries; eager→final expressed as Provisional→Final revisions (test).

title: [d7bb 2a] App-owned semantic/acoustic endpointer (fallback + confirmer for non-Flux paths)
description: Effort L. NEEDS-MODEL (executable but pulls a model download — coordinate w/ model-mgmt). Epic d7bb pillar 2(a) move 2. Wire a small CPU turn model on the AudioAccumulator seam (speech/mod.rs:2711-2805, aec_vad scaffold home) for the Whisper path (zero endpointing today, fixed 2s cuts) + non-Flux cloud providers. Candidates: pipecat Smart Turn v2 (~360MB,~12ms) or LiveKit audio turn-detector (~400MB,~25ms). Feature-gate like make_diarization_config (speech/mod.rs:943-987); DEFAULT OFF; Flux stays primary; hard silence-timeout floor. Acceptance: Whisper path gets turn detection behind a flag; disabled by default; no regression when Flux selected.

title: [d7bb 2a] Streaming re-punctuation / sentence sub-segmentation for long turns (DEFER — lowest ROI)
description: Effort XL. DEFER (lowest ROI — helps only long single-speaker turns, needs a new model, no existing analog; grep 'sentence' empty). Epic d7bb pillar 2(a) move 3. Split long monologue turns into sentence-sized sub-units via streaming re-punctuation (Streaming Punctuation dynamic windows +13.9% seg F0.5 / +0.66 BLEU, Behre 2023 arXiv:2301.03819; or SaT/WtP arXiv:2305.18893). Slots below TurnUnit seam (speech/mod.rs:4712) as a sub-turn splitter. Acceptance: a long monologue turn becomes sentence sub-units; measurably better downstream note quality. Do LAST or drop.

title: Wire session-export UI to export_session_bundle + persist/rehydrate projection scheduler queue
description: Wave-8 residual of 9c89 (backend landed: load_session_impl replays ledger+projections commands.rs:6049, export_session_bundle registered lib.rs:473; GAP: src/ never invokes export_session_bundle — only a comment src/types/index.ts:2184; projection_scheduler.rs:493 reset() is in-memory, pending_basis never persisted). EXECUTABLE. Acceptance: (a) a Sessions/Export UI action invokes export_session_bundle(sessionId) and downloads the full transcript+events+projection+notes+graph bundle; (b) projection scheduler pending/in-flight state persists per session and rehydrates on load, with a crash/restart test proving no queued-job loss.
closeReason: Merged as #62 (master fed9247): (1) session-export UI — SessionsBrowser Export button → export_session_bundle + downloadAsFile, vitest-covered, locale parity; (2) projection-scheduler queue persistence — snapshot/save/load/restore with the CodeRabbit Major fixed: restore DEMOTES persisted in_flight to pending (coalesced pending wins), never phantom-restores, +3 tests incl. a real disk round-trip; start_job clears pending_basis (stale-baseline corner). All gates green locally + CI. Review nits (URL.createObjectURL stub, stale-error clear) fixed.

title: Add env-gated OpenRouter routed-smoke harness scaffold (offline; metrics-only sanitized report)
description: Wave-8 residual of 8772 (live RUN is secrets/live-gated → stays on 8772; the OFFLINE scaffold is executable now). Acceptance: an #[ignore]/env-gated test that, when a real OpenRouter cred is present, issues ONE tiny synthetic completion and produces a sanitized metrics-only report {status, model, sanitized routing policy, timing, token counts, request_id-hash} — NO raw prompt/response/secret in the artifact (redaction-safe by construction). Without the env cred it is skipped. This is the harness plumbing; 8772 keeps the live-smoke RUN.
closeReason: Merged as #63 (master b4b9240): env-gated OpenRouter routed-smoke harness scaffold. RoutedSmokeReport structurally content-free (all String fields sanitized: served-model slug, count-derived policy, FNV request-id hash; has_no_content_fields anchor test). Offline redaction test runs in CI; live run #[ignore]+env-gated (owned by 8772). fmt+clippy+build passed locally; test exec deferred to CI.

title: Persist + surface OpenRouter runtime accounting snapshot per session (disk)
description: Follow-up split from closed 713c (Wave-8 audit). The in-memory process-wide accounting sink (OpenRouterRuntimeAccounting, openrouter.rs:797) has zero consumers outside openrouter.rs. NEW capability: persist snapshot_global per session to disk and surface it to Settings/session-usage. Acceptance: a session's OpenRouter token/cost accounting persists across restart and is viewable in the session-usage UI; content-free (counts/costs only, no prompt text). Depends on the session-usage surface.
