title: Session timeline: who-said-what-when-in-relation-to-what (hybrid projection + views)
description: VERDICT: hybrid-new-projection-plus-view — a pure read-only fold over the already-event-sourced transcript/diarization/graph logs + two shell-integrated views (After seek-timeline, Analysis as-of scrubber), reusing the ADR-0024/0025 retcon+basis substrate. NOT a parallel agent, NOT a new subsystem. The only real backend work is closing the per-utterance relates-to provenance gap (surface stripped edge source_segment_id; wire test-only PromotionSourceReference). Design: docs/plans/2026-07-04-session-timeline-design.md; ADR-0026 (Proposed). CRITICAL design constraint (sev4 critique fix): the per-utterance edge join folds over the LIVE TemporalKnowledgeGraph (TemporalEdge.source_segment_id) — MaterializedGraphEdge has NO source_segment_id; MaterializedGraph is only for the bitemporal as-of scrubber. 8 children below.

title: [0d72 P1] build_session_timeline read-model fold + Tauri command
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE. Epic 0d72. Backend fn over TranscriptLedger+SpeakerTimeline+LIVE TemporalKnowledgeGraph (+MaterializedGraph ONLY for as-of fields) → duplicate-free start_time-ordered Vec<TimelineEntry{span_id,start_ms,end_ms,received_at_ms,turn_id,speaker_id,speaker_label,text,related_edge_ids}> via one Tauri command. Speaker = latest-revision-wins. related_edge_ids grouped by TemporalEdge.source_segment_id (NOT MaterializedGraphEdge — it has no source_segment_id; sev4 critique). PRIVACY: hand-impl redacted fmt::Debug for TimelineEntry (raw text field) + sessionDataMovement ledger event per design F2. Acceptance: unit test folds fixture session, asserts order+attribution+edge grouping; Debug output carries no raw text.
closeReason: Fixed in PR #72 (b0eddad): timeline.rs read-model fold + build_session_timeline_cmd; live-graph source_segment_id join per ADR-0026 sev4 correction, latest-wins speaker attribution, redacted Debug, 6 unit tests. CI green; main-loop adversarial diff-read as review (fold-in provenance-thinning observation carried to b272).

title: [0d72 P2] Surface TemporalEdge.source_segment_id on emitted GraphLink/GraphEdge
description:
Effort S. EXECUTABLE. Epic 0d72. Frontend-facing GraphLink/GraphEdge payload (graph/entities.rs:74,137) carries the source_segment_id already stored on backend TemporalEdge (temporal.rs:38); graph delta from an extraction includes triggering segment id; FE types updated so graph view can resolve edge→source utterance. Acceptance: emitted delta includes source_segment_id; FE type + test.

---
PR #72 diff-read note (2026-07-05): supersede_entity has two re-point paths — the clone/new-edge path preserves source_segment_id, but the fold-weight-into-existing-live-edge path DROPS the folded edge's segment provenance (survivor keeps its own source_segment_id). Timeline related_edge_ids therefore thins on fold-in retcons. b272 should decide: accumulate a Vec<segment_id> on fold-in vs accept thinned provenance — and state the choice in the PR.
closeReason: Fixed in PR #75 (34ae77b): source_segment_id surfaced on snapshot GraphLink + delta GraphEdge (added/updated), TS types + store pass-through. Provenance policy: accept-thinned on fold-in (survivor keeps own id; rationale + trade-off documented at code site), clone/repoint preserves verbatim — both paths tested. CI green; CodeRabbit rate-limited so main-loop adversarial diff-read served as review (posted on PR).

EXECUTABLE (gated on P1 backend fold per F3 critique — loaded sessions have NO diarization revisions in store; do NOT ship a frontend-selector-only slice or attribution falls back to untrusted inline ASR labels). Epic 0d72. Component sibling to LiveTranscript in the After grid: DOM/SVG speaker lanes positioned by start_time, existing CSS tokens, ~200-segment cap, click a moment → scroll LiveTranscript to segment. During/Analysis unaffected. Acceptance: renders lanes from backend fold; click-seek works; narrow-width sane.
closeReason: Fixed in PR #80: After-mode SeekTimeline — speaker lanes on media-clock axis via build_session_timeline_cmd, click/Enter→transcript seek with SR announcement, edge-count badges voiced in aria labels, shared TRANSCRIPT_WINDOW_SIZE constant aligning timeline/transcript windows, stale-fold race guard, keyboard-navigable, en+pt, empty/loading states. 2 Codex P2s + 5 CodeRabbit findings fixed pre-merge; final sweep clean. Follow-up a2a7 (badge→graph focus) filed.

title: [0d72 P4] Analysis graph as-of/replay scrubber (parameterize KGV bitemporal filter)
description: Effort M. EXECUTABLE. Epic 0d72. Parameterize KnowledgeGraphViewer isActiveMaterializedNode/Edge predicates by as-of sequence/updated_at_ms driven by a DOM slider over sessionProjectionEvents; scrubbing re-derives snapshot at that point; canvas render unchanged; default 'now'. NOTE (F5): both materialized axes are learned-time; audio-time exists only on live TemporalEdge — label the scrubber axis honestly. Acceptance: scrub shows graph as-of; invalidated edges render as ended (not removed).

title: [0d72 P5] Wire PromotionSourceReference: approved cards keep span-granular provenance (ADDITIVE)
description: Effort L. EXECUTABLE. Epic 0d72. On card approval the durable patch preserves the card's source_span_ids via a production PromotionSourceReference constructor — ADDITIVE to the whole-window basis (F4: basis stays for staleness validation; span refs are provenance, do not replace basis or validate_basis breaks). Acceptance: approved artifact cites originating utterance (span-granular wasDerivedFrom) AND retains window basis; test asserts both.

title: [0d72 P6] Persist diarization span revisions on live path (timeline hard dep)
description: Effort S. EXECUTABLE. Epic 0d72 — HARD cross-session dep (F6: retcon-into-timeline is only 'free' for LIVE sessions; replay needs durable revisions). Same gap as d7bb-719d: append_diarization_span_revision (persistence/mod.rs:516) called only from a test; add the call in emit_and_dispatch_diarization_span_revision (speech/mod.rs:378-427). ALSO (F3): add diarization_events to LoadedSession + populate diarizationSpanRevisions in loadSession so loaded sessions get trusted attribution. Acceptance: mid-session relabel replays from disk with corrected attribution; timeline fold reflects latest revision after reload.

Tests: load_session_includes_persisted_diarization_events + empty-without-log + 2 store vitest. Unblocks 0d72 P1 fold + P7 retcon-reflection.

title: [0d72 P7] Retcon reflection into timeline (relabel/late-final re-fold; mark superseded rows)
description: Effort M. NOT-YET (depends P1+P6). Epic 0d72. After SpeakerLabelRemap or late-final revision, re-running build_session_timeline yields updated attribution/text/related_edge_ids with no special path; invalidated edges render as ended; superseded timeline state visibly marked, not silently rewritten. Scoped per F6: live-session retcon is inherent; cross-restart requires P6.

title: [0d72 P8] Reverse provenance: select time range → highlight derived notes/nodes
description: Effort L. NOT-YET (depends P1/P2/P5). Epic 0d72. Selecting a time range on the After timeline highlights notes/graph nodes derived from spans in that range (FE index over related_artifact_refs); corroborative vs contributive links visually distinguished; empty/dense range states handled. The MeetMap-style differentiator.

Contrast openai_realtime replay (:686) and Deepgram start-time keying. title: file_override precedence resurrects stale credentials.yaml key over freshly saved keychain value (Deepgram 401 loop)
description:
MANUAL-TEST FINDING (2026-07-06, Windows build 04e59bf, user-observed 401 loop persists across key re-saves).

EVIDENCE (log fingerprints, no key material):
- 12:37:14 save_credential_cmd: key=deepgram_api_key fingerprint=sha256:99ddc96a (the NEW key the user typed) → persisted to keychain, mark_migrated.
- 12:37:44 + 12:39:30 Deepgram connect: fingerprint=sha256:e1c463ff → 401.
- credentials.yaml (mtime Jun 20) STILL contains deepgram_api_key with fingerprint e1c463ff — exactly the stale key used at connect.
- credentials-state.yaml: deepgram_api_key in migrated_keys.

MECHANISM: DefaultCredentialBackend::get + the snapshot loader implement 'edited credentials.yaml override' (BUG 7fc5): for a migrated key, a non-empty plaintext entry in credentials.yaml BEATS the keychain. But save() writes ONLY the keychain + migration state — it never removes/updates the yaml entry. So any key that existed in yaml before migration permanently shadows every future keychain save: user re-enters a rotated key, save succeeds, reads still return the old yaml value → perpetual 401 with a 'valid' save. The readiness probe reads through the same precedence, so it validates the STALE key too.

FIX DIRECTION (decide one, symmetric across save+delete per review-check-symmetric-writers):
(a) save_credential clears/updates the yaml entry for that key in the same critical section (CREDENTIAL_IO_LOCK held), so file_override can only represent a HAND-EDIT NEWER than the last save; or
(b) file_override only wins if yaml mtime > migration-state snapshot time for that key; or
(c) drop file_override for keys with a live keychain value and surface hand-edits via an explicit import action.
Tests: save-after-yaml-preexisting returns NEW value on get; delete symmetric; fingerprint both paths.

USER-MACHINE REMEDIATION APPLIED: removed stale deepgram_api_key line from credentials.yaml (backup credentials.yaml.bak-20260706-stale-deepgram). NOTE: openai/gemini/openrouter entries also remain in yaml and are migrated — same shadow risk if those rotate.
closeReason: Shipped in PR #94 (squash ecf29be): YamlCredentialBackend::clear_key removes the stale plaintext entry inside the SAME CREDENTIAL_IO_LOCK critical section on every successful keychain set() (and symmetrically delete()) — a pre-migration credentials.yaml entry can no longer shadow a freshly saved key (the user's Deepgram 401 loop root cause; their machine was hand-remediated, this fixes it for everyone). file_override now only ever represents a hand-edit NEWER than the last save. Invariants preserved: file_backend mode untouched, import still works, hand-edit-after-save still overrides, delete tombstone + plaintext removal. Fix round: delete() yaml-clear failures degrade to warn+Ok (Codex P2), set() propagates by design. 49 credentials tests incl. 7 new. Follow-up: 3d6b (save-path degradation UX).

title: Projection fallback chain misreports failure as 'mistral.rs LLM is not loaded' — last_error swallows the real OpenRouter error
description:
MANUAL-TEST FINDING (2026-07-06, Windows build 04e59bf, 4.5-min Deepgram capture).

SYMPTOM: 80 of 86 'Projection job generation failed' warnings say 'mistral.rs LLM is not loaded' — but the user's provider is OpenRouter and mistral.rs was never selected or loaded.

MECHANISM (src-tauri/src/llm/executor.rs run_projection_attempts ~:701): the chain for LlmProvider::OpenRouter is [openrouter, api, native, mistralrs]. On failure it returns only the LAST error. So when the OpenRouter attempt fails (the real cause — parse/decode errors), api + native + mistralrs also fail with their 'not configured/loaded' boilerplate, and the surfaced error is ALWAYS 'mistral.rs LLM is not loaded'. The real failure never reaches the log/UI. Diagnosability bug: made a real OpenRouter incident look like a local-model config problem.

FIX DIRECTION: keep the first error from the SELECTED provider (or join all attempt errors: 'openrouter: <err>; api: not configured; ...'). The selected provider's error is the one to surface; downstream 'not loaded' noise belongs at debug level.

RELATED same-session data: 103 successful backend outputs all provider=openrouter; 74 Graph + 12 Notes jobs failed. Companion seeds: parse-robustness (see 401-recovery + structured-output seeds this session).
closeReason: Shipped in PR #96 (squash a804570): run_projection_attempts returns the FIRST (selected-provider) error — kills the 'mistral.rs LLM is not loaded' masking that hid the real OpenRouter failure from the user's logs; downstream fallback errors demoted to debug; empty-chain text corrected. Gate round 2 fixed the same masking class in the new escalation fallback (producing backend's repair error surfaces raw, regression test added).

title: OpenRouter projection/extraction JSON frequently invalid: no structured-output enforcement + repair uses same failing backend
description:
MANUAL-TEST FINDING (2026-07-06, Windows, 4.5-min capture, model=openai/gpt-oss-120b via OpenRouter).

DATA: 103 projection backend outputs (all openrouter) but 86 generation failures + 6 'patch draft invalid and repair failed' (missing field id/tags/title) + 17 extraction failures (9 'error decoding response body', 8 missing field relations/target/name, invalid-null). Notes projections: 0 applied in the whole session — the During view showed no notes. Graph got 54 deltas through despite the noise.

MECHANISMS:
1. projection_openrouter (executor.rs ~:830) requests JSON with chat_completion_with_usage_cached(..., true, ...) — JSON *mode*, not a JSON *schema*. gpt-oss-120b emits schema-invalid patches (missing id/tags/title). Contrast ProjectionStructuredOutputMode::MistralRsJsonSchema which enforces. OpenRouter supports structured outputs (response_format json_schema + strict) on many models incl. gpt-oss.
2. Repair retries the SAME attempt backend with a repair prompt (run_projection_patch_with_attempts) — when the model is the problem, repair usually fails the same way (observed: 6/6 double-failures).
3. 'error decoding response body' (9x) = reqwest-level decode on /chat/completions — likely mid-body connection drop or non-JSON payload; there is no HTTP retry on the blocking extraction path (provider-connections review M4, PR #86 scope was WS only).

FIX DIRECTION: (a) send response_format=json_schema with the projection patch schema + strict on OpenRouter (fallback to json mode for models without support); (b) extraction M4 retry: bounded 2-3 attempt jittered retry on decode/timeout/429/5xx for the blocking extraction call; (c) repair escalates to next backend in chain instead of same-model retry; (d) consider defaulting projection model away from gpt-oss-120b or documenting model requirements.

EVIDENCE: audio-graph.log 12:54-12:59 session 63f86915; Sentry AUDIO-GRAPH-3 llm.openrouter.* events 192+.
closeReason: Shipped in PR #96 (squash a804570): OpenRouter structured outputs (response_format json_schema strict, schema >= validator via projection_patch_strict_json_schema, kind-partitioned); schema-rejection fallback with per-model session cache gated on model-level evidence only (404 under require_parameters); provider.require_parameters pinned for schema requests (Codex P2); repair escalates to next backend; body-decode folded into bounded retry. Cache-hint orthogonality test-asserted (ADR-0025 survives). 112 scoped tests. Restores notes generation (user session: 0 notes, 86 failures on gpt-oss-120b). Outer gate: 2 fix rounds. Follow-ups: a271, f01d.

title: Sentry AUDIO-GRAPH-3 groups unrelated events (info llm.openrouter.routed + frontend.invoke.error) into one issue — fingerprinting too coarse
description: OBSERVATION (2026-07-06): issue AUDIO-GRAPH-3 accumulated 197 events spanning at least: level=error frontend.invoke.error (provider=start_transcribe, the 401 loop) AND level=info llm.openrouter.routed (routing telemetry). These are unrelated surfaces sharing one Sentry issue, which (a) buries real errors under info-level routing beacons, (b) makes issue counts meaningless, (c) triggered 'new issue' noise. Likely cause: the native sentry client groups by message template/transaction rather than a per-event-name fingerprint, and ADR-0023 telemetry events are sent as 'default' type events at info level into the error stream. FIX DIRECTION: set explicit fingerprint = [event.name] (category+kind) for captureFrontendError/relay events; route info-level telemetry (routed/accounting beacons) to a separate logger or drop to breadcrumbs attached to real errors; keep level=error only for actionable failures. Verify grouping with two distinct event.names after the change.
closeReason: Shipped in PR #95 (squash 9eb51cf): explicit [category, event.name] Sentry fingerprint on every relayed event (distinct event.names never share an issue — fixes the AUDIO-GRAPH-3 197-event mixed blob); info-level telemetry beacons routed as breadcrumbs via scrub_breadcrumb/sanitize gates instead of standalone issue events; error/warning unchanged. ADR-0023 hardened in the P1 round: breadcrumb message+category now scrubbed through sanitize_tag_value (credential-shaped values dropped) with a premise-proving regression test. Outer gate: 2 fix rounds (clippy; Codex P1 breadcrumb leak). Lane ran standalone after 4 workflow watchdog deaths. Follow-ups: afc1 (typed routing, webview parity, agent clippy), c56a (allowlisted-key test cases).

title: Wire check:endpoint-credential-routing + sibling generated-file check scripts into CI frontend job
description: From PR #89 (ed48) follow-up: generated-file drift (endpointCredentialRouting.ts, providerRegistry.ts, audioSource.ts, sessionDataMovement.ts) is currently caught only by Rust cargo tests — the fast 'Frontend (TypeScript)' CI job doesn't run the check:* script pair, so a TS-only PR that hand-edits a generated file gets its first failure signal from the slow Rust matrix instead of the fast job. Fix: add the check:* scripts as an explicit step in the frontend CI job. NOTE: CI edits are approval-gated per mission standards — surface the diff to the user before merging.

title: [ed48 follow-up] Derive endpoint predicates from routing table + fix drift-test doc-comment location
description: Two CodeRabbit nits from PR #89 sweep (both on 7dfe8e9, neither blocking): (1) TRIVIAL: base_url_const_name (endpoint_credential_routing.rs:146-152) and the TS isCerebrasEndpoint/isSambanovaEndpoint in NORMALIZE_AND_MATCHER_TS (:266-272) hardcode the two current endpoints instead of deriving predicates from ENDPOINT_CREDENTIAL_ROUTING — adding a third routed endpoint means editing 2 extra places, partially undercutting single-source. (2) MINOR: doc-comment at settings/mod.rs:1300 says the drift test lives in provider_registry.rs but generated_endpoint_credential_routing_ts_is_current is in settings/mod.rs:4185. Fix both in one small PR.

title: [db8a follow-up] Diarization override warn logs twice per start_transcribe (preflight + spawn both log)
description: Found independently by BOTH the in-script Opus review (nonblocking) and CodeRabbit (Minor) on PR #90: start_transcribe runs apply_diarization_settings + log_overrides at the pre-flight validation block (commands.rs:1375-1376) AND the speech-processor spawn block (commands.rs:1619-1620), each over a fresh settings.asr_provider.clone() — so an active override emits the identical warn line twice per successful start. Keep the preflight mutation for validation but log only at the dispatch site (or dedupe). Trivial fix; deferred at the gate to avoid another CI round on a log-spam nit.
closeReason: Shipped in PR #93 (squash 2259399): diarization override warn now logs only at the speech-processor spawn/dispatch site; preflight keeps the apply for validation without logging.

title: [8925 follow-up] Fold Bedrock cacheRead/cacheWrite tokens into StreamUsage accounting
description: Codex P2 on PR #91 sweep, verified against SDK 1.124.0 (TokenUsage.cache_read_input_tokens / cache_write_input_tokens exist as Option<i32> accessors): with cachePoint active, Bedrock documents inputTokens as ONLY the non-cached portion, so bedrock_event_from_sdk → stream_usage_from_counts(input, output, total) under-reports prompt/total tokens on cache-hit turns in the session usage panel. Was an explicitly deferred criterion of seed 8925 (cache-read metering); now unblocked. Fix: extend stream_usage_from_counts (or the ~:495 construction site) to add cache_read+cache_write into prompt/total (decide the display semantics vs billing semantics deliberately — cache reads are billed cheaper, consider surfacing them as a separate field like the OpenRouter accounting snapshot in 6ce8/713c rather than inflating prompt_tokens). Tests: cache-hit metadata event maps to expected usage.

title: [8925 follow-up] Structured Bedrock model-id matching for cachePoint allowlist (not raw contains)
description: CodeRabbit Minor on PR #91 sweep: model_supports_cache_point uses case-insensitive substring contains(), so a CUSTOM inference-profile name or ARN that merely embeds a marker (e.g. an app-inference-profile named 'my-nova-pro-router' pointing at an unsupported target) would get a cachePoint and could hard-fail. Parse the known Bedrock id shapes ([region.]vendor.model-id[:version] and arn:aws:bedrock:...) and match markers only against the model segment; add a negative test for an unrelated id embedding a marker. Low urgency: current UI only offers curated model ids, custom ARNs are not yet a configured path.

title: [57cc follow-up] Gemini rejected API key returns HTTP 400 API_KEY_INVALID, not 401 — credential-rejected recovery never fires
description: Found by the in-script integration-surface review on PR #92: test_gemini_api_key probes generativelanguage.googleapis.com, which returns HTTP 400 with API_KEY_INVALID (not 401) for a rejected key — so classify_credential_rejected_message never fires for Gemini and a bad Gemini key falls back to generic errorWithCredentials copy instead of the new dedicated 'replace your key' recovery. Consistent with 57cc's 401-only scope, not a regression. Fix options: (a) treat Gemini's 400+API_KEY_INVALID as a credential rejection in gemini_api_key_connection_error_message (needs body inspection, defanged fixtures only), or (b) document the exclusion in the helper. 5 of 6 wired providers surface the feature today.

title: [db8a follow-up] maxSpeakersHint copy wrong under Fixed/Unbounded + surface DiarizationOverride in settings UI
description: Second nonblocking from the db8a in-script review (PR #90): en.json:616 maxSpeakersHint says 'Provider-specific caps still remain in advanced provider controls' — only true under Auto speaker-count; under Fixed/Unbounded the global policy overrides the per-provider cap (exactly what the backend now warns about via DiarizationOverride). Natural fix: correct the hint AND surface the override in the settings UI using the DiarizationOverride struct (it was explicitly designed as that seam). Pair with 2dfb (double-log dedupe) in one small PR. pt.json parity required (locale-parity test).
closeReason: Shipped in PR #93 (squash 2259399): maxSpeakersHint corrected in en+pt to state that Fixed/Unbounded modes override provider caps (Auto preserves them); diarizationOverrideActive hint surfaces the override in AsrProviderSettings. Follow-up cb6a: hint rendering test.

title: ModelCatalogPicker onBlur setTimeout leaks past test teardown — 'window is not defined' unhandled error flakes CI
description: PR #92 frontend job failed with 0 test failures (905/905 pass): ModelCatalogPicker.tsx:133 onBlur schedules window.setTimeout(..., 100) that can fire AFTER vitest tears down the JSDOM env → ReferenceError: window is not defined → 'Vitest caught 1 unhandled error' → job fails. Pre-existing (PR #92 doesn't touch the file; error originates while ModelCatalogPicker.test.tsx runs). Fix: store the timer id and clearTimeout in a useEffect cleanup (component unmount), which is correct app behavior anyway (blur→unmount currently dispatches setState on unmounted component). One test: unmount before 100ms, assert no unhandled error.
closeReason: Shipped in PR #93 (squash 2259399): blur timer stored in blurTimeoutRef, cleared on unmount AND before rescheduling (Codex P2 round: blur-refocus-blur orphan); regression test strengthened to spy on setTimeout/clearTimeout after in-script review caught the vacuous version. Kills the 'window is not defined' CI flake.

title: [3a6c follow-up] Regression test for diarizationOverrideActive hint in AsrProviderSettings
description: CodeRabbit Trivial on PR #93: the new diarizationOverrideActive hint (renders when diarizationSpeakerCount !== 'auto') shipped without a rendering test. Add a small test asserting the hint appears under Fixed/Unbounded and disappears under Auto.

title: [79aa follow-up] clear_key yaml-IO failure fails the whole save/delete — degrade gracefully instead
description: In-script review nonblocking on PR #94, verified: YamlCredentialBackend::clear_key propagates load()/save() errors, so a malformed or unwritable credentials.yaml makes keychain-mode set()/delete() return Err even though the keychain write + mark_migrated ALREADY SUCCEEDED. Consequence chain: save_credential_impl then skips bump_provider_credential_epoch + rehydrate_app_settings_cache (both gated on Ok), so the running session keeps the stale cached key and the user sees 'save failed' for an operation that effectively succeeded — the same stale-cache 401 class 79aa fixed. Remedy: log-and-continue on clear_key failure (matching the read paths' let Ok(..) else / .ok().flatten() convention). Edge case (requires corrupt/locked yaml) hence not blocking, but the failure mode is user-facing. Test: corrupt yaml + successful keychain save → Ok, epoch bumped, warn logged.

title: [e6e6 follow-ups] Type-enforce beacon-vs-issue level routing + webview fingerprint parity + agent pre-push clippy
description: Three nonblocking follow-ups from the e6e6 lane (PR #95): (1) capture_diagnostic accepts Level::Info from any caller, which would recreate the beacon-as-issue problem the PR fixed — add a typed wrapper or debug_assert at the two entry points so level→path routing (info=breadcrumb, warn/error=issue event) is structural, not conventional. (2) When the deferred @sentry/browser webview integration lands (seed 2d77 / ADR-0023 consequence), it must apply the same [category, event.name] fingerprint frontend-side rather than relying on the backend relay. (3) Operational: standalone-agent pre-push verification should run clippy (cloud feature set) in addition to cargo fmt --check — PR #95's only CI failure was clippy::redundant_closure that fmt-check can't see.

title: [e6e6 follow-up] Breadcrumb data-scrub tests must exercise allowlisted keys (provider/http_status) with invalid values
description: CodeRabbit Minor on PR #95 final SHA (add605e): the breadcrumb data-map tests use NON-allowlisted keys (provider2, http_status_num), so they only prove key filtering — not that a secret-shaped value under the real allowlisted 'provider' key, or a non-string under 'http_status', is rejected. Production path is believed correct (same sanitize gate as message/category, and the message-path leak has a regression test from the P1 round), but the data-map branch lacks direct coverage. Add cases: provider=SECRET → dropped/scrubbed; http_status=non-string → dropped. Bundle with afc1's typed-routing work.

title: [a324 follow-ups] Per-model strict-schema tightening + structured-output-capable model hint in Settings + resilience doc note
description: Three nonblocking follow-ups from the a324/cb46 lane (PR #96): (1) The strict projection schema deliberately omits numeric-range/minLength keywords for strict-engine compatibility (several 400 on them); if a provider's strict mode honors minimum/maximum we could tighten per-model — low priority, repair covers it. (2) Product decision from a324 fix-direction (d): default the projection model away from gpt-oss-120b, or add a 'structured-output-capable model recommended' note in LLM Settings — the user's original 0-notes session was gpt-oss-120b emitting schema-invalid JSON. (3) extract_entities (non-projection) shares chat_completion_send and now inherits the decode retry — add a one-line note to docs/reviews/2026-07-05-provider-connections-review.md resilience section.

title: [a324 follow-ups 2] Schema-unsupported cache expiry + persistent-400 request-volume + 2xx parse-failure context
description: Three CodeRabbit Minors/Trivials from PR #96 final sweep (79fe14f), deferred by scope discipline: (1) OPENROUTER_SCHEMA_UNSUPPORTED cache is process-lifetime — make it session-scoped or add expiry so a model isn't demoted forever after a transient 404 (reviewer: 'Make the schema-unsupported cache session-scoped or add expiry'). (2) By design 400/422 provider quirks are NOT cached, so a persistently-schema-rejecting routed provider doubles request volume + warn noise every projection tick — consider a short-TTL per-provider backoff. (3) Terminal 2xx parse failures drop response context (status/request-id) that would aid debugging — preserve it in the error (redaction-safe). Bundle with a271.
